races.name: William Hill Bill Turner Memorial EBF Brocklesby Stakes (A Novice Stakes) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: A Bear Affair
runners.spotlight: 68,000gns yearling; February foal by Kodi Bear; dam Graded stakes-placed 5f-7.5f winner (including 2yo/US/Canada; RPR 100), half-sister to Listed-placed 5f/6f 2yo winner Neola; bred to be sharp and represents the 2022 and 2025 winning stable; makes plenty of appeal on paper.
runners.comment: Bred to be sharp and represents the 2022 and 2025 winning stable; makes plenty of appeal

runners.name: The Kalonji Man
runners.spotlight: 10,000gns yearling; January foal, so the earliest in this field; Belardo half-brother to five winners including Quayside (5f-7f including 2yo; RPR 96) and Glorious Charmer (5f-6f including 2yo; 80); dam 6f 2yo winner (89), half-sister to two Queen Mary winners; powerful yard runs two.
runners.comment: Earliest foal in this line-up and bred to be sharp as well; stablemate of A Bear Affair

runners.name: Nevernotrememberu
runners.spotlight: 2,000gns yearling; February foal; dam unraced half-sister to 6f/7f winner Bold Spirit, out of half-sister to high-class 6f/7f winner Danehill; represents Kathy Turner, whose late father Bill won the Brocklesby six times and in whose honour this year's race is named.
runners.comment: Cost just 2,000gns; represents Kathy Turner whose father Bill won the Brocklesby six times

runners.name: Bill The Bull
runners.spotlight: 17,000gns yearling; April foal; Coulsty half-brother to seven winners including Jungle Inthebungle (5f 2yo; RPR 90), The Nazca Lines (5f including 2yo; 80) and Russellinthebushes (5f 2yo; 79); dam 5f 2yo winner (80); speedily bred; rookie trainer has made promising start.
runners.comment: Speedily bred son of Coulsty; rookie trainer has made promising start

runners.name: Arrbob
runners.spotlight: 28,000gns yearling; March foal; Ardad half-brother to 6f winner Dubai Magic (RPR 65); dam once-raced sister to 6f Group 3 winner Strath Burn and half-sister to several useful winners at up to 1m; has the right sort of pedigree for this race and his yard won the 2017 and 2019 runnings.
runners.comment: Has the right sort of pedigree for this race and his yard won the 2017 and 2019 runnings

runners.name: Blixen Force
runners.spotlight: 30,000euros foal, Â£110,000 yearling; February foal by Persian Force; dam unplaced 1m (RPR 76), sister to Group-placed 5f winner Show Me Show Me; the most expensive of these sold at public auction; has an attractive pedigree; one of two runners for Robson De Aguilar and Amo Racing.
runners.comment: The most expensive of these sold at public auction and has an attractive pedigree

runners.name: Dance A Jig
runners.spotlight: Home-bred February foal; closely related to 7f winner Dance In A Storm (RPR 95), half-brother to six winners including Dancing Star (6f/7f including 2yo/Group 3; 113) and Foxtrot Lady (6f/7f including Group 3; 106); dam 6f winner (93), out of Nunthorpe winner Lochangel; has a classy pedigree and represents a powerful stable.
runners.comment: Home-bred February foal with a classy pedigree and representing a powerful stable

runners.name: Step To Glory
runners.spotlight: 15,000gns yearling; February foal by Cotai Glory; dam unraced half-sister to 5f Group 3 winner Hurricane Ivor from a family of sprinters; fits the bill on pedigree and his stable has a respectable (20-165) record with its 2yos, winning this race in 2024; one of the more interesting candidates.
runners.comment: Fits the bill on pedigree and yard won this race two years ago; interesting

runners.name: Downloadmylife
runners.spotlight: Â£11,000 yearling; March foal by Mohaather; dam 6f 2yo winner (RPR 72), sister to 5f 2yo/6f winner Prissy Missy; yard often has runners in this race but yet to win it.
runners.comment: Â£11,000 yearling; yard often has runners in this race but yet to win it

runners.name: Rlasthope
runners.spotlight: February foal, already gelded; Rajasinghe half-brother to winners Lazyitis (5f including 2yo; RPR 82), Roshambo (6f; 77), Mu Mu Land (6f 2yo; 75) and Mayo County (6f/7f; 69); dam 5f winner (including 2yo; 77); bred for sprinting but stable has a modest strike-rate with its 2yos.
runners.comment: Bred for sprinting but stable has a modest strike-rate with its 2yos

runners.name: Boutblummintime
runners.spotlight: Â£32,000 yearling; March foal; Nando Parrado half-brother to winners Zardonic (Czech 1m3f) and Fauda (Slovakian 1m); once-raced dam out of 7f 2yo Group 3 winner; yard has a respectable (13%) strike-rate with 2yos on turf.
runners.comment: Â£32,000 yearling by Nando Parrado; yard has a respectable strike-rate with 2yos on turf

runners.name: Brandenburg
runners.spotlight: Â£10,000 yearling; February foal by Ubettabelieveit; half-brother to 6f winner Oneforsue (RPR 72); dam 7f 2yo winner (71); worth a market check.
runners.comment: Â£10,000 yearling by Ubettabelieveit; half-brother to a 6f winner; worth a market check

runners.name: Invincible Isaac
runners.spotlight: 16,000gns yearling; March foal by Mayson; dam 5f winner (RPR 63), half-sister to winners Be Like Me (6f including 2yo) and As It Was (5.7f 2yo); bred to be speedy and has to be considered.
runners.comment: 16,000gns yearling; bred to be speedy and has to be considered

runners.name: Ocean Club
runners.spotlight: 14,000euros foal, 33,000euros yearling; March foal by Persian Force; dam unraced half-sister to French 1m3f winner Lady Lilnoi, out of once-raced half-sister to useful 1m2f-1m4f winner Setting Sail; stablemate of Blixen Force and by the same sire; worth monitoring in the market.
runners.comment: Stablemate of Blixen Force and by the same sire; worth monitoring in the market

runners.name: Seathegulls
runners.spotlight: 27,000gns yearling; February foal; Twilight Son half-brother to winners Rogue Assassin (7f; RPR 76), West Tyrone (1m; 76) and Al Khawaneej River (11.4f; 73); dam placed 6f-7.4f (including 2yo; 71), out of 6f 2yo Group 3 winner; stable only 1-56 with 2yos in the last few seasons.
runners.comment: By Twilight Son; stable only 1-56 with 2yos in the last few seasons

races.name: William Hill Price Boosts Every Day Cammidge Trophy Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Spycatcher
runners.spotlight: Won this race 12 months ago (good to soft) when others including James's Delight met trouble; ended 2025 better than ever with convincing successes in Group 3 at Chantilly (very soft) and Listed race here (heavy; Montassib and Art Power unplaced); penalised this time but should go well again.
runners.comment: Won this 12 months ago and ended 2025 better than ever; should go well again

runners.name: My Mate Alfie
runners.spotlight: Previously trained by Ger Lyons in Ireland; best efforts in 2025 when runner-up in Listed event and Group 3 at the Curragh (both 6f, good); changed hands for 250,000gns in the autumn; major player if cherry ripe on his first start for new connections and needs watching carefully in the market.
runners.comment: Smart sprinter; big-money purchase in the autumn; needs market check on debut for new yard

runners.name: Mitbaahy
runners.spotlight: Pounced late under Jamie Spencer to snatch Curragh Group 2 (6f, good) in May 2024, but has raced only three times since, last seen finishing a respectable second in AW Listed event at Lingfield in February 2025; others carry fewer risks but he's no forlorn hope if ready to roll after 419 days off.
runners.comment: Lightly raced in recent years and returns from 419 days off; chance if ready to roll

runners.name: Montassib
runners.spotlight: Arrives late on the scene and had a stellar 2024, his three wins including this race (soft) and the Group 1 Sprint Cup at Haydock (good); wasn't seen last year until September and didn't fully fire in three runs, only fifth behind Spycatcher when favourite over C&D on last of them; dangerous if rebounding but has to prove he retains all his ability.
runners.comment: Won this race and the Haydock Sprint Cup in 2024; below par last term; needs to rebound

runners.name: Jasour
runners.spotlight: Not the easiest of rides (sometimes slowly away and can pull hard) but he's smart on his day and he wasn't beaten far when sixth in the July Cup at Newmarket last summer; now having first run since and this is a significant drop in grade; stablemate of James's Delight and gives Clive Cox a strong hand.
runners.comment: Not the easiest of rides but smart on his day; last seen in Group 1 July Cup; interesting

runners.name: Jamess Delight
runners.spotlight: Hampered when fifth to Spycatcher in this race last year; gained ample compensation in Group 2 at the Curragh (6f, good; gamely) two months later; didn't give his running in two Group 1s in the autumn but this is easier and he has gone well when fresh in the past; high on the list.
runners.comment: Met trouble in this race 12 months ago; Group 2 winner after; high on the list

runners.name: Aramram
runners.spotlight: Notably tough and consistent in handicaps, posting another personal best when winning narrowly at Ascot (6f, soft) last October; this Listed event requires another step up the ladder but he always gives his all and it's not out of the question.
runners.comment: Tough and consistent in handicaps; this Listed race demands more but he's not ruled out

runners.name: Myal
runners.spotlight: Unplaced first four starts but most progressive since, winning six of his 12 races and running a cracker when second in Listed event at Redcar last autumn; may not have reached his limit just yet but all his successes have come at 7f and he doesn't have an obvious chance on the ratings.
runners.comment: Progressive 5yo but all wins at 7f and doesn't have an obvious chance on the ratings

runners.name: Caburn
runners.spotlight: Useful 2yo who returned to form to win handicap at Yarmouth (6f, good to soft) last autumn on second run for James Tate; however, he was no threat off his revised mark at Southwell (AW debut) on return, and he looks up against it in this higher grade.
runners.comment: Last year's win came in Yarmouth handicap; looks up against it in this higher grade

runners.name: Super Soldier
runners.spotlight: Winless after debut (5f, good to firm) last spring but he did finish second in Listed race (5f, good to soft) and Group 2 (6f, soft) in France; however, this looks a stern assignment for his reappearance and it's over 20 years since a 3yo won this race.
runners.comment: Made frame in Listed/Group races as 2yo but faces stern assignment on reappearance

runners.name: Annaf
runners.spotlight: Signed off last season with game success in Group 3 at Ascot (6f, soft) in October, and he repeatedly met trouble when only seventh in Saudi Arabia (a race he'd won two years earlier) on return; sole previous run at Doncaster resulted in a win in the Portland; needs considering despite a penalty.
runners.comment: Group 3 winner last autumn; met trouble on reappearance; needs considering despite penalty

runners.name: Art Power
runners.spotlight: Usually races up with pace; fine sprinter over the years but the majority of his big wins have come at the Curragh, where he's won five Group races; well beaten over this C&D when last seen in November and, although he'd be dangerous on a going day, others seem more likely to give their running.
runners.comment: High-class on his day but well beaten when last seen in November; others appeal more

races.name: William Hill Epic Boosts Spring Mile Handicap

runners.name: Classic Encounter
runners.spotlight: Yet to encounter ground softer than good; consistent record in 1m/1m1f races on turf features wins at Redcar/York last April/June and creditable third at Newmarket on last appearance; in the mix.
runners.comment: Consistent record in 1m/1m1f races on turf features two wins; in the mix

runners.name: Mezcala
runners.spotlight: Outstayed over 1m1f when last seen; progressive at 1m otherwise and still has very low mileage; bred to take well to this scenario, being related to 2022 Lincoln winner Johan for same connections; remains of interest.
runners.comment: Has progressive 1m form and low mileage; bred to take well to this scenario; interesting

runners.name: Pressures On
runners.spotlight: Best form over 7f, including second-place finishes on Wolverhampton AW this year; faded into midfield, having raced freely, when joint-favourite for this contest 12 months ago; not crying out for return to 1m.
runners.comment: Best form over 7f; faded into midfield when joint-favourite for this race last year

runners.name: High On Hope
runners.spotlight: AW form this year features two wins in January and creditable fifth in the Lincoln Trial; only 1-14 on turf and looks unfavourably handicapped, being 4lb higher than last appearance in this sphere.
runners.comment: Two AW wins this year; only 1-14 on turf and looks unfavourably handicapped

runners.name: On The River
runners.spotlight: Suited by ground softer than good; record of 11-52 on turf features a win off 2lb higher in the 2025 Carlisle Bell; respectable second to Prosperitas at Doncaster on last appearance; competitively weighted.
runners.comment: Won the 2025 Carlisle Bell off 2lb higher; ran respectably at Doncaster final outing

runners.name: Lir Speciale
runners.spotlight: Ended his 2025 campaign with a heavy-ground 7f double that took his overall record at Doncaster to 3-3; acts on any going and has been very competitive (223) in 1m races on turf; respected kept to this track.
runners.comment: Ended his 2025 campaign with a double that took his overall record at Doncaster to 3-3

runners.name: Carron
runners.spotlight: Only 1-8 on turf but appears to hold a fighting chance with Jack Callan's useful 5lb claim offsetting a penalty for a game success at Newcastle (1m) that took his AW record to 3-4; may do better still for current yard.
runners.comment: Fighting chance with Jack Callan's 5lb claim offsetting a penalty for a game AW win

runners.name: First Ambition
runners.spotlight: Has been unable to sustain his early progress, most recently out of the money when a 20-1 shot in the Lincoln Trial at Wolverhampton; needs to raise his game.
runners.comment: Has been unable to sustain his early progress; needs to raise game

runners.name: Cadarn
runners.spotlight: Two wins at Redcar last spring represent his peak form for George Boughey; 0-6 elsewhere and has something to prove off current mark in this scenario on debut for Kevin Ryan; still with same owner.
runners.comment: Something to prove on debut for new stable, having shown peak form at Redcar

runners.name: Far From Dandy
runners.spotlight: Career-best form (all over 1m) since November features two wins on Dundalk AW and fourth-place finish in the Irish Lincolnshire at the Curragh returned to turf; has frame possibilities off unaltered mark.
runners.comment: Creditable fourth in the Irish Lincolnshire returned to turf; frame possibilities

runners.name: Vincent Rocks
runners.spotlight: Flopped in 1m2f handicap at Newbury when bidding to take record to 3-3; however, gained his novice wins at 1m and may be capable of progress returned to this distance; by no means written off.
runners.comment: Flopped over 1m2f when bidding to take record to 3-3; gained his wins at 1m

runners.name: Milteye
runners.spotlight: Good strike-rate in the second half of last season featured wins in lower grades at Carlisle (1m1f; twice) and Leicester (1m, soft); faces a stiffer assignment off career-high mark at this level, however.
runners.comment: Showed a good strike-rate in second half of last season but this is a harder task
runners.owner: Rise One Racing And W A Bethell

runners.name: Rainbow Nebula
runners.spotlight: Record of 51531 since handicapping includes a couple of respectable efforts at Doncaster (7f, heavy); held on by only a whisker at Newcastle (AW) upped to 1m but may have further improvement in him.
runners.comment: Record of 51531 since handicapping, winning narrowly upped to 1m on AW last time

runners.name: Zowal
runners.spotlight: Won maiden at Musselburgh (1m) on debut for current yard; holds very weak claims on subsequent handicap form and made a respiratory noise in last two races; comes with risk.
runners.comment: Holds very weak claims on handicap form; made respiratory noise the last twice

runners.name: Sterling Knight
runners.spotlight: Record of 8-41 on turf, the most recent win at Doncaster last July; doesn't look the best treated runner in this big field but could go well with AW reappearance under his belt and Toby Moore taking off 7lb.
runners.comment: Gained last win at Doncaster; could go well with Toby Moore taking off 7lb

runners.name: Empirestateofmind
runners.spotlight: Has attempted this contest twice, finishing third in 2022 but only ninth in 2025; the 8yo is unlikely to make it third time lucky, as most of his form since September is in selling races.
runners.comment: Campaigned mostly in selling races since September; third attempt at this prize

runners.name: Naples
runners.spotlight: Raced in four maidens for Paddy Twomey, winning by a neck at Listowel (1m, heavy) the final time; sold for 20,000gns since; goes into rather deep waters on stable/handicap debut but is still unexposed.
runners.comment: Won his final start in maidens for Paddy Twomey; sold for 20,000gns since; unexposed

runners.name: Principality
runners.spotlight: Not disgraced (fifth of 14) in sole Doncaster attempt and current mark is workable; however, may be more interesting when back at Goodwood with this reappearance under his belt, having gained both wins there.
runners.comment: May be more interesting when back at Goodwood, having gained both wins there

runners.name: Intrusively
runners.spotlight: Ties in with Desperate Dan on the C&D form shown on final start for Ed Bethell but it remains to be seen whether that 50-1 effort is backed up; gelded since; still in same ownership; gained sole win in 2yo maiden.
runners.comment: Remains to be seen whether he'll back up 50-1 C&D effort; debut for new yard

runners.name: Prosperitas
runners.spotlight: Record of 2-2 on heavy ground includes a Doncaster success (7f) in first-time cheekpieces when last seen; ideally needs same conditions, being 0-7 on other surfaces; steps up to 1m with the headgear retained.
runners.comment: Won at Doncaster when last seen; ideally needs heavy ground (2-2 on that surface)

runners.name: Desperate Dan
runners.spotlight: An ideal type for this prize having ended last term with a C&D win, showing career-best form; that was on heavy but he seems versatile as regards ground; further progress is not out of the question; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Ended last term with a C&D win, showing career-best form; ideal type for this prize

runners.name: Perfect Part
runners.spotlight: Ran well in the Cammidge on this card 12 months ago but this new trip presents a question mark and she has become a slow-starter, markedly so when last seen; 0-12 since debut win.
runners.comment: New trip presents a question mark and she has become a slow-starter

races.name: William Hill Doncaster Mile Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Docklands
runners.spotlight: Won the Group 1 Queen Anne at Royal Ascot (1m, good to firm) last June; Ascot brings out the best in him but he's run well in defeat in Group 1s elsewhere since that last win, finishing fourth in the Prix Jacques le Marois in August (good to soft) and the Hong Kong Mile in December when last seen; drops back in grade today and he'll be tough to beat if close to his best.
runners.comment: Group 1 performer who drops back in grade and will be tough to beat if close to his best

runners.name: Volterra
runners.spotlight: 0-4 last season but this prominent-racer ran well more often than not, including when third in the Group 3 John Of Gaunt at Haydock (7f, good) on his reappearance; also effective at 1m and on slow ground; there can be optimism he'll be at or close to the top of his game on this seasonal debut; chance.
runners.comment: 0-4 last term but he ran well more often than not and has a positive reappearance record
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Treble Tee
runners.spotlight: Soft-ground debut winner who comfortably landed a C&D handicap (good) last September; failed to kick on subsequently and he's narrowly bottom of this pack on form, but this lightly raced 4yo may still have more to offer and he's the only course winner in the line-up; not discounted.
runners.comment: C&D handicap winner; improvement necessary on reappearance but that can't be ruled out

runners.name: Excellent Believe
runners.spotlight: Three wins from his first five starts before finishing second on final two runs last season at Haydock (1m Group 3, good) and Craon (1m Listed, very soft); he has something to find today but this lightly raced 4yo is open to further improvement this term and could give a good account.
runners.comment: Group 3/Listed runner-up last autumn; something to find but further improvement possible

runners.name: Qirat
runners.spotlight: 150-1 winner of the Group 1 Sussex Stakes at Goodwood (1m, good) in July when acting as a pacemaker; that form can't be taken at face value but he was fifth in the Group 1 City Of York Stakes (7f, good to firm) next time and that performance reads well in the context of most of today's opposition; versatile ground-wise; goes well fresh and could be involved on his reappearance.
runners.comment: Shock 150-1 winner of Group 1 Sussex Stakes; could be involved on his reappearance

races.name: William Hill Lincoln (Heritage Handicap)

runners.name: Eternal Force
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who made it 3-3 since being gelded (1m, good to firm/good to soft) when winning by 3l at Haydock last October; sole runner for trainer who has won this five times and he may well evolve into something better than a handicapper; firmly in calculations up 8lb for last time.
runners.comment: Trainer has won this 5 times; he may well evolve into something better than a handicapper

runners.name: La Botte
runners.spotlight: Went close in the Britannia Handicap at Royal Ascot (1m, good to firm) last June on his fourth start; then absent until the Trial at Wolverhampton (8.6f, AW) three weeks ago, when he was fourth despite a slow start and having to come very wide in the straight; could play a leading role on the back of that eyecatching run, provided conditions suit (unraced on slow turf).
runners.comment: Went close at Royal Ascot last June and eyecatching return to action on AW three weeks ago
runners.owner: Halo Racing N Greenhalgh J Degenhardt

runners.name: Christian David
runners.spotlight: Won at Newbury (7f, good to soft) last September and at Lingfield (7f, AW) in December, and he scored over 1m in 2024; however, he has an up-and-down profile and was well beaten on his reappearance three weeks ago (8.6f, AW); this is tougher than he's used to and he may be best watched.
runners.comment: Two wins in final third of 2025 but this is tougher than he's used to; may be best watched

runners.name: Galeron
runners.spotlight: Winless since September 2022 and 0-9 last year after returning from a stint in Australia; however, last season's best efforts came over C&D, including when fifth in this (good to soft) and runner-up in October (heavy); 7lb lower than 12 months ago and Jack Callan removes valuable 5lb; each-way claims.
runners.comment: 0-9 last season but best efforts came over C&D; Jack Callan removes 5lb; each-way claims

runners.name: Rogue Diplomat
runners.spotlight: Won over 7f here in September (good to soft) and October (soft) to complete a four-timer; all of those wins came by less than 1l, suggesting he's a type who only just does enough, and there may be more left in the tank; worth another crack at 1m and could be in the mix if seeing it out.
runners.comment: Completed 7f four-timer last season (two wins here) and could be more left in the tank

runners.name: Botanical
runners.spotlight: He wasn't at his very best last year for George Boughey, albeit he did finish third in the Listed Doncaster Mile over C&D (good to soft) on this card; now down to a mark 3lb lower than when runner-up in the John Smith's Cup at York (1m2f, good to soft) in July 2024 and his new trainer is a dab hand with topweights in big handicaps; goes well fresh and could have a big say.
runners.comment: On a handy mark and new trainer is a dab hand with topweights in big handicaps
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Greek Order
runners.spotlight: Went off the boil on his final three runs last season but made the frame three times in a row in quality 1m handicaps last summer (good to firm/soft) and he's on a handy mark on that form; perhaps a break and/or the first-time visor will prompt a revival and his stable is in excellent form.
runners.comment: Went off the boil last season but on a handy mark on summer form; a break/visor could help

runners.name: Great Acclaim
runners.spotlight: Thrived in 7f handicaps last season when blinkers went on; however, he failed to shine when tackling 1m last term and reappears minus any headgear (tried cheekpieces when last seen; down the field); others preferred.
runners.comment: Thrived in 7f handicaps last season when blinkers went on but reappears minus headgear

runners.name: Anno Domini
runners.spotlight: Won in summer 2024 on first two starts; 0-4 since but he was inconvenienced by a wide draw when sixth of 14 in handicap at Meydan in January (1m, good) when last seen; could yet be capable of better for top trainer who won this with 4yos in 2016 and 2019; Toby Moore removes 7lb.
runners.comment: Inconvenienced by wide draw at Meydan when last seen; in top hands and still lightly raced

runners.name: Urban Lion
runners.spotlight: Close fifth in the Royal Hunt Cup (1m, good to firm) last June and close third next time, again at Ascot (1m, good to firm); has shown promise on soft ground; not at the top of his game on his final two outings last term but has an each-way shout if back to his best on this reappearance.
runners.comment: Ended last term with 2 lesser runs but each-way shout if back to his best on reappearance

runners.name: Alpha Crucis
runners.spotlight: Good fourth in this in 2024 (soft) and respectable eighth last season (good to soft), making his reappearance each time; runner-up on three of his five subsequent starts last term (good to soft/soft); his four wins have come on soft/heavy going and such testing ground today seems unlikely.
runners.comment: Has previous in this race; wins have been on soft/heavy; better ground is expected today

runners.name: Shout
runners.spotlight: Won at Ascot (1m, soft) last September and very creditable fourth of 20 in another big-field handicap there (1m, good) in October; this 4yo course winner is unexposed over 1m and could have more to offer this season; chance.
runners.comment: Ended last season in fine form in big fields at Ascot and he's unexposed at 1m; chance

runners.name: Theoryofeverything
runners.spotlight: Second of 14 off this mark behind Thunder Roar at Ayr (1m) last September on his favoured soft ground; however, he was below that level more often than not last season and is likely to find this too competitive.
runners.comment: Second off this mark at Ayr last September but he's likely to find this too competitive

runners.name: Tribal Chief
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent last season but he ran a big race first time up when runner-up at Newmarket (1m, good) and he won at Goodwood (1m, good to firm) in August; versatile ground-wise and he might not be far away on his reappearance, for a yard that won this in 2023.
runners.comment: Inconsistent last season but ran big race first time up; might not be far away on comeback

runners.name: Thunder Roar
runners.spotlight: First past the post in the Spring Mile over C&D last March (good to soft; demoted due to interference) and showed even better form in the autumn (on soft/good); a career-best is needed from this 7yo but further improvement can't be ruled out and he has an excellent record at Doncaster; each-way player, in the expectation he's improved for his recent comeback run on AW.
runners.comment: Career-best needed from this 7yo but he improved last year & has an excellent record here

runners.name: Tarkhan
runners.spotlight: Listed win in Germany in October 2024 and went close at Sandown and Newbury last term; however, after a disappointing October he made it 0-10 for the season and this is a tough race in which to try to break his British duck.
runners.comment: German Listed winner but this is a tough race in which to try to break his British duck
runners.owner: R A Pegum And P A Henry

runners.name: Valvano
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 5yo who has run well in defeat more often than not in handicaps, finishing third of 14 over C&D (heavy) in October when last seen; however, he may need testing ground to be a serious force at this trip, having run well at up to 1m4f here in the past; wears first-time tongue-tie/blinkers.
runners.comment: Third over C&D (heavy) in October; may need testing ground to be a serious force at 1m

runners.name: Mirabeau
runners.spotlight: Trainer won this in 2024; ran well when third of 20 off 1lb higher at Ascot (7f, good to soft) in September but unable to match that level on final four starts last season; returns having been gelded; needs something extra.
runners.comment: Not at very best at the end of last season and needs something extra, having been gelded
runners.owner: The Ffrench Revolution & Mrs T Hamilton

runners.name: Bopedro
runners.spotlight: 10yo who frequently runs well in defeat in quality handicaps and he's 5lb lower than when third in the Royal Hunt Cup last June; however, there's a suspicion he'll improve for this comeback run and he's winless since 2023.
runners.comment: Frequently runs well in quality h'caps but may improve for this run and winless since 2023

runners.name: Orandi
runners.spotlight: Won the Irish Lincolnshire (1m, soft) last March before finishing third in this (good to soft) and at the Galway festival (8.5f, yielding); a market check is advised but he's now been below par on each of his last four runs, having finished down the field in the Irish Lincolnshire on his reappearance.
runners.comment: Third in this last year; a market check is advised but a return to form is needed

runners.name: La Trinidad
runners.spotlight: 9yo who went 0-10 last year but he's well handicapped on the pick of last season's efforts, which came in the spring, and his Doncaster form-figures of 1413 catch the eye on his first visit here since 2023; interesting.
runners.comment: 9yo who went 0-10 last year but he's down to a handy mark and has liked it here previously

runners.name: Metal Merchant
runners.spotlight: Well treated on his 2024 form for Jack Channon and he won the Spring Mile at Newbury (1m, good to soft) on his reappearance that season; however, just two runs last term, finishing down the field in April/May, and he has plenty to prove making his stable debut after an absence; blinkers go on.
runners.comment: Well treated on 2024 form but down the field on both runs last season; makes stable debut

races.name: William Hill Best Odds Guaranteed Maiden Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: High Storm
runners.spotlight: Gelded prior to running well at Southwell (1m3f, AW) on reappearance, beating all bar an odds-on Godolphin newcomer; helps to set the standard back on turf and delaying the handicap route for which he's now qualified.
runners.comment: Gelded prior to running well in AW event on reappearance; helps to set the standard

runners.name: Valenday
runners.spotlight: Placed in maidens at York (7f/1m, good) last term; came off a true line both times and ideally needs to learn to keep straighter; otherwise a major contender on form and will stay middle distances if taking after his sire.
runners.comment: Needs to learn to keep straighter but otherwise holds major claims on his York efforts
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Marasi Bay
runners.spotlight: Godolphin debutant who is by Siyouni and the first foal of 7f-1m3f winner (including Listed; RPR 111), from family of good milers Librettist and Dubai Destination; has had wind surgery; heed the market signals.
runners.comment: Godolphin debutant who is by Siyouni and from a good family; has had wind surgery

runners.name: Rebel Legend
runners.spotlight: 42,000euros foal, 110,000gns yearling; Australia half-brother to four winners, notably Irish Legend (1m-1m3f; RPR 98); dam 7f scorer (93); one of two newcomers for the stable and seems the second string.
runners.comment: 110,000gns yearling; by Australia; looks the Haggas second string

runners.name: Super Crown
runners.spotlight: 950,000gns yearling; St Mark's Basilica half-brother to three French winners, notably Ottery (1m-1m4f including Group 3; RPR 109); dam German 1m2f Group 2 scorer (107); entered in the Derby and Irish Derby; respected debutant, particularly if the betting reflects his good credentials.
runners.comment: 950,000gns yearling; by St Mark's Basilica; entered in Derby and Irish Derby; respected

runners.name: Turty Tree
runners.spotlight: Derby entry who finished nicely for eye-catching second of seven, best of the newcomers, in autumn contest at Newmarket (1m, good); should be well suited by middle distances this year; high in calculations.
runners.comment: Derby entry; eye-catching second in autumn contest at Newmarket; high in calculations

runners.name: Sailor Song
runners.spotlight: Finished over 6l behind High Storm in Southwell contest won by a shorter-priced stablemate but seemed in need of the outing/experience; may do better; again one of two runners for his powerful yard.
runners.comment: Finished over 6l behind High Storm at Southwell but seemed to need the run

runners.name: Faeberon
runners.spotlight: Beaten just over 5l, over 1m, in both 2yo runs; has something to find on bare form and may be more interesting in handicaps soon but is bred to improve for the new trip, being by Sea The Stars out of a 1m2f winner.
runners.comment: Has something to find on bare form but is bred to improve for this new trip

runners.name: Three No Trumps
runners.spotlight: Belied his 100-1 odds with keeping-on third of 13 at Kempton (1m, AW) in sole 2yo start; open to progress but needs a big step forward, having recorded an ordinary RPR; low in calculations.
runners.comment: Belied his 100-1 odds with third at Kempton in sole 2yo start (ordinary RPR)

races.name: William Hill NRMB On The Grand National Apprentice Handicap (Go Racing In Yorkshire Series) (Div I)

runners.name: Up The Jazz
runners.spotlight: Has come down in the weights (rated 10lb higher on his 2025 reappearance) and took a step back in the right direction when 2l third over the extended 1m at Wolverhampton last Friday; his latest turf win came over 1m2f on good to soft; one to consider.
runners.comment: Down in weights and better signs on AW last week; one to consider back on turf

runners.name: Blackwater Lilly
runners.spotlight: Won a maiden hurdle last May but disappointing on AW Flat this winter and a well-held fourth back hurdling at Sedgefield 18 days ago.
runners.comment: Hurdle winner but disappointing on AW this winter; well-held fourth back hurdling latest

runners.name: Yokohama
runners.spotlight: Drawn a blank since AW debut success but was placed over this trip on turf (good/good to firm) off higher marks last season; hard to get too excited by this winter's AW efforts but it's possible an eight-week break could have had a reviving effect; one to note in the betting.
runners.comment: Placed off higher marks on turf last year and needs keeping an eye on in the betting

runners.name: Bownder
runners.spotlight: Successful over this trip on Polytrack at Lingfield in December and mostly creditable efforts since, including second back at Lingfield (1m2f again) last time; just as effective on turf (won at Goodwood off 1lb lower last summer); acts on soft; has to enter calculations.
runners.comment: Arrives in form from AW and just as good on turf; has to enter calculations

runners.name: Powerful Response
runners.spotlight: Mostly creditable in-frame efforts on AW this winter but below-par fifth last time; has finished fourth and fifth in this race in the last two years.
runners.comment: Mostly creditable efforts on AW this winter; fourth and fifth in this in recent years

runners.name: Sassy Glory
runners.spotlight: Her breakthrough win at Newcastle (1m, Tapeta) in September came on her 23rd career start; below-par fourth there (1m again) when last seen in October; stays 1m2f and acts on good to soft; has run well fresh before but her record suggests it's probably best to look elsewhere for win purposes.
runners.comment: 1-25, the win coming over 1m on AW last autumn; probably best to look elsewhere
runners.owner: Ursa Major & Mark Walford Racing Club

runners.name: Hibernate
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who won off 2lb lower last summer and was a good second off this mark on his next two outings (all 1m2f, good; also acts on soft); interesting on return under Toby Moore.
runners.comment: Front-runner; nicely treated on last year's turf form; interesting under Toby Moore

runners.name: Sovereign Applause
runners.spotlight: Eight-race maiden who was a well-beaten 33-1 shot at Southwell (2m; first-time tongue-tie) in January; this is more realistic than the Listed hurdle she tackled here last time but she's still hard to make a strong case for; has first-time cheekpieces added to the tongue-tie now.
runners.comment: Well held on AW Flat in January and in course Listed hurdle since; others preferred

runners.name: Dissident
runners.spotlight: Won over hurdles here (good to soft) in December but has disappointed since, including back on the Flat (2m, AW) last week; it remains to be seen whether this drop back in trip improves matters.
runners.comment: Struggled since course hurdle win in December, including back on Flat last week
runners.owner: Ben Haynes And Mrs Samantha Dunn

runners.name: Emery Down
runners.spotlight: AW maiden winner last spring but has drawn a blank in eight handicaps since, including well held over C&D on her only start beyond an extended 1m; first start for new stable after leaving John Butler.
runners.comment: AW maiden winner but drawn a blank in eight handicaps since; first run for new yard

runners.name: Mao Shang Wong
runners.spotlight: Comfortably off the mark when seeing off nine rivals over 1m2f at Newcastle nine days ago; can race keenly and that improved performance coincided with the first time he was partnered by Danny Tudhope but he won readily enough to think he can cope with a 5lb rise if he's not jockey reliant.
runners.comment: Comfortable winner over 1m2f on Newcastle's AW last week and a 5lb rise looks manageable

runners.name: Glint Of Light
runners.spotlight: Best effort in her six outings for Hughie Morrison last season when winning 1m2f Ffos Las handicap (good to soft) in cheekpieces; a first-time hood is the choice of headgear setting out for a new yard after 197 days off; less exposed than most of these and would need considering if the betting vibes are positive.
runners.comment: 1m2f win for Hughie Morrison last year; fairly unexposed on return for new yard; hood on
runners.owner: E S-O Racing Club And Partner

runners.name: Expressionless
runners.spotlight: Came from last to first in a seven-runner race at Nottingham (1m6f, good to soft) last October; not in anything like the same form when a remote fourth at Newbury (1m4f, heavy) on final start; races mainly over further but he was a creditable third over this trip on his 2025 reappearance.
runners.comment: Races mainly over further but creditable third over 1m2f on last year's reappearance

runners.name: Electric Avenue
runners.spotlight: Won two fillies' handicaps (7f and 1m) at Newcastle in November but has lost her form in recent AW starts; it remains to be seen whether a return to turf and step up in trip sparks a revival.
runners.comment: AW wins over 7f/1m in November but lost way on AW recently; something to prove

runners.name: Avatar Jet
runners.spotlight: Plenty of good efforts in handicaps over a variety of trips (including an extended 1m2f) on good/good to yielding in Ireland for Mark Fahey in 2024; sold for 27,000euros in November of that year; absent since but he's been given a chance by the handicapper so is worth monitoring in the market now starting out for Declan Carroll.
runners.comment: Nicely treated on his Irish form in 2024 but not seen since; the betting should help guide

races.name: William Hill NRMB On The Grand National Apprentice Handicap (Go Racing In Yorkshire Series) (Div II)

runners.name: Tee Aitch Aye
runners.spotlight: Came good on soft ground at Hamilton (1m1f) last year; hard to be enthused by his recent 1m4f AW reappearance but a revival wouldn't come as a bolt out of the blue given he's only 1lb above that win mark.
runners.comment: AW reappearance poor but has dipped to only 1lb above mark he defied on turf last year

runners.name: Hashtagnotions
runners.spotlight: All four wins around 1m but last week's creditable reappearance third of ten to Mao Shang Wong (runs in first division) at Newcastle came over 1m2f; successful on soft; won't need to pull out much more with a first-time hood added to his regular cheekpieces.
runners.comment: All four wins over 1m but last week's creditable reappearance third came over 1m2f

runners.name: Relevant Range
runners.spotlight: In fine form in 0-50 classified events in a busy January (22131) but has found things a bit tougher in handicaps since; back on turf with his stamina not totally assured (very best form around 1m).
runners.comment: In fine form in classified events in January but found it tougher in handicaps since

runners.name: Miners Gamble
runners.spotlight: Fourth is the best he's managed in seven AW outings this winter, despite being prominent in the market a few times; his sole win from 24 starts came on turf but his claims still aren't obvious.
runners.comment: AW exploits of late don't suggest he's likely to add to his sole win this afternoon

runners.name: Scarfo
runners.spotlight: Sole win from 28 starts was in July 2024; hasn't shown enough in two AW classified events in recent months to think a second win is imminent, particularly as he's 6lb out of handicap here.
runners.comment: Just one win to his name and that's unlikely to change here from out of the handicap

runners.name: Golspie
runners.spotlight: Mostly creditable efforts on Tapeta this winter but the handicapper has been winning the argument lately and he returns to turf off 2lb higher than when successful at Pontefract (1m2f, good) last summer; likely to be on the premises but might be vulnerable for the win.
runners.comment: Nothing obviously in hand of his mark but he's been in decent form on AW; place claims

runners.name: Dancing Tiger
runners.spotlight: Ploughed through the mud to get off the mark at Goodwood (1m2f) last September but failed to reproduce that level, despite reaching the frame, on his final two outings; been off for five months.
runners.comment: Goodwood winner over this trip but below best final two starts; been off for five months

runners.name: Fire Eyes
runners.spotlight: Won three turf handicaps in 2025, all in a tongue-tie and the latest with cheekpieces on for the first time; without any headgear when sixth on her recent Wolverhampton reappearance and the accessories are missing again.
runners.comment: Three turf wins last year but will need to step up on last week's AW return to add another

runners.name: Recobella
runners.spotlight: Came good at the second time of asking for Phil Kirby in a 1m2f Nottingham classified event (good) last July; second in a soft-ground handicap at Beverley six days later; failed to fire in two subsequent runs in the autumn but the yard was quiet at the time; been off for five months.
runners.comment: 1m2f win last July; second on soft next time; given break since below-par runs in autumn

runners.name: Three Dons
runners.spotlight: Multiple Flat wins at 1m-1m4f, including on heavy; unable to make a significant impact on the Flat last autumn but he's run with credit over hurdles in recent months and is well treated back on the level (6lb below his last winning mark); considered.
runners.comment: Multiple Flat winner who is on a good mark; arrives in form from hurdling; considered

runners.name: Its Only Fun
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark at Musselburgh (1m1f) last April but ended his time with Jennie Candlish below par and has finished down the field in two outings for this yard over four months apart.
runners.comment: Won off 2lb higher last spring but hard to be enthused by his two runs for this yard

runners.name: Bearwith
runners.spotlight: C&D winner (also good second on his other visit) who got his head back in front at Newcastle (1m2f) last month; too keen in a steadily run race back there since and worth treating as if still in form; the draw could have been kinder but still has a lot more going for him than most.
runners.comment: C&D winner; back to winning ways at Newcastle last month and excuse there since; respected

runners.name: Distinction
runners.spotlight: Third in this race last year and won over 1m2f (twice, including on soft) and 1m3f in a productive spell last summer; the final win came off this mark so he's a player if ready to roll on reappearance.
runners.comment: Three wins last summer; back on winning mark and third in this last year; claims

runners.name: Endless Whisper
runners.spotlight: Emerged from off the pace to be second in smallish fields over 1m4f here (good/good to firm) last summer, her first two handicaps; absent since finishing tailed off over 2m on AW in July but the first of those runner-up efforts here came off a six-month break so she's evidently capable when fresh; not discounted.
runners.comment: Runner-up over 1m4f here in first two handicaps; off since July but still respected

runners.name: Bay Dream Believer
runners.spotlight: Reeled off a fast-ground hat-trick over this trip last August; also runner-up off this mark at Redcar (1m2f, good to soft) in the autumn; given a break since a lesser run at Newcastle in November; capable when fresh; could go well.
runners.comment: Has gone well fresh before and her mark is feasible; enters calculations

races.name: Virgin Bet Handicap

runners.name: Im Workin On It
runners.spotlight: Prominent racer who began the year with two C&D wins; creditable effort over 1m 24 days ago and he'll be happier back at this trip; others look better handicapped though.
runners.comment: Two C&D wins this year but came up short in a 1m handicap last time; others better treated

runners.name: Golden Redemption
runners.spotlight: Began his career on dirt in the UAE but he showed better form on turf for Andrew Balding in the second half of 2025, winning a 6f maiden at Newbury before placed efforts in handicaps at Newbury (7f) and Newmarket (6f); returns to action with his stable in fine form; contender.
runners.comment: Progressive after joining this yard last year; one consider with the stable going so well

runners.name: Eminency
runners.spotlight: He has turned in a string of good efforts since winning at Windsor last September; close second off this mark at Newcastle on New Year's Day (6f); no obvious reason why he wouldn't make another bold bid.
runners.comment: String of good runs since September; should be in the shake-up once again

runners.name: Sisyphean
runners.spotlight: Down the field in Dubai on his stable debut in December (1m1f, good to firm); can go well fresh so a 106-day absence no bad thing; hasn't run over 7f since his debut but he lurks on a dangerous mark and new cheekpieces could give him a lift.
runners.comment: On a handy mark if dealing with the drop in trip; headgear now added; check betting

runners.name: Bold Impact
runners.spotlight: Only seen twice since her runaway Epsom success (7f, soft; blinkered first time) in September 2024; sold our of Ralph Beckett's yard for 32,000gns last October; goes without headgear on his stable debut; yard also run I'm Working On It.
runners.comment: Sold for 32,000gns since last seen; no headgear on stable debut; yard run two here

runners.name: Al Arbeed
runners.spotlight: Disappointed at Wolverhampton on his final run last season but his other two AW starts last year read well; brings potential to this reappearance and he is high on the list.
runners.comment: Absent since disappointing at Wolverhampton in November; not fully exposed; interesting

runners.name: Electrifarhh
runners.spotlight: 1m win here on her second 2yo start; couldn't cope with Blue Bolt in a Windsor novice last May but that rival was getting 6lb and ended 2025 with a BHA rating of 110 so it was a tough task; she has another absence to defy but brings potential to her handicap debut.
runners.comment: 1m course win as a 2yo; good 2nd in sole run in 2025; brings potential to handicap debut

runners.name: Cogitate
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with three solid runs in 1m handicaps here; he has more to do in this grade (unplaced all 12 runs above Class 4) after five months off but Billy Loughnane has been booked.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 campaign with some good course runs; unplaced all 12 starts above Class 4

runners.name: Defence Minister
runners.spotlight: Good efforts in 7f handicaps at Sandown and Goodwood last July; had excuses at Doncaster when last seen; been gelded since; on a good mark but this free-going sort could do with settling better.
runners.comment: Two good 7f handicap runs last July; excuses latest; been gelded; needs to settle better

races.name: Virgin Bet Queen's Prize Handicap (London Stayers' Series Qualifier) (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Sheradann
runners.spotlight: Not the most consistent but won off this mark over C&D in October and ran well at Lingfield on penultimate outing; a bit below form when hooded first time 15 days ago in a small field; player if it's one of his good days.
runners.comment: Not the most consistent but won off this mark over C&D in October; ran well two runs back

runners.name: Belgravian
runners.spotlight: Second on Chelmsford Polytrack in latest AW race; tremendous progress last year once sent handicapping in cheekpieces, racking up four wins and the last three of those (in small fields) were over 2m, increasingly impressive; improvement stalled at Newmarket in the Cesarewitch Trial and Cesarewitch itself on last two outings, but he remains of major interest on this return.
runners.comment: Improvement stalled on last two outings last season but he remains of major interest

runners.name: Trojan Storm
runners.spotlight: Effective here; finished strongly when upped to 1m6f for his win at Ascot last July; absent 167 days afterwards and beat a total of one rival in his three races this winter but they were over just 1m-1m2f, when an outsider, and he should be vastly more interesting on this first crack at 2m.
runners.comment: Finished strongly for his 1m6f Ascot win last July, whereas recent runs were over 1m-1m2f

runners.name: Anniversary
runners.spotlight: 4yo who has had only six races; moved up to an extended 1m6f and 2m on last two outings, neither of which really proved his stamina; tried blinkers on first occasion (last June) and never got seriously involved without them in a slowly run race on AW debut at Lingfield last time (favourite four weeks ago); blinkers return and 5lb claimer is enlisted.
runners.comment: Only six races, favourite on recent return from layoff; bit to prove with blinkers and 2m

runners.name: Brasil Power
runners.spotlight: Basically progressive in a busy winter AW campaign and probably stays 2m, although the last three wins were over 1m4f (twice) and 1m6f (latest start); raised another 3lb but needs respect.
runners.comment: Basically progressive in busy winter and probably stays 2m, although wins were at shorter

runners.name: Blindedbythelights
runners.spotlight: Versatile tactically; off since last July but short-headed last May after a longer absence and he was also runner-up on his two other starts last term, taking his handicap record to 121224222; upped to 2m with cheekpieces enlisted for last two outings, one of which was on Chelmsford Polytrack; edging up the weights but should go well again.
runners.comment: 121224222 in handicaps, at 2m in cheekpieces for last two outings; layoff shouldn't worry

runners.name: Lavender Hill Mob
runners.spotlight: 1m4f winner from the front (under Mason Paetel) at Newmarket last May who also scored with a prominent ride over hurdles at Cheltenham (2m5f, soft) in November; not seen since tailed off trying nearly 3m in December; returns to Flat with a bit to prove on AW and over 2m on Flat.
runners.comment: Off since December; returns to Flat with a bit to prove on AW and over 2m on Flat

runners.name: Spirit Mixer
runners.spotlight: Won the Northumberland Plate at Newcastle (2m, Tapeta) last June and bagged a second valuable 2m handicap success of the year at Chester in September; however, disappointing in this race in 2024 on his only race at Kempton and this stage of the season may not be the time to catch him.
runners.comment: Two major 2025 wins include Northumberland Plate; return may not be the time to catch him

runners.name: Align The Stars
runners.spotlight: On a losing run of 13 since his hat-trick in summer 2024; has dropped in the weights, was thereabouts on occasions in 2025 and it can be argued that today's headgear switch will help, but there's a bit for him to prove on AW (it's only his third AW race) and others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: On a losing run of 13 since his hat-trick in summer 2024; only his third AW race

races.name: Virgin Bet A Good Bet Rosebery Handicap (London Middle Distance Series Qualifier) (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Respond
runners.spotlight: Solid efforts in defeat in his first three handicaps, before taking his form to another level when winning a six-runner race at Chelmsford (1m2f, Polytrack) 16 days ago; he was 1-2 that day, made most and won in good style; 8lb higher and not nearly so well drawn but this could still be a good mark.
runners.comment: 1-2, won six-runner race at Chelmsford (1m2f, Polytrack) 16 days ago in good style; up 8lb

runners.name: Whitcombe Rockstar
runners.spotlight: Most of his wins have been at 1m but he swooped late on to score over 1m3f in January and led 1f out when asserting in tidy style over 1m4f early this month, both at this track; back up another 5lb and he's a 7yo, but peak form (October 2024) was a little better still and he's now 7-12 at Kempton; on the shortlist.
runners.comment: 7yo on hat-trick; peak form (October 2024) was a little better and he's 7-12 at Kempton

runners.name: Triple Double A
runners.spotlight: Won three in a row to kick off his 3yo and handicap careers last term; resumed progress when reappearing to score in six-runner race at Newcastle (Tapeta) last month on the step up to 1m2f; up 4lb in this much harder race but tries another new trip and is unexposed.
runners.comment: Reappeared to score in six-runner race at Newcastle (Tapeta) last month on step up to 1m2f

runners.name: Rathgar
runners.spotlight: Ran creditably on first three starts last season, before two lesser displays in the summer; versatile ground-wise on turf and although he's raced only twice on AW, in first half of 2024, the first of those runs was creditable; the widest draw does not help today, however.
runners.comment: Ran creditably on first three starts last season, before two lesser displays in the summer

runners.name: Marhaba Ghaiyyath
runners.spotlight: Dual AW winner early in career; ran well for most of 2025 and his form peaked when he went close at Glorious Goodwood (1m2f) in July; not beaten far at York next time but he comprehensively failed to live up to his market prominence in the Cambridgeshire at Newmarket on final outing (his first race since gelded); no way does that rule him out today.
runners.comment: No way does his poor show in the Cambridgeshire (just 11-1) last time rule him out today

runners.name: Gamrai
runners.spotlight: Won a maiden at Windsor (1m2f, good to firm) in August on reappearance last term; no further progress when favourite in his only two subsequent starts (handicap debut on latest) but gelded later in the autumn and, after just four races, he should retain potential.
runners.comment: Did not live up to bright start to last season but gelded and has raced just four times

runners.name: Night Breeze
runners.spotlight: 0-10 on AW compared to 5-15 on turf; beaten about only 3l on last two outings and placed earlier at this track, but he needs to find extra to win in this sphere and this race looks too hot.
runners.comment: 0-10 on AW compared to 5-15 on turf; needs to find extra to win in this sphere

runners.name: Kings Code
runners.spotlight: Busy 6yo who is 10-28 on AW, with three of those wins last autumn (two C&D) and latest at Newcastle in January; the handicapper has reacted, though, and the creditable efforts of last two starts weren't nearly so rewarding.
runners.comment: The handicapper has reacted and last two creditable efforts weren't nearly so rewarding

runners.name: Real Dream
runners.spotlight: No win since July 2023 and 0-8 since joining this stable but most of those runs were creditable, including his third in this race last year and close fourth in Saudi Arabia six weeks ago; place chance.
runners.comment: No win since July 2023 but most runs for this yard were creditable; 3rd in this in 2025

runners.name: Military Academy
runners.spotlight: Ex-Gosden 5yo; something of a revival with his second and third in small fields at Lingfield (1m2f) on last two outings for current yard, the latter a Group 3; bit more to prove back in the different scenario of this handicap but, with 7lb claimer recruited, he's not entirely dismissed.
runners.comment: Something of a revival lately; bit more to prove in different scenario of this handicap

runners.name: Saint Etienne
runners.spotlight: Won three AW handicaps during his 22-race French career, plus a turf win at Longchamp last May; those have earned him a lofty mark and he's not been seen since a 100,000euros sale last July; also not proven beyond 1m2f and he was tongue tied for all his French races, but isn't this time and he wore today's hood only twice (2024); there are probably more solid options.
runners.comment: Ex-French, triple AW winner; 100,000euros buy; not proven over this far; tongue-tie is off
runners.owner: The Cat And The Mag Partnership

runners.name: El Burhan
runners.spotlight: Won on reappearance at Chester (7.5f, good) last May and tapped back into that promise for a clearcut win at Ayr (1m2f, good to soft) in September; up 6lb for this first run since and it's also his AW debut, but he probably retains potential.
runners.comment: Clearcut win at Ayr (1m2f, good to soft) in September; up 6lb and this is also AW debut

runners.name: Mustazeed
runners.spotlight: Increasing tendency to blow the start last season culminated in a refusal to race on final appearance; that was at Newbury, where he'd previously been at his best; he's contrastingly 0-6 on AW and none of his form in this sphere lives up to today's mark; probably best watched.
runners.comment: Increasing tendency to blow the start saw refusal to race on his final go; best at Newbury

races.name: Virgin Bet Snowdrop Fillies' Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Survie
runners.spotlight: Smart in France at up to 1m4f; sold for 1,900,000gns in December; capitalised on a straightforward opportunity on her stable debut at Lingfield in January (1m2f) before keeping on for a never-dangerous third in a valuable Group 1 in Saudi Arabia six weeks ago (extended 1m2f); 3-3 on AW and holds leading form claims but this drop in trip is a slight query.
runners.comment: Third in valuable Group 1 (10.5f) in Saudi Arabia six weeks ago; 3-3 on AW; drops in trip

runners.name: Cathedral
runners.spotlight: She is yet to add to her debut success (6f, Polytrack; only AW run thus far) but was highly tried last year and finished fourth in Group 1 events on three occasions, notably the Breeders' Cup Filly and Mare Turf (1m3f, firm) when last seen in November; down in trip/grade for her return and she looks a major player.
runners.comment: Failed to win last year but finished close up in 4th in three Group 1s; leading contender

runners.name: All Moonshine
runners.spotlight: Improved with each of her four starts; completed her hat-trick when making all in a small-field fillies' handicap over C&D last month; should still have more to offer but she is pitched in much deeper today.
runners.comment: Won her last three, including C&D latest; can do better but this is a big step up in class

runners.name: Never Let Go
runners.spotlight: Ran on well to win the Sandringham Stakes at Royal Ascot last summer; ended that campaign with two fair runs in Listed company; others have stronger form claims but she has only had eight starts and could progress again this year.
runners.comment: Royal Ascot winner last June; fair form in Listed races after; still has time to do better

runners.name: Sweet Princess
runners.spotlight: Confirmed the promise of her Wolverhampton debut in December when easily seeing off two rivals over 1m at Newcastle last month; that form is well short of what will be needed at this level but the fact she's been pitched into Listed company suggests she can take a good step forward.
runners.comment: Straightforward task last month and won easily; huge step up in class but bred to be smart
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Radiant Beauty
runners.spotlight: Four wins in 2025, two of them at this track; big step up in class after six months off and others have much more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Won four times last year, two of them here, but looks to be flying too high on her return

runners.name: American Gal
runners.spotlight: C&D winner; advanced her form last year, winning a Listed race in France and finishing second in a Group 3 at Ascot; ended her 3yo campaign with two lesser efforts but she has run well on her seasonal return in both seasons of racing; each-way shout.
runners.comment: C&D winner; second in a Group 3 last summer; can go well fresh; each-way shout

runners.name: Glistening
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the seventh attempt when winning a C&D fillies' novice last month; well beaten in a Southwell handicap since; stiff task hiked in class with cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Won a fillies' novice over C&D last month but she has a mountain to climb at this level

runners.name: Glittering Surf
runners.spotlight: C&D win on debut in December 2024; made a winning reappearance on this card 12 months ago, again over this trip; two subsequent turf defeats in Listed/Group 3 company suggest she has her work cut out but returning to the AW could spark something extra.
runners.comment: 2-2 over C&D; improvement necessary but not out of the question

runners.name: Pina Sonata
runners.spotlight: Won a fillies' maiden on her reappearance last April (8.6f, Tapeta); better form when defying a penalty at Leicester one month later; came up well short in Group 1/Listed company in the second half of 2025; could resume her progress this year but she will find easier Listed races than this one.
runners.comment: Needs to raise her game but after just five runs that is a possibility

runners.name: Miss Nightfall
runners.spotlight: Just one win to her name and she has done much of her racing over shorter trips; did run well at Royal Ascot on her only 1m start, finishing sixth of 25 behind Never Let Go (1lb better off for a 3.5l defeat); promise on AW; may progress again this year.
runners.comment: Promise in one attemt at 1m but improvement is essential judged on the balance of her form

runners.name: Betty Clover
runners.spotlight: Useful 2yo; had some good form early in her 3yo campaign too and although her form dropped off on her final two starts, soft ground may not have been ideal; half-sister to an AW winner so the surface shouldn't be an issue; even her best efforts need bettering in this field though.
runners.comment: Listed winner at two; came to hand early last year; needs improvement for the switch to AW

races.name: Virgin Bet Daily Extra Places/British EBF Fillies' Conditions Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Spinning Lizzie
runners.spotlight: She could do with settling better now up in trip for her reappearance but the removal of cheekpieces could help; went close in 7f nursery at Goodwood last July before respectable fifth of seven in 7f Group 3 there in August when last seen; entitled to respect on her first AW start.
runners.comment: Could do with settling better now up in trip but she's entitled to respect on her 2yo form

runners.name: Ellusive Butterfly
runners.spotlight: Listed third at Deauville (7f, soft) last July on second start and she was racing for the first time since when readily winning a maiden at Southwell (7f, Tapeta) in January; this is a tougher test of her credentials but she's unexposed and has shaped as though she'll be suited by 1m.
runners.comment: Readily made all in 7f Southwell maiden; this is tougher but she's unexposed; 1m can suit

runners.name: Mystic Moment
runners.spotlight: Her eight 2yo runs for Eve Johnston Houghton yielded one win, a 7f maiden at Epsom in July; looked exposed as the season went on and improvement is needed on her stable/seasonal debut; remains in same ownership.
runners.comment: Her eight 2yo runs yielded one win (7f maiden); looked exposed; has changed stables

runners.name: Ourbren
runners.spotlight: 0-5 last season but she had some tough assignments, and ran well when 25-1 third in a Listed race at Newbury (7f, soft) in October; a reproduction of that form would put her firmly in the picture on this AW/seasonal debut.
runners.comment: 0-5 last season but posted a Listed third in October; that form puts her firmly in picture

runners.name: Eskimo Pie
runners.spotlight: Highly tried during last season's four-race, winless campaign, the highlight her 3l seventh of 23 in the Queen Mary at Royal Ascot (5f, good to firm); goes beyond 6f for the first time and has been absent since July, but she can play a leading role if at the top of her game and seeing out 1m.
runners.comment: Up in trip following an absence but showed considerable promise at Royal Ascot last June

runners.name: Tryst
runners.spotlight: Improved with each of her three 2yo starts and won on AW debut at Wolverhampton (8.6f, Tapeta) in October when last seen; did it nicely and there can be optimism she'll have more to offer this season; respected.
runners.comment: Won at Wolverhampton on third and final 2yo start; should have more to offer this season

races.name: Virgin Bet/British EBF Conditions Stakes (Colts & Geldings)

runners.name: Sunset On Leros
runners.spotlight: Created a positive impression when winning a 7f novice at Doncaster on his second 2yo run; better form when a close fourth in Listed company at the St Leger meeting and ended that campaign with a close third in an Italian Group 3; stepping up to 1m should suit; more to come.
runners.comment: Promising 2yo, last seen finishing third in a Group 3 in Italy; 1m will suit; e-w shout

runners.name: Rochfortbridge
runners.spotlight: Highly tried after making a successful racecourse debut at Ayr last summer and he came up short, albeit he ran well in the Acomb at York and when runner-up in Listed company last October; the switch to AW shouldn't present an issue but others appeal more for win purposes.
runners.comment: Highly tried after a winning debut last summer; this more manageable but still vulnerable

runners.name: Archer Royal
runners.spotlight: Made a winning debut in a 7f novice at Chelmsford last August; came up short in two Group 3 events at Newmarket in the autumn, after which he was gelded; the Gosden stable has won three of the last seven runnings of the race but each of those was with a once-raced novice winner.
runners.comment: Limitations exposed in Group 3 company after a winning debut; may come up short again

runners.name: Conclave
runners.spotlight: Enjoyed the run of the race but couldn't see off the challenge of a well-backed newcomer over C&D 24 days ago (pair clear), only to be awarded the race after the first past the post weighed in light; that rival's subsequent impressive Southwell win adds some substance to the form but this one will need a good jolt of progress in this company.
runners.comment: Solid effort over C&D on debut 24 days ago; up in grade but capable of better

runners.name: Hes Waliim
runners.spotlight: Absent since a heavy defeat in a York nursery last October (1m, good; strong favourite); gelded over the winter; even his peak 2yo efforts will need bettering in this field.
runners.comment: Absent since flopping at York in October; gelded over the winter; improvement a must

runners.name: Kings Trail
runners.spotlight: Fine pedigree and he made a perfect start to his career when scoring by 2.25l in a C&D novice 117 days ago (5-6f); clearly in need of the experience on that occasion but he will have learned plenty, having to be switched in the straight and then picking up well without his rider having to get too serious; he has more to do today but he looks a potential Pattern-race performer; strong claims for a yard which took this with the subsequent 2000 Guineas winner in 2024.
runners.comment: Clearly green but ran out the cosy winner of a C&D novice on debut; potentially high class

runners.name: Sin City
runners.spotlight: Made all the running (at a slow pace) in a 1m maiden at Lingfield five weeks ago, the front two pulling well clear of the third; sent off the odds-on favourite there so a big run was clearly expected; can do better but this will be a stiffer test.
runners.comment: Clear with the 2nd when rewarding odds-on backers at Lingfield last month; in 2000 Guineas

runners.name: Lord Britain
runners.spotlight: 7f course win last August and he then ran to a similar level under a penalty one month later; 250-1 and tailed off in the Group 2 Royal Lodge Stakes when last seen; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Two solid course runs (7f) as a 2yo but his form lags behind several of today's opponents

runners.name: Lake Como
runners.spotlight: Ran just once for Aidan O'Brien before changing hands for 16,000gns last October; proving money well spent by new connections, improving with each start and unextended to win a novice at Newcastle (7f) and a 1m handicap at Southwell on his last two starts; unlikely he has reached his peak just yet but he is in deeper today and he has to concede weight all round.
runners.comment: Improving run by run but this race will be a much stiffer test of his credentials

races.name: Virgin Bet A Good Bet Handicap (London Sprint Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Fast Track Harry
runners.spotlight: He has taken well to AW this winter, winning 6f handicaps at Lingfield and Newcastle; up only 7lb for those wins and it's quite possible there is more in the tank; not ideally drawn but Ferrous overcame stall 11 in this race two years ago.
runners.comment: 2-2 on AW this year; doubt his progress has finished yet and he's high on the list

runners.name: Wild Clary
runners.spotlight: Just one win to his name after 12 starts but he ended his 3yo campaign with two fine efforts, notably when chasing home a well-backed sprinter at Haydock in September (6f, good to soft; traffic problems at a crucial stage); 2lb higher mark less of a concern than the fact he has been well beaten in his two seasonal debut runs.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 with two fine handicap efforts; record fresh tempers enthusiasm slightly

runners.name: Fleetwater
runners.spotlight: Both her wins have come at Windsor in midsummer; she made an encouraging seasonal return when second of seven in a fillies' handicap at Southwell two weeks ago but another step forward will be required to trouble the principals today.
runners.comment: Solid reappearance at Southwell two weeks ago but she's up in class and looks vulnerable
runners.owner: Ms H N Pinniger And Partner

runners.name: Stanley Spencer
runners.spotlight: Two-time C&D winner; has been competitive off this sort of mark in recent months, beaten two noses into third over C&D 24 days ago (blinkered first time); up in class but likely to give it another good go.
runners.comment: Conditions to suit and he ran well here last time (blinkered first time); up in class

runners.name: City Cyclone
runners.spotlight: Arrives seeking a four-timer, two of the wins over C&D, and he hasn't been treated punitively by the assessor having gone up just 6lb; this is a much stiffer test of his credentials but there are no doubts surrounding his wellbeing.
runners.comment: Won his last three starts and only gone up 6lb; rise in class may just find him out though

runners.name: Change Sings
runners.spotlight: Three-time course winner; better than ever last season despite failing to win; 1lb lower than when a close third in the corresponding race 12 months ago; another big run on the cards.
runners.comment: Third in this race last year off 1lb higher; another big run on the cards

runners.name: Durham Castle
runners.spotlight: Lightly-raced 5yo who has won three of his six starts; readily justified short-priced favouritism at Windsor (6f, good to firm) when last seen in June and it is unlikely this mark is beyond him; takes on match-fit rivals here though and may come on for the run.
runners.comment: Unexposed 5yo; this mark unlikely to prove beyond him but absent 285 days

runners.name: Brian
runners.spotlight: Useful on his day and he was a good second at Newbury last October (7f, soft) off this mark; effective over C&D but an extra furlong probably wouldn't go amiss.
runners.comment: Feasible mark and has C&D form; would be more appealing over 7f though

runners.name: Tuco Salamanca
runners.spotlight: Hold-up sprinter who won five times in 2025; fifth in a big-field event at the Curragh last September when he had to wait for an age for a gap to appear; appeals as the type to progress again this year and he's high on the list, for all he will need luck in running.
runners.comment: Hugely progressive 3yo; will need luck in running but he remains capable of better

runners.name: Seraphim Angel
runners.spotlight: Last seen making all to win a 6f handicap (Class 4) at Windsor 257 days ago; has since left Tom Dascombe; up in class and could prove vulnerable; connections also run Tuco Salamanca.
runners.comment: Absent since making all at Windsor (6f) 257 days ago; new yard; up in class

runners.name: Knebworth
runners.spotlight: 2-2 this year, winning 6f handicaps at Chelmsford in January and at Wolverhampton three weeks ago; 6lb rise for latest success takes him to a career-high mark but he is well drawn and should give it another good go.
runners.comment: 6f wins at Chelmsford and Wolverhampton this year; career-high mark but thriving

races.name: QuinnBet Daily Free Bet Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Saccary
runners.spotlight: Won on the Flat in Germany and France in his youth; 0-7 over hurdles but he was a creditable second off a reduced mark at Huntingdon (2m5f, good) 17 days ago; would have finished closer if he didn't make mistakes at the last two hurdles and he's a player if he can repeat that form back in trip.
runners.comment: 0-7 over hurdles but was a creditable second at Huntingdon latest; respected back in trip

runners.name: Laplander
runners.spotlight: 0-8 on the Flat and he's been well held at biggish prices in three hurdle runs this winter; now switches to a handicap but opening mark looks on the high side and he needs improvement.
runners.comment: 0-8 on Flat and well held in three hurdles runs this winter; now switches to a handicap
runners.owner: A Lofts C & R Russell & Partner

runners.name: Son Of Tyran
runners.spotlight: Went close in a point and bumper in his first two starts but he has form figures of P0000 since switched to hurdling in October; with top yard and this is only his second handicap but he needs a transformation.
runners.comment: Unexposed 5yo and this is only his second handicap but he needs a transformation

runners.name: Garavogue
runners.spotlight: Showed promise in novice/maiden events including an 8l third of 13 at Hereford (2m, heavy) last month; was never travelling when pulled up on handicap debut there (2m6f, soft) five weeks ago but he was prominent in the market and it's still early days; dangerous if he can bounce back on this drop back in trip.
runners.comment: Pulled up on handicap debut at Hereford but he has shown promise and it's still early days

runners.name: Fond Farewell
runners.spotlight: 1m3f Flat winner who is unexposed over hurdles; missed last year but he attracted support on his comeback at Huntingdon (2m, good; equipment added) 17 days ago and was travelling when he unseated two out; on same mark here and he's an interesting contender for in-form yard.
runners.comment: Unexposed hurdler who was going well when he unseated two out on his comeback; respected

runners.name: All One Word
runners.spotlight: Placed on Irish point debut in 2023; returned from mammoth absence with three runs over hurdles this year and he showed some promise when fifth of 12 at Ayr (2m, soft; 150-1) three weeks ago; this is more realistic on handicap debut and has possibilities if he can build on that last run.
runners.comment: Handicap newcomer and he's a player if he can build on his encouraging fifth at Ayr

runners.name: Legendary Day
runners.spotlight: Seven-time Flat winner who has also scored over hurdles (2m1f, soft) and fences (2m, good); on dangerous mark back over jumps but he's not seen much action in the last 12 months and has been tailed off in his last two Flat runs; risks attached.
runners.comment: On dangerous mark back over hurdles but he's been tailed off in his last two Flat runs

runners.name: Babychino
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in seller for Adrian Keatley in sole Flat start; pulled up in her first two hurdles runs late last year and she's finished a well-held seventh in last two starts; now goes handicapping off a lowly mark but she still needs plenty of progress.
runners.comment: Has struggled at big prices in all four hurdle runs; needs improvement on handicap debut

races.name: Halso High-Speed Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Inishcorker
runners.spotlight: Runner-up twice over 2m4f in the mud at Ffos Las, including latest start, and also second of four over 3m at Uttoxeter (good to soft) on penultimate outing; has the best form and a big chance if taking to the new cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Has the best form and a big chance if taking to the new cheekpieces

runners.name: Crackling Fire
runners.spotlight: Fourth at Chepstow (2m4f, good to soft) on debut in November came in a warm race but he wasn't nearly so good in his two starts since; had wind surgery early this month and steps up markedly in trip.
runners.comment: Easily best show was debut; had wind surgery early this month and upped markedly in trip

runners.name: Solid Performer
