runners.spotlight: Won last April in his sole British point (3m, good to firm); minor honours in three soft-ground maiden hurdles this term, going close at Catterick (about 3m1f) on last occasion, before he fell two out at Haydock (2m5f, good to soft) this month when 10l clear as 5-4 favourite; set the pace on last two outings; looks sure to go well again if none the worse.
runners.comment: Went close at 3m1f; fell when 10l clear last time; sure to go well again if none the worse

runners.name: Limerick Star
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in his Irish point and returned from a long absence with three minor honours over hurdles this year; steps up in trip but Inishcorker ran on much more strongly when this 6yo finished 6l behind him at Ffos Las (2m4f, heavy; with cheekpieces) last time; work to do.
runners.comment: Inishcorker ran on more strongly when this 6yo finished 6l behind him at Ffos Las (2m4f)

runners.name: Arcadian Star
runners.spotlight: Showed ability over an extended 2m4f in two Leicester novice hurdles, before pulled up over 3m on handicap debut at Wetherby one month ago; there's stamina in his pedigree and he had wind surgery soon after that latest start, but even his best form leaves him with a fair bit to find.
runners.comment: Wind surgery after latest start but even his best form leaves him with a fair bit to find

runners.name: Western George
runners.spotlight: Third at Carlisle (good) in his second bumper and about 9l fifth of 12 at Fontwell (2m3f, soft; 66-1) on hurdling debut in January also showed significant ability; much more is needed to upset the best of these, however.
runners.comment: Hurdle debut again showed significant ability; much more is needed to upset best of these

races.name: Doocey Group Mares' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Looking As You Are
runners.spotlight: Triple hurdle winner who scored off 3lb higher at Ludlow (2m5f, good to soft) last February; some creditable efforts since but she didn't fire in a mares' handicap at Market Rasen (tenth of 12) last month; needs to step up again after that and others are more persuasive.
runners.comment: Triple hurdle winner but she didn't fire at Market Rasen last time; others preferred

runners.name: Mermaids Cave
runners.spotlight: Seven-time hurdle winner who is versatile trip-wise; came up a bit short when favourite for a claimer at Catterick (1m7f, good) three weeks ago but she rallied well in that race and looks interesting off a dangerous mark back in a handicap.
runners.comment: Seven-time hurdle winner and she's on a dangerous mark back in a handicap; possibilities

runners.name: Crazierthandaisy
runners.spotlight: Won on seasonal debut at Warwick (2m5f, good to soft) in November but has mixed record in three subsequent runs and was tailed off at Ascot (2m7f, good to soft) last month; still has low mileage but she's been hard to predict and needs to bounce back again.
runners.comment: Low mileage 7yo but she's been hard to predict and was tailed off latest; risks attached

runners.name: Notnowlinda
runners.spotlight: In good form in spring of 2024; returned from long absence with two low-key runs in November but, after another break, she ran well to a point when fifth of 13 at Haydock (2m3f, good to soft) three weeks ago; continues to drop down the weights and she's not ruled out with cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: On reduced mark and she ran well to a point at Haydock three weeks ago; cheekpieces added

runners.name: No No Fizz
runners.spotlight: Got back in the groove after three months off when landing a gamble at Huntingdon (2m4f, good) 17 days ago; back up 4lb in the weights but she still looks feasibly treated and has form figures of 12431 under Tom Broughton; key player.
runners.comment: Record of 12431 under Tom Broughton and that last win was 17 days ago; key player

runners.name: Blue Marvel
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 5yo who was turned over as favourite in a mares' maiden at Huntingdon (2m5f, heavy) last month but she was only beaten around 2l in third of nine; should have more to offer now switched to handicapping and she needs a close look for in-form yard.
runners.comment: Unexposed 5yo who was close up in a Huntingdon maiden latest; respected on handicap debut

runners.name: Rialannah
runners.spotlight: 15-race maiden who has pulled up in three of her last five races but she went close at Doncaster (3m, good) on her penultimate run last season; returns for new yard after 12 months off but she's on a workable mark and needs watching in the betting.
runners.comment: Form figures of P2P2P in final four runs last season; market should guide on stable debut

races.name: Support The UK Sepsis Trust Novices' Limited Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: As Legends Have It
runners.spotlight: 0-5 over hurdles but he made it fourth time lucky over fences when scoring at Stratford (2m7f, soft) 19 days ago; was left clear at the second-last but he still hit a personal best with that success and should have more to offer in this sphere; respected up 3lb.
runners.comment: Made it fourth time lucky over fences when scoring at Stratford; respected up 3lb

runners.name: Daytime Dreaming
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 6yo who made it 2-3 over fences when completing a double in a Newbury handicap (2m, good to soft) last month; up another 5lb but he rallied well for that cosy success and is open to more progress on this step back up in trip; key player.
runners.comment: Made it 2-3 over fences with a cosy win at Newbury; big player again back up in trip

runners.name: Walk On High
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 9yo who completed a hurdle hat-trick when landing a Plumpton handicap (2m4f, good) in November; his winning streak came to an end at Taunton (last of five) in January but that was a Class 2 event and he travelled well to a point; returns after another break but he's out of a useful chaser and should have a future at this new discipline; needs a close look.
runners.comment: Low mileage 9yo who has won three times over hurdles; needs a close look on chase debut

runners.name: Caughtinyourtrance
runners.spotlight: Useful hurdler who made a solid start over fences when front-running second in a Newbury handicap (3m, good to soft) two weeks ago; should be unfazed by this drop back in trip and has possibilities if he can build on that last run.
runners.comment: Useful hurdler who was a front-running second on chase debut at Newbury (2m7f); in the mix

runners.name: Admiral Stewart
runners.spotlight: Promising as a novice hurdler last season but, following a walkover in November, he's been disappointing in last three runs over fences; had wind surgery last month but this is highly competitive and he needs a transformation after a short break; cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: After a walkover in November, he's been disappointing over fences; had wind op last month

runners.name: Bowenspark
runners.spotlight: Dual bumper and hurdle winner; mixed results in his first three runs over fences but he really got his act together when justifying favouritism at Bangor (2m5f, soft) last month; that was a four-runner race but he forged clear in the closing stages and a 6lb rise looks fair; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Came good over fences with a clearcut win at Bangor last month; respected up 6lb

runners.name: Shengai Enki
runners.spotlight: Ended last season with string of good efforts over hurdles for the Newland-Insole stable; sold for 18,000gns last spring; may have needed his reappearance run here in October but he now returns after another break and this is a tough assignment on his chase debut.
runners.comment: Triple hurdle winner but he returns after another break and this is tough on chase debut

races.name: Tom Rowley Simply The Best Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Largy Force
runners.spotlight: Progressive mare who has form figures of 2121 since joining Olly Murphy and she kept on well to beat a clear second at Wetherby (2m3f, soft) last month; this is a different test at this new trip but she has quite a bit of stamina in pedigree and still looks on a workable mark; respected.
runners.comment: Improver who has form figures of 2121 since joining Olly Murphy; respected upped to 3m

runners.name: The Long Point
runners.spotlight: 7yo who made it 2-4 over fences when completing a double at Wincanton (3m1f, good to soft; first run after wind surgery) last month; up 3lb for that narrow success but he beat a clear second and is open to more progress in this sphere; respected.
runners.comment: Made it 2-4 over fences when scoring after wind surgery at Wincanton; respected up 3lb

runners.name: Bank On Frank
runners.spotlight: 0-6 over hurdles but he made it second time lucky over fences when justifying favouritism after a break at Southwell (3m, soft) last month; up 10lb but that was a dominant display and he could have a lot to more to offer as a stayer in this sphere.
runners.comment: Won by 11l at Southwell (3m) and that was only his second run over fences; shortlisted

runners.name: Jo Lescribaa
runners.spotlight: Made all to win two chase (3m; good to soft and heavy) last winter but he's flopped in his last two starts and latest was at this track in November; still unexposed in this sphere but he needs to get back on track after another break.
runners.comment: Made all in two chases last winter but he's flopped in last two starts; bit to prove

runners.name: The Newest One
runners.spotlight: 11yo who was runner-up at Warwick (3m, good to soft) on New Year's Eve but he's hard to predict and was tailed off at Ludlow (visor added) last time; comes with risks attached and he needs to bounce back again.
runners.comment: Runner-up at Warwick in December but he's hard to predict and was beaten 46l last time

runners.name: Taita Hills
runners.spotlight: Point and hurdle winner who has shown promise at different trips (2m-2m7f) in his three chase runs since December; finished last of three at Leicester last time but he wasn't beaten far and he should have more to offer in this sphere; not ruled out with cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Unexposed over fences and he should have more to offer at staying trips; cheekpieces added

runners.name: Jasmin De Cotte
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish 7yo; well held in his first three runs for Dan Skelton and he was around 17l down when he was gifted a race here (2m4f, soft) two weeks ago; this is a deeper contest and he has work to do to back up in trip.
runners.comment: Fortunate win over 2m4f here last time and he has work to do back up in trip

runners.name: Inflexible
runners.spotlight: Won over fences at Wincanton (3m1f, good) in April 2024 but he's had only two runs since and was pulled up back from a long absence on recent stable debut at Haydock; overall record is now 1-14 and others are preferred.
runners.comment: Back from long absence with a pulled-up effort on stable debut at Haydock; passed over
runners.owner: H Stephen Smith & The Gabbertas Family

runners.name: Bali Body
runners.spotlight: Well handicapped on old form but he's not seen much action in recent years and has been pulled up or tailed off in his last three runs; this is only second start for current yard but he needs a major revival after another wind operation; cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Veteran who has lost his way and needs a major revival after another wind operation

runners.name: Masters Legacy
runners.spotlight: Well treated on old form but he's been lightly raced since his chase win at Exeter (3m, good) in November 2022; returned from 11 months off with fair fourth at Ascot in November but he was tailed off at Hereford the following month; cheekpieces are now added and he has something to prove after another break.
runners.comment: On dangerous mark but he's been lightly raced in recent years and was tailed off latest

races.name: Adam Johnson Money Maker Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Getaway Theatre
runners.spotlight: Won at Chepstow (2m7f, good to soft) last winter and she returned after nine months off with a near-miss at Warwick (3m1f, soft) in November; looks interesting on that form but she's not matched it in two runs since and needs to rediscover her spark.
runners.comment: Had a near-miss at Warwick (3m1f) in November but she's not matched that in two runs since

runners.name: Sunray Shadow
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 7yo who justified favouritism in Aintree maiden (2m4f, good) in October 2024; hasn't made a big impact in handicaps since but his latest fourth at Haydock was a fair effort after a short break; continues to slide down the weights and he's one to keep an eye on at this new trip.
runners.comment: Still has potential after only eight runs and he's one to keep an eye on at this new trip
runners.owner: S T Racing - The Founders Syndicate

runners.name: Goodwin
runners.spotlight: Front-runner whose record of 4-14 over hurdles includes two wins this term; ran right up to his best when second at Ascot (2m5f, soft) in December but he's lost his way with heavy defeats in last two run; now drops back into a Class 4 event but he needs to get back on track.
runners.comment: 4-14 over hurdles but he's lost his way in last two starts; needs to get back on track

runners.name: King Jon Oliver
runners.spotlight: Clear promise in both hurdle starts for Lorna Fowler in Ireland prior to Â£54,000 sale; won a Fakenham maiden (2m4f, good to soft) on stable debut in January but he flopped on his handicap debut at Hereford last month; still early days but that was a laboured effort last time and he needs to bounce back at this new trip.
runners.comment: Unexposed 6yo but he flopped on handicap debut at Hereford (2m3f); needs to bounce back

runners.name: Idefix De Ciergues
runners.spotlight: Completed a double over hurdles at Doncaster (3m, good to soft) in November 2024 but he's been up and down since and was pulled up over fences at Chepstow (11-4 favourite) last month; new headgear is now tried and he comes with risks attached back in this sphere.
runners.comment: Has a patchy profile and was pulled when favourite over fences last time; new headgear

runners.name: Libre De Choeur
runners.spotlight: Runner-up at Warwick (2m5f, good to soft) in December on second handicap start and bounced back from lesser run to win at Ffos Las (3m, heavy) last month; up 5lb but he's still unexposed and is open to more progress in staying handicaps; big player.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 6yo who won at Ffos Las last month and is open to more progress; key player

runners.name: Oh My Johnny
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdle winner who was placed in a big field at Ascot on his penultimate run and he backed that up with a front-running at Warwick (3m2f, good to soft) three weeks ago; remains on last winning mark and he should go well again back in trip.
runners.comment: On last winning mark and he's run well in his last two starts; respected back in trip
runners.owner: Richard Johnson Racing- Oh My Johnny

runners.name: Magical King
runners.spotlight: Runner-up off this mark at Newbury (3m, soft) last February but he's not hit the same level since and was beaten 19l back at Newbury (third of nine) last month; overall record is now 1-15 and others are more persuasive.
runners.comment: On workable mark but he's 1-12 over hurdles and was well held when third at Newbury latest

runners.name: Champagne Twist
runners.spotlight: Very promising novice during 2023-24 season but generally very disappointing over last two campaigns; has dropped a long way in the weights but he was pulled up three times during the winter before a remote fifth at Newbury (3m, good to soft) last time; down the list.
runners.comment: On much-reduced mark but last win was two years ago and he's struggled this winter

runners.name: Goshhowposh
runners.spotlight: Won a Pertemps qualifier at Wincanton (3m, good to soft) on Boxing Day 2023; well handicapped on that form but he's had only four runs since and was pulled up on his comeback at Newbury last month; has something to prove and yard also runs Oh My Johnny.
runners.comment: Well treated on old form but was pulled up on his Newbury comeback last month

runners.name: Individualiste
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner (hurdles/fences) but he's lost his way and was pulled up in four of his last five runs in Ireland; left Gavin Cromwell since his last run in January and has plenty to prove for new yard; equipment removed.
runners.comment: Pulled up in four of his last five runs in Ireland and has plenty to prove for new yard

races.name: QuinnBet Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 2 Elimination) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Cowboy Casanova
runners.spotlight: Promising third in his sole Irish point and he made a good start under rules when second of 15 behind an impressive winner at Newcastle (2m1f, good to soft) in January; sets the standard on that form and he's a big player back from another short break; hood added.
runners.comment: Second of 15 on bumper debut at Newcastle (2m1f) and he sets the standard on that form

runners.name: Seven Airs
runners.spotlight: Finished sixth of 10 on recent Kempton debut (2m, good; 10-1) but he looked inexperienced and that was an encouraging effort in a decent race; should know much more this time and he's in the mix.
runners.comment: Ran green at Kempton but he shaped with promise in that decent race; in the mix

runners.name: Baron Du Brizais
runners.spotlight: Bought for Â£90,000 after a comfortable win on his point debut (2m4f, good to soft) in November; half-brother to a bumper/useful hurdle winner and he's an interesting contender on his this switch to rules.
runners.comment: Bought for Â£90,000 after his point win in November; interesting contender on rules debut
runners.owner: Les De La Haye & Rodney Nelson-Jones

runners.name: Moore Brion
runners.spotlight: 80,000euros 3yo; heavily backed into 8-13 at Market Rasen (2m, good to soft) in November and he picked up late to score with something in hand; that was in a small field but he should improve for that experience and is respected under a penalty on his return.
runners.comment: Landed the odds on Market Rasen debut in November; respected under a penalty on his return

runners.name: The Last Promise
runners.spotlight: Eighth foal; half-sister to winners Everlastingpromise (useful 2m1f/2m2f chase), Wyenot (useful 2m-3m hurdle/chase), Nada To Prada (useful bumper and 2m/2m3f hurdle) and Heavenly Promise (2m4f-3m chase); dam 2m hurdle winner; plenty to like on paper and yard is 2-10 in bumpers this season.
runners.comment: Plenty to like on paper and yard is 2-10 in bumpers this season
runners.owner: Mrs P And Miss S Bailey

runners.name: Solar Verse
runners.spotlight: 11,000euros foal, 42,000euros 3yo; first foal; dam well held bumper/maiden hurdles, half-sister to winners Likearollingstone (2m4f hurdle), Arthur's Quay (1m7f-2m5f jump) and The Big Apple (bumper); this looks a tough starting point and he could be one for later on.
runners.comment: This looks a tough starting point and he could be one for later on

runners.name: Twiddle Dee
runners.spotlight: Well held on Hereford debut last month and was tailed off at another big price over C&D (soft) two weeks ago; cheekpieces are now added and she needs major improvement.
runners.comment: Struggled at big prices in both runs this year including over C&D two weeks ago

races.name: Richards 60th Birthday Celebration Mares' Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Watamu
runners.spotlight: Debut bumper winner for Pat Murphy in October 2024; has made up into a fairly useful hurdler for current yard this term, landing 2m novice at Huntingdon in December; failed to settle upped to 2m4f when pulled up in EBF Mares' Handicap at Newbury a week ago and looks hard to beat back in these much calmer waters.
runners.comment: Fairly useful but failed to settle at 2m4f when pulled up at Newbury latest; hard to beat

runners.name: Alibey
runners.spotlight: A fair Flat maiden for William Haggas but she's proved too keen on both her runs in this sphere for her new stable, hooded when fifth in 2m Huntingdon novice 17 days ago; could still do better if becoming more tractable.
runners.comment: Fair Flat maiden; too keen sent hurdling, fifth at Huntingdon latest; may still do better

runners.name: Burning Embers
runners.spotlight: Getaway mare who shaped well when a debut bumper sixth but she came in a remote fourth on her hurdling debut in 2m Kempton novice two weeks ago; worth another chance for top yard.
runners.comment: Remote fourth on hurdling debut at Kempton two weeks ago; worth another chance
runners.owner: S T Racing Burnt Ends Partnership

runners.name: Rose Light
runners.spotlight: A fair 11f Flat winner but she was hooded when a well-held fifth on her yard/hurdling debut in 2m Doncaster maiden 15 days ago; in good hands though so not written off.
runners.comment: Fair Flat winner; remote fifth on yard/hurdling debut at Doncaster; not written off

runners.name: Nowherenearmilan
runners.spotlight: Has offered little in a pair of bumpers at Newcastle and Hereford this year; cheekpieces go on for her hurdling debut.
runners.comment: Has offered little in pair of bumpers at Newcastle and Hereford; cheekpieces go on

runners.name: Blue Siam
runners.spotlight: Modest 1m6f Flat maiden for Hugo Palmer who wasn't disgraced when sixth in 1m6f Southwell handicap for her new handler 15 days ago; no forlorn hope now starting out over hurdles.
runners.comment: Modest Flat maiden; no forlorn hope now starting out over hurdles for her new stable

runners.name: Simply A Dancer
runners.spotlight: Cut little ice in a pair of bumpers before being pulled up on her hurdling debut in Huntingdon novice (2m, good to soft) 27 days ago; hard to warm to.
runners.comment: Pulled up on her hurdling debut in 2m Huntingdon novice 27 days ago; hard to warm to

runners.name: Siorai
runners.spotlight: Masar filly who showed fair form in three Flat runs for Jim Bolger last summer; tongue tied and went too freely on her yard/hurdling debut when a well-held fifth of six in 2m4f Musselburgh novice last month; hood is added now and she remains with potential.
runners.comment: Fair ex-Irish maiden; too free on yard/hurdling debut at Musselburgh; can do better

runners.name: Footage Detectives
runners.spotlight: 18-1 and green when last of eight in Worcester bumper on her debut in October; lots more is needed now going hurdling.
runners.comment: Green when last in Worcester bumper on her debut; lots more needed going hurdling

runners.name: Tangled Webb
runners.spotlight: 150-1, pulled up on her hurdling debut in Warwick juvenile (2m, good to soft) in November; needs to take a major step forward.
runners.comment: Pulled up on hurdling debut in Warwick juvenile; needs to take a major step forward

runners.name: Minnie Belle
runners.spotlight: 10-1, well-beaten fifth of six on her first go over hurdles in Ludlow juvenile (2m, good to firm) in October; significantly more is required.
runners.comment: Well-beaten fifth on hurdles debut at Ludlow in October; significantly more is required

races.name: Luke And Michaela's 1st Wedding Anniversary Selling Hurdle

runners.name: Junker dAllier
runners.spotlight: A three-time hurdles winner (around 2m) last season; failed to go on in three starts over fences this term and he was pulled up back in this sphere at Warwick (2m5f) last month; player in this grade if first-time cheekpieces spark a revival.
runners.comment: Failed to go on over fences and pulled up back hurdling at Warwick latest; cheekpieces on

runners.name: Demoiselle Kap
runners.spotlight: Successful four times (around 2m) over hurdles and fences last season but she's proved a big disappointment in two starts since her reappearance fifth at Ludlow, coming in last over fences at Leicester 22 days ago.
runners.comment: Big disappointment last two starts, last over fences at Leicester 22 days ago

runners.name: Hecouldbetheone
runners.spotlight: A fairly useful winning hurdler/chaser at his best but his form has nosedived and he jumped poorly when last of six in 2m4f handicap chase at Leicester in December; back from a break now and needs it to spark a resurgence.
runners.comment: A fairly useful winning hurdler/chaser at his best but his form has nosedived

runners.name: Hara Kiri
runners.spotlight: Scored three times (around 2m) over hurdles in 2024; very lightly raced since and he failed to build on an encouraging reappearance Wetherby third when sixth of eight in 2m Uttoxeter handicap in December; reportedly finished lame there so worth another chance in this grade.
runners.comment: Encouraging Wetherby third and reportedly lame at Uttoxeter since; worth another chance

runners.name: Disco Davis
runners.spotlight: Started the season with a good 2m4f Uttoxeter fourth but he's been pulled up all three starts since in handicaps from 2m4f to 3m; in top hands but is hard to warm to despite the drop in grade.
runners.comment: Pulled up on is last three starts; hard to warm to despite the drop in grade here

runners.name: Embittered
runners.spotlight: Scored over hurdles and fences for Eric McNamara in Ireland last summer; well beaten on both runs for his current yard recently, though he faced a very stiff task in the Plate at Cheltenham last time; back hurdling now with something to prove.
runners.comment: Well beaten on both chasing runs for current yard; back hurdling with something to prove

races.name: Happy 70th Birthday Steve Parsons Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Tapley
runners.spotlight: Consistent sort who posted a solid third of six in 2m2f handicap at Kempton two weeks ago; eased 2lb here, so needs considering in his bid for a second success of the season.
runners.comment: Solid third at Kempton latest; considered in his bid for a second success of the season

runners.name: Classic King
runners.spotlight: Scored over fences at Doncaster (2m3f) last term; in good nick without winning over hurdles this season until 17th in Pertemps Hurdle at Cheltenham 16 days ago; back chasing here with cheekpieces fitted and no forlorn hope.
runners.comment: Largely running well over hurdles this term; not ruled out back chasing; cheekpieces go on

runners.name: Lunar Discovery
runners.spotlight: Running well over fences for her current yard this season and opened chase account in 2m3f Chepstow handicap in December (race has thrown up three subsequent winners); big shout given she has gone well after a break too.
runners.comment: Opened chase account at Chepstow in December; big shout given she has gone well fresh

runners.name: Kilbarry Hill
runners.spotlight: Still to get his head in front in this sphere but he comes here on the back of a fair fifth of eight in 3m1f handicap at Wincanton last time; this three-time hurdles scorer is in the mix with cheekpieces fitted for the first time.
runners.comment: Fair fifth at Wincanton last time; in the mix with cheekpieces fitted for the first time

runners.name: Happy And Fine
runners.spotlight: Scored at Warwick (3m) in December but he isn't the easiest to catch right and was pulled up in 2m7f handicap at Newbury last time; others are preferred.
runners.comment: Not the easiest to catch right and he was pulled up in 2m7f handicap at Newbury last time
runners.owner: L Cross S Cross & J Fitzpatrick

races.name: Madeleine Wann - Weight Loss Coach Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Pottersjetamay
runners.spotlight: 0-18 but he posted a solid third of nine back hurdling in 2m Huntingdon handicap 17 days ago; considered off a 2lb lower mark.
runners.comment: 0-18 but solid third back hurdling in 2m Huntingdon handicap 17 days ago; considered

runners.name: No Mean Feat
runners.spotlight: Remains winless and he's offered little after a 15-month absence in handicaps (up to 2m4f) at Fontwell and Newbury this winter; cheekpieces are reached for now.
runners.comment: Remains winless and he's offered little after a long absence this winter; cheekpieces on

runners.name: Genbu
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark at Wetherby (2m) in October but he failed to build on it, coming in last on the Flat at Wolverhampton last month on his final run for Sam England; sold for 1,400gns since; lots more is required.
runners.comment: Won at Wetherby but failed to build on it for Sam England; more needed for new yard

runners.name: Klapton Boy
runners.spotlight: Placed in a pair of 2m4f Wetherby novice hurdles in 2024-25; very lightly raced since and has failed to go on, undergoing breathing surgery before coming in a well-held fifth of seven in 2m4f Sedgefield handicap 18 days ago; not easy to make a case for.
runners.comment: Had wind op before well-held fifth at Sedgefield 18 days ago; not easy to make a case for

runners.name: Lord Of The Glance
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 7yo who has advanced his form since sent handicapping, runner-up (over 2m) at Fakenham and Plumpton this year; can go well again nudged up 1lb.
runners.comment: In good form, second at Plumpton latest; lightly raced and a player nudged up 1lb here

runners.name: Beorma
runners.spotlight: Placed in a bumper in September 2024 for Thomas Gallagher but she's failed to offer much in four runs for current yard, off 15 months before being pulled up on her first go in handicap company at Southwell (2m) latest.
runners.comment: Off 15 months before pulled up on her first go in a handicap at Southwell 26 days ago

runners.name: River Glance
runners.spotlight: Yet to offer much in bumpers and a trio of runs over hurdles, only tenth in 2m Ludlow novice last month; needs this switch to handicaps to spark major improvement.
runners.comment: Yet to offer much in three hurdles runs; needs switch to h'caps to spark improvement

runners.name: Global Eclipse
runners.spotlight: Went the wrong way in a trio of 2m juvenile hurdles for Dan Skelton in 2024; off 15 months before being pulled up for his new yard in 2m4f Huntingdon handicap 17 days ago; hard to warm to.
runners.comment: Off 15 months before pulled up for new yard at Huntingdon 17 days ago; hard to warm to

runners.name: Peace In The Park
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 6yo who posted easily his best run on his handicap debut when second of nine at Huntingdon (2m) last month; he needs to back it up now off a 2lb higher mark.
runners.comment: Easily best run when second at Huntingdon latest; needs to back it up now off 2lb higher

runners.name: Night Burlesque
runners.spotlight: Showed some fair form in bumpers but she's yet to reproduce it in this sphere, hooded when beating only one on her handicap debut at Wetherby (2m) last month; others are preferred.
runners.comment: Showed some fair form in bumpers but she's yet to reproduce it in this sphere

runners.name: Ritaan
runners.spotlight: A fair Flat maiden for Harry Eustace but she's failed to kick on in this sphere, pulled up on her handicap debut at Huntingdon (2m4f) last month; she may still do better, though, if becoming more tractable.
runners.comment: A fair Flat maiden but she's failed to kick on in this sphere; may still do better though

runners.name: Daring To Dream
runners.spotlight: Fair bumper performer who has hinted at promise in a trio of runs over hurdles, fading after a late blunder when seventh of ten in 2m Chepstow novice in January; she's not without interest now handicapping after a break.
runners.comment: Faded after blunder when Chepstow seventh in January; not without interest on h'cap debut

races.name: Steve & Madeleine Wann Back At Stratford Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Churchman
runners.spotlight: 0-18 over hurdles but placed on two of his last three starts, runner-up at Hereford (2m4f; in second-time cheekpieces) on latest; his one attempt at this sort of trip (last March) was a heavy defeat, though.
runners.comment: 0-18 over hurdles; placed on two of last three starts, runner-up at Hereford (2m4f) latest

runners.name: Pooroldmackley
runners.spotlight: Won off 4lb lower at Worcester (2m7f, good) last August under usual rider Eleanor Williams but out of form since; needs to be back to his peak.
runners.comment: Won off 4lb lower at Worcester (2m7f, good) last August but out of form since

runners.name: Harbour Light
runners.spotlight: Better when upped in trip for his two handicaps, winning by 7l over C&D (soft) last time; up 8lb but plenty to like after that smooth win.
runners.comment: Better upped in trip for both handicaps, winning by 7l over C&D (soft) last time; up 8lb

runners.name: Annies Gold
runners.spotlight: Well beaten all nine starts and she's particularly hard to fancy on the strength of her three handicaps (2m4f/2m7f).
runners.comment: Well beaten all nine starts; particularly hard to fancy on the strength of three handicaps

runners.name: Farhh Echo
runners.spotlight: Fourth in a maiden at Worcester last July but he's struggled in three handicaps since; Flat bred and this is a radically new trip, after all previous starts over about 2m, but he rallied well in that Worcester race; could be interesting if strong in the market.
runners.comment: Struggled in 3 handicaps; rallied well for best effort and this is a radically new trip

runners.name: Shes All Pride
runners.spotlight: Second in an Irish point in November 2024; has not shown much in a bumper or three hurdle races but going handicapping (albeit from 3lb wrong) offers a much more realistic chance in theory and she's bumped upped substantially in trip, which looks a good move on pedigree.
runners.comment: Second in an Irish point; has not shown much under rules but makes h'cap debut at new trip

runners.name: Easter Icon
runners.spotlight: Productive spell from August 2024 to April 2025 over hurdles and on the Flat; Fontwell win over nearly 2m6f started that sequence but this season's three runs over hurdles were heavy defeats.
runners.comment: Enjoyed a productive spell before this season's three runs over hurdles were heavy defeats

runners.name: Ballerina Boxer
runners.spotlight: Beat a big field at Punchestown (3m, good to yielding) last June but mostly well held afterwards and was sold for Â£1,200 in November; soft ground may well have been against her when pulled up at 125-1 on stable debut in January, but others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Ex-Irish; soft ground may well have been against her when 125-1 on stable debut in January
runners.owner: Mike Harris Racing & Nikki Evans Racing

runners.name: Harksman
runners.spotlight: No impact in any of his seven races, the last two of which were handicaps at Kempton (3m, good; hooded) and Hereford (3m2f, soft); hooded on penultimate start and now tries cheekpieces; fair bit to prove even though the move back down in trip may help.
runners.comment: No impact in his seven races, the last two of which were handicaps; first cheekpieces

races.name: Molson Coors Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Regal Renaissance
runners.spotlight: On a losing sequence and she came in a below-par fourth of eight in 2m4f Ludlow handicap chase last month; needs to take a step forward.
runners.comment: On a losing sequence and a below-par fourth of eight in Ludlow handicap chase last month

runners.name: Two To Tango
runners.spotlight: Consistent over hurdles/fences but she's still to register a victory, runner-up for the third time this season in 2m Leicester handicap chase in December; can't be discounted after a break with cheekpieces added, though.
runners.comment: Still to win but runner-up at Leicester in December; not discounted after a break

runners.name: Gotthereintheend
runners.spotlight: Made a winning start over fences, following wind surgery, in 2m5f Fakenham handicap 15 days ago; up 5lb but she showed a good attitude there and jumped well so enters calculations.
runners.comment: Had wind op before winning start over fences at Fakenham; up 5lb but enters calculations

runners.name: Duhallow Tommy
runners.spotlight: Ended a losing run at Southwell (2m4f) in February and not discredited given he was harried for the lead when third of five in 2m4f Wincanton handicap 23 days ago; well in the mix eased 1lb.
runners.comment: Solid third of five in 2m4f Wincanton handicap 23 days ago; well in the mix eased 1lb

runners.name: Ballyhiho
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark over hurdles at Worcester (2m4f) in August and not discredited after three months off when a fading fourth of nine in 2m6f handicap here 19 days ago; much respected on her chase debut.
runners.comment: Not discredited when fourth over hurdles here latest; much respected on chase debut

runners.name: Politacus
runners.spotlight: Yet to add to her two hurdling successes (at about 2m4f) in this sphere but she comes here after a very good second of six in 2m Warwick handicap 20 days ago; that form is working out well so she's a big player eased 1lb for yard back among the winners.
runners.comment: Very good second at Warwick 20 days ago; that form is working out well so she's a player

races.name: OOH Cooling Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Soldiers Leap
runners.spotlight: Scored twice in the autumn, including over C&D, and comes here after a respectable fifth of 14 in 2m1f Carisle handicap last month; in the mix off a 3lb lower mark.
runners.comment: C&D winner; respectable fifth at Carisle last month; in the mix off a 3lb lower mark

runners.name: Get The Value
runners.spotlight: Course winner but he arrives well out of sorts, pulled up in 2m Warwick handicap 36 days ago; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Course winner but arrives well out of sorts, pulled up in 2m Warwick handicap 36 days ago

runners.name: Juan Bermudez
runners.spotlight: Bagged a third C&D win last June and not discredited when last of six in 2m6f handicap here in October, scuppered by a blunder two out; off since but needs considering with Harry Cobden up, especially if the market vibes are positive.
runners.comment: Three-time C&D winner; off since October but needs considering with Harry Cobden up

runners.name: Morning Mayhem
runners.spotlight: In fine form for his current yard and he got back on the up with 2m Fakenham handicap success 15 days ago; up 3lb but strong at the finish there so he holds excellent claims back over a longer trip.
runners.comment: Back on the up with 2m Fakenham win (strong at the finish); big player back up in trip

runners.name: Caliach Point
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 6yo who has failed to build on a promising Doncaster second in November, making some mistakes when fourth of 13 on his handicap debut at Newbury (2m4f) 29 days ago; cheekpieces are reached for now.
runners.comment: Has failed to build on a promising Doncaster second in November; cheekpieces go on now

runners.name: Whatsgoingonmarvin
runners.spotlight: A useful 1m5f Flat winner for Sir Mark Prescott; changed hands for 115,000gns and shaped well when runner-up in 2m Catterick juvenile hurdle in January; faced a stiff task in Haydock's Victor Ludorum last time and remains with potential now handicapping.
runners.comment: Useful Flat winner; Catterick second before stiff task at Haydock; remains with potential

runners.name: Oakley
runners.spotlight: Ended a losing sequence at Hereford (2m4f) in November but he's been below par both runs since, a well-held fourth of seven back at Hereford (2m4f) last month; he needs to get back on track.
runners.comment: Well-held fourth of seven at Hereford (2m4f) last month; he needs to get back on track

runners.name: Moon Over Clyro
runners.spotlight: Placed in a pair of 2m Worcester maiden hurdles but he's been off the track since coming in 13th of 16 on his handicap debut at Chepstow (2m3f) in October; yard is going well so this very lightly raced 6yo may still do better.
runners.comment: Off since 13th on Chepstow h'cap debut in October; lightly raced so may still do better

runners.name: Telhimlisten
runners.spotlight: Made light of an absence when scoring at Sedgefield (2m1f) in January 2025 and backed it up with a very good second of five at Plumpton (2m4f) the following month; returns from 13 months off here but can't be ruled out with yard going well.
runners.comment: Very good second at Plumpton 13 months ago; has won off an absence so not ruled out

races.name: Join The Midnite Movement Handicap

runners.name: Ancient State
runners.spotlight: Races freely and unbeaten (2-2) at 5f; quickened readily when winning at Lingfield last August on final start for Martin Dunne; new trainer has made a promising start and this gelding may still be unexposed; very interesting despite a 7lb rise.
runners.comment: 2-2 at 5f; ready winner when last seen in August; new trainer has made promising start

runners.name: Irish Dancer
runners.spotlight: Goes well here and was short-headed over C&D 15 days ago on his first outing for over five months; may well come on for the run and can race off the same mark; leading contender.
runners.comment: Short-headed over C&D 15 days ago; may well come on for the run; leading contender

runners.name: Colors Of Freedom
runners.spotlight: Was banging at the door all winter and again made the frame when a close fourth at Wolverhampton on Tuesday; unlikely to be far away but her losing sequence is mounting; new headgear today.
runners.comment: Fourth at Wolverhampton on Tuesday; unlikely to be far away but losing run is mounting

runners.name: Westgate Warrior
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo, last seen running well when second over this trip at Wolverhampton on Boxing Day; may not have reached his ceiling just yet and he has possibilities off the same mark if fit after three months off.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 4yo; good second at Wolverhampton on Boxing Day; possibilities off same mark

runners.name: Street Life
runners.spotlight: Five-time course winner but finished behind Irish Dancer and Woodhay Whisper here last time, with no obvious excuses.
runners.comment: Finished behind Irish Dancer and Woodhay Whisper here last time with no obvious excuses

runners.name: Herakles
runners.spotlight: Does most of his AW racing at Newcastle but showed he's just as effective here when a close fourth of 12 over C&D 18 days ago; that was his first outing for four months and he could now be ready to strike.
runners.comment: Close fourth over C&D last time after a break; could now be ready to strike

runners.name: Papa Dont Preach
runners.spotlight: Led close home to score at Wolverhampton just before Christmas and ran creditably over C&D in February; raced wide from an outside draw back at Wolverhampton last time; that run is best ignored and he can bounce back.
runners.comment: Had an excuse last time and has claims on C&D third the time before; commands respect

runners.name: Woodhay Whisper
runners.spotlight: Closely matched with Irish Dancer and Street Life on C&D form from two weeks ago when she was short of room late on; all her wins have come at 6f but she's effective at 5f and she should be in the shake-up again.
runners.comment: Fourth here two weeks ago when short of room late on; should be in the shake-up again

runners.name: Popular Dream
runners.spotlight: Four-time C&D winner, the latest in January off a 2lb higher mark; however, he seems to be going through the motions at present, never dangerous when sixth last time; others preferred.
runners.comment: Four-time C&D winner but seems to be going through the motions at present

runners.name: Basholo
runners.spotlight: Dual Wolverhampton winner in the autumn but not at her best on her last four starts; slipping in the weights but others still make more appeal.
runners.comment: Not at best last four starts; slipping in the weights but others still make more appeal

runners.name: South Shore
runners.spotlight: In reasonable form last autumn, not beaten far when fifth of 14 at Newcastle (5f) on latest start; again of interest if ready to do himself justice after five months off.
runners.comment: Creditable fifth at Newcastle last autumn; again of interest if ready to roll after break

races.name: Daily Profit Boosts At betmgm.co.uk Handicap

runners.name: Bad Habits
runners.spotlight: Just one win and often pulls hard; a few near-misses at Wolverhampton of late but this more demanding 7f is a cause for concern unless he's more amenable.
runners.comment: Just one win and often pulls hard; this more demanding 7f is a cause for concern

runners.name: Amelias Joy
runners.spotlight: Got it together lately, having looked regressive, and is only 2lb higher than when off the mark at Wolverhampton recently (first-time cheekpieces added to the usual hood); tonight's more demanding 7f should work in her favour; player.
runners.comment: Has got it together now; tonight's more demanding 7f should work in her favour; player

runners.name: Bullington Bry
runners.spotlight: Longstanding maiden who's finished close up in a couple of 6f handicaps here in the past fortnight; extra furlong fine if he settles and he's proving consistent for this yard.
runners.comment: Longstanding maiden; extra furlong fine if he settles; proving consistent for this yard

runners.name: No Nay Nevermind
runners.spotlight: Regressive maiden who usually pulls hard; the early impact of the visor seems to have faded and Jkr Cobbler looks her trainer's best chance.
runners.comment: Regressive maiden who usually pulls hard; early impact of the visor seems to have faded
runners.owner: S Jones L Donaldson & K Elliott

runners.name: Create
runners.spotlight: Blows hot and cold, and has been on the chilly side in her two runs back from a break; it's no certainty that the drop back from 1m will help and she holds only outside claims.
runners.comment: Blows hot and cold, and has been on the chilly side in two runs back; only outside claims
runners.owner: Dixon Wylam M Baldry J&s Harrod

runners.name: Call Glory
runners.spotlight: Two wins here in January for Ryan Kavanagh, who's back up tonight after a rare poor effort from this 5yo as favourite last time; not much wiggle room off this mark but should fare better.
runners.comment: Two wins here in January for Ryan Kavanagh; not much wiggle room off this mark

runners.name: Gladiadora
runners.spotlight: Drops to shy of 1m for the first time since handicapping on her return from three months off; yard an excellent 6-15 this year and she's one of the likelier winners.
runners.comment: Drops to shy of 1m for the first time since handicapping; yard an excellent 6-15 this year
runners.owner: Mrs R Coleman And G Glendining

runners.name: Rusheen Boy
runners.spotlight: Landed a third win here when going in over C&D two months ago; that came after a similar absence and he's only 1lb higher, so has to go on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Landed a third win here when going in over C&D two months ago; on the shortlist

runners.name: Reenies Dream
runners.spotlight: Regressive maiden who's often pulled hard and needs to settle early from the outside stall; virtually refused to race last time anyway, so there are plenty of risks involved.
runners.comment: Can pull hard; virtually refused to race last time, so there are plenty of risks involved

runners.name: South Kensington
runners.spotlight: 2-3 for Martin Dunne last autumn for the tongue-tie first going on; easy to back ahead of her Wolverhampton comeback for this yard a fortnight ago, when pulling hard in a small field, and should do better if more amenable.
runners.comment: Pulled hard in a small field on comeback and should do better if more amenable

runners.name: Court Of Session
runners.spotlight: Goes well at Chelmsford but is fine on Tapeta too, including when second to Call Glory over 6f here early in the year; more consistent than most at this level and is adaptable regarding tactics.
runners.comment: More consistent than most at this level and adaptable regarding tactics; each-way squeak

runners.name: Siviez
runners.spotlight: Huge prices all runs, including on her recent seasonal/handicap debut; still early days but seeing some support for her would be preferable before she is added to calculations.
runners.comment: Early days but seeing some support for her would be preferable before she's considered

runners.name: Kamaway
runners.spotlight: Best effort when fourth in a C&D novice four weeks ago; this half-sister to a dirt winner starts handicap life at a lowly level and is lightly raced, so one to consider.
runners.comment: Starts handicap life at a lowly level and is lightly raced, so one to consider

races.name: Bet Â£10 Get Â£40 With BetMGM Handicap

runners.name: Tamashal
runners.spotlight: All starts have been at Chelmsford, building on an eyecatching debut in two runs since, both from the front over 1m; it's hard to know the precise merit of his win last time and this mark demands more now he's handicapping but he is open to further improvement.
runners.comment: This mark demands more now he's handicapping but he is open to further improvement

runners.name: Illys Roo
runners.spotlight: All or nothing for Jack Channon, from whom he was picked up for 7,000gns; sprung a surprise off 4lb lower over C&D recently on debut for new yard, beating Dagger Strike by a head; now has to prove he can string together two performances.
runners.comment: All or nothing for Jack Channon but won debut for this yard; needs to back that up

runners.name: Delinquent
runners.spotlight: Debut odds-on second last August came in a weak, small-field affair and he ran well below market expectations in two more starts; gelded since and it will be interesting to see how he goes in the betting now handicapping.
runners.comment: Gelded and it will be interesting to see how he goes in the betting now handicapping

runners.name: Dagger Strike
runners.spotlight: Nursery win in December (first run since gelded) came off a 12lb lower mark and he's been expensive to follow since; nudged up a further 3lb since his C&D second (first-time cheekpieces; retained) last time to Illy's Roo and needs to find a bit more.
runners.comment: Nudged up 3lb since his C&D second last time to Illy's Roo; needs to find a bit more

runners.name: Watchdog
runners.spotlight: Newcastle win last December came off 9lb lower and his revised mark looked to find him out when last seen two months ago; won't mind going back up from 6f though.
runners.comment: Revised mark looked to find him out when last seen but he won't mind going back up from 6f
runners.owner: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd

runners.name: Kanishka
runners.spotlight: Easy to back on his recent C&D comeback, his first start in five months having been gelded, and cracked late on off tonight's mark; entitled to have come on for that but is exposed after a busy first season.
runners.comment: Entitled to come on for his recent C&D comeback but is exposed after a busy first season
runners.owner: C Booth P Boyle S Coleby & S Smith

runners.name: The Resdev Scholar
runners.spotlight: Finished last season on a high, winning under Joanna Mason on turf in his last three starts (a seller and two heavy-ground nurseries); another 4lb higher, returning from a break for a yard that's been among the winners of late; player.
runners.comment: Finished last season on a high; player for a yard that's been among the winners of late
runners.owner: N Bradley 29 Resdev & South Bank Racing

runners.name: Acclaimed Freedom
runners.spotlight: Underwent wind surgery not long after winning a Chelmsford novice last September; last of ten off 1lb higher than this on his Polytrack comeback ten days ago and has something to prove in first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Last of ten on his comeback ten days ago; something to prove in first-time cheekpieces
runners.owner: J Sarkar D Sarkar O Sarkar & D Sarkar

runners.name: Mayaada
runners.spotlight: Didn't achieve much when winning a small-field Epsom maiden last August and took backward steps in three subsequent starts; needs to have improved for the winter break.
runners.comment: Backward steps in three runs after Epsom win; needs to have improved for the winter break

races.name: Read Meg Nicholls' Blog At betmgm.co.uk Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Aqpan
runners.spotlight: 400,000gns yearling who shaped with a lot of promise when a clear second behind a useful-looking winner last December; pulled away with another interesting Godolphin runner next time (both Kempton 7f) and sets a good standard on comeback.
runners.comment: Twice finished a clear second to useful-looking winners last winter; sets a good standard

runners.name: Aura Champagne
runners.spotlight: 110,000gns yearling who has plenty of speed in her pedigree; supported at prices on her Newbury debut last autumn (7f, good to soft) when coming away with the market principals; looks to have Aqpan to worry about on comeback.
runners.comment: Came away with the market principals on debut six months ago; has Aqpan to worry about

runners.name: Enduring Story
runners.spotlight: First foal of a once-raced, well-related mare; likely to need more of a stamina test and this looks a tough debut for a yard not noted for newcomers.
runners.comment: Likely to need more of a stamina test; this looks a tough debut

runners.name: Ay Up Duck
runners.spotlight: Dam won over 1m but has produced a 2m winner; every chance this gelded newcomer will want more of a test in due course and it would be a shock to see him take this on debut.
runners.comment: Dam won over 1m but has produced a 2m winner; likely to need more of a stamina test

runners.name: Street Dancer
runners.spotlight: Related to a few useful winners but has offered very little in two runs to date, both in the hood and finishing a long way back; low-grade handicaps are probably next on the agenda.
runners.comment: Offered very little in two runs to date; low-grade handicaps are probably next up

runners.name: Battle Standard
runners.spotlight: 80-1 for his 1m debut here a fortnight ago and finished last of the ten runners.
runners.comment: 80-1 for his 1m debut here a fortnight ago and finished last of the ten runners
runners.owner: J Sarkar D Sarkar O Sarkar & D Sarkar

runners.name: Clear Above
runners.spotlight: Bettered an ordinary turf debut when fourth in an unexciting Newcastle maiden (7f) when last seen nine months ago; gelded since and it's likely that handicaps will be his time to shine.
runners.comment: Off for nine months; has been gelded and it's likely handicaps will be his time to shine

races.name: Midnite A Next Generation Betting App Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Golden Muse
runners.spotlight: Promising third on debut at Chelmsford (1m, Polytrack) last month and built on that with convincing win at Wolverhampton (9.4f, Tapeta) a fortnight ago; there's every chance this Golden Horn 3yo will stay today's longer trip and she's a leading contender for her in-form yard under a 5lb penalty.
runners.comment: Made all in convincing fashion at Wolverhampton; leading claims under a 5lb penalty

runners.name: Nuptown Girl
runners.spotlight: Runner-up over 1m/1m2f on two of her last four starts (including here) but she made it 0-11 when soundly beaten in 1m3f handicap here last time and this exposed 4yo may be vulnerable once more.
runners.comment: Twice runner-up this year but exposed and now 0-11; may be vulnerable once more

runners.name: La Tosca
runners.spotlight: Third foal; closely related to 1m7f hurdle winner Diamond Jim, half-sister to 1m6f turf/2m AW winner Senor Cortez (RPR 81); dam French 1m2f-1m6f winner (including Listed) from classy family; she might not come into her own until tackling further but it will be no surprise to see a good run on debut.
runners.comment: Potential in pedigree; might need further but no surprise to see a good run on debut
runners.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum

runners.name: The Lakes
runners.spotlight: Her dam was useful on the Flat but this 4yo was sent off at big prices and struggled to get competitive in her three bumpers; others preferred.
runners.comment: Struggled to get competitive in her three bumpers and others are preferred

runners.name: Itsonlyrockandroll
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten at Wolverhampton (8.6f/9.4f, Tapeta) this year on her first two starts; improvement is necessary but she's in top hands and her dam was a two-time Group 2 winner over about 1m4f; perhaps today's step up in trip will help things click.
runners.comment: Improvement needed but in top hands and perhaps this longer trip will help things click

runners.name: Littlecote
runners.spotlight: Slowly away when fifth of seven at 33-1 at Nottingham (1m, good to soft) last October on her sole start; however, her dam was runner-up in the Irish Oaks and this Camelot 3yo could relish the step up in trip; interesting.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten on sole start but pedigree suggests she could relish this step up in trip

races.name: Make The Move To Midnite Handicap (Div I)

runners.name: Shielas Well
runners.spotlight: Ran encouragingly over C&D on final start during brief spell with Thomas Faulkner and found 7f too sharp on debut for Ian Williams; very well handicapped on best form and looks interesting back up in trip.
runners.comment: Interesting off an attractive mark back at a suitable trip on second run for new yard

runners.name: Moon Over The Sea
runners.spotlight: Seven AW wins, the most recent off 1lb higher over C&D in January; far from consistent since; chance of turning up in top form depends on how well he responds to first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Inconsistent since last win; chance depends on how well he responds to cheekpieces

runners.name: Regal Glory
runners.spotlight: Record of 0-6 for current yard and it's a long time since her sole success; however, third-place finish at Southwell (1m4f) last time makes her one of the stronger contenders in this field, receiving weight all round.
runners.comment: Third-place finish at Southwell last time makes her one of the stronger contenders

runners.name: Abu Royal
runners.spotlight: Campaigned at Southwell this year, hinting at a revival in two starts prior to a heavy defeat; remains a long time since sole win; chance depends on how well he reacts to first-time visor.
runners.comment: Long time since sole success; chance depends on how well he reacts to visor

runners.name: Arranmore
runners.spotlight: Bagged a classified contest last August, ending a long losing spell, and has proved consistent at the same level this year; however, this 9yo hasn't won a handicap since 2021.
runners.comment: Consistent classified efforts this year; hasn't won a handicap since 2021

runners.name: Dodging The Bullet
runners.spotlight: Maiden who has raced mainly over 1m; ran respectably from the front at Kempton most recently; doesn't appear to be crying out for this return to a middle distance.
runners.comment: Maiden who has raced mainly over 1m; not crying out for step back up in trip

runners.name: Balqaa
runners.spotlight: Regained the winning thread in Wolverhampton classified race (9.5f) last week and may remain competitive despite a 6lb rise in her mark; well treated on best handicap form at up to 1m2f.
runners.comment: Won classified event at Wolverhampton last week; well treated on best handicap form

runners.name: Shabu Shabu
runners.spotlight: Turf form last year featured an 11.5f success and some good efforts; likely player at this level provided he's as effective back on AW and manages to settle (pulled too hard when last seen).
runners.comment: Likely player at this level provided best turf form is transferred back to AW
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing Xxii & Da Bin Lo

runners.name: Lady Kameko
runners.spotlight: Maiden who isn't particularly solid on her 6f-1m form for current stable; bred to stay further but failed to convince with her stamina over middle distances for previous yard.
runners.comment: Maiden who isn't particularly solid on her 6f-1m form for current stable

runners.name: Lieutenant Lily
runners.spotlight: Nine-race maiden; far from solid on overall handicap form but shaped with some promise at Newcastle (1m2f; second start for new yard) most recently; check for market support.
runners.comment: Maiden; shaped with some promise last time (second start for new yard)

races.name: Make The Move To Midnite Handicap (Div II)

runners.name: Jack Andrea
runners.spotlight: Good second at Chelmsford last time when making debut for John Butler and bidding to complete a 1m2f double (maiden previously); remains in form and he has shaped well over C&D; respected.
runners.comment: Good second at Chelmsford last time (stable debut); has form over C&D; respected
runners.owner: J Rayment J Stephenson & R Eddery

runners.name: Cezarro
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish maiden; beat all bar the market leader at Kempton (1m; second run for new yard) most recently, getting closer to the target than previously; fighting chance, provided he's as effective back up in trip.
runners.comment: Maiden who ran well over 1m at Kempton (second start for new yard) most recently

runners.name: Dash Power
runners.spotlight: Gained sole win in a classified event and has made the frame several times at that level in 2026; not the percentage call returned to a handicap and going into unknown territory trip-wise.
runners.comment: Not the percentage call returned to a handicap and tackling new trip

runners.name: Newfire
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish mare who is 0-6 for current stable and remains an inconsistent sort; recorded best recent effort at classified level; beaten about 17l in Southwell handicap last time.
runners.comment: 0-6 for new stable and remains inconsistent; well held at Southwell last time

runners.name: Highfield Comrade
runners.spotlight: Record of 4133 since switched to AW, the highlight an account-opening success over C&D (classified event); may have more to offer back in this scenario and remains of interest at a modest level.
runners.comment: May have more to offer back at the scene of his success; remains of interest in Class 6

runners.name: Fiftyshadesaresdev
runners.spotlight: Placed numerous times, including at Southwell, but remains a longstanding maiden and sole effort this year doesn't suggest he's about to belatedly get off the mark.
runners.comment: Placed numerous times but remains a longstanding maiden

runners.name: Knight Of Magic
runners.spotlight: Successful three times at up to 1m6f already this year; ran below market expectations at Southwell (1m6f) most recently but looks solid in this scenario, with his C&D form figures reading 231.
runners.comment: Three wins in 2026; solid in this scenario, with his C&D form figures reading 231

runners.name: Kings College
runners.spotlight: Five-race maiden; showed signs of improvement with fifth of nine in Wolverhampton handicap (9.5f) on seasonal debut; possibilities, provided he builds on that effort and stays the new trip.
runners.comment: Showed signs of improvement on reappearance; possibilities if building on that effort

runners.name: Marcello Si
runners.spotlight: Usually runs at Newcastle and has never won elsewhere; inconsistent results in classified races this year; not beaten far at Southwell on penultimate but lacks solidity.
runners.comment: Usually runs at Newcastle; inconsistent results in classified races this year

runners.name: Night Bear
runners.spotlight: Only 1-29 on AW but gained the win at Southwell (in 2022) and is currently in a consistent vein of form, mainly here and most recently at Chelmsford; frame possibilities.
runners.comment: Only 1-29 on AW but is currently in a consistent vein of form; frame possibilities

races.name: BetMGM Supports Safer Gambling Handicap

runners.name: Roaring Ralph
runners.spotlight: Right back to form of late, winning over C&D in February and March; travelled strongly and just did enough for the second of those successes, and a 2lb rise may well not prevent the hat-trick.
runners.comment: C&D wins in February and March; 2lb rise may well not prevent the hat-trick

runners.name: Ardaddy
runners.spotlight: Regularly on the premises but he has just one win (over 7f on this course) from 18 attempts, and first-time cheekpieces failed to galvanise him over 6f here last time.
runners.comment: 1-18; first-time cheekpieces failed to galvanise him over 6f here last time

runners.name: Back From Dubai
runners.spotlight: 9yo course regular; beaten only 1l when fifth over C&D in January; should make his usual bold bid from the front.
runners.comment: 9yo course regular; fifth over C&D in January; should make usual bold bid from the front

runners.name: Pink Socks
runners.spotlight: Rattled off a hat-trick here and at Newcastle in January, but she was disappointing last time on her first outing since leaving Ian Williams; needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: Disappointing on first start for new yard last time and needs to bounce back

runners.name: Danehill Star
runners.spotlight: Has a solid AW record and he ran creditably in first-time blinkers when second of eight at Lingfield three weeks ago; should be thereabouts again off an unchanged mark.
runners.comment: Second at Lingfield three weeks ago; should be thereabouts again off an unchanged mark

runners.name: Teggy Lasso
runners.spotlight: Unplaced all five starts but he's shown clear signs of ability, 1m2f possibly stretching his stamina when he was fifth of nine at Newcastle (favourite) 17 days ago; dropped 2lb since, cheekpieces are now fitted and he returns to 1m; may still be unexposed.
runners.comment: 1m2f possibly too far when he was fifth at Newcastle; now back at 1m; may be unexposed
runners.owner: Alfa Site Services Ltd & Wendy OLeary

runners.name: Drumcondra
runners.spotlight: 0-9; went closest to breaking his duck at Musselburgh (1m1f) last summer; not disgraced both starts since, latest in October; faces some race-fit and in-form rivals on AW debut.
runners.comment: 0-9; faces some race-fit and in-form rivals on AW debut

runners.name: Finally Escaped
runners.spotlight: Regressive form in three runs from 7f-1m3f; does not look obviously well treated on handicap debut and best watched unless the market speaks in her favour.
runners.comment: Not obviously well treated on handicap debut; best watched unless strong in market

races.name: Godolphin Mile Sponsored By Emaar (Group 2) (Dirt)

runners.name: Banishing
runners.spotlight: Grade 2 winner in US last year; distant sixth in the Pegasus and was well held in the Saudi Cup since; however, this hold-up performer should get a good pace to aim at and this is a drop in class; it's also worth noting that he has form on sloppy tracks in US (including narrow defeat in Grade 1) if more rain arrives.
runners.comment: Grade 2 winner; hold-up performer who should get a good pace and drops in class
runners.owner: Sharaf Mohammed Al Hariri / David Jacobson & Lawrence Roman

runners.name: World Record
runners.spotlight: Winner over sprint trips in US and Grade 1 placed at Del Mar last year; chance he'll stay this new trip on pedigree but it was a low-key yard debut in the Group 3 Mahab Al Shimaal (6f) last month and this is tougher.
runners.comment: Winner over sprint trips in US; chance he'll stay but low-key yard debut in Gr 3 latest

runners.name: Commissioner King
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who has been progressive this term, winning at Sharjah on return before finishing runner-up in the Al Maktoum Mile and Firebreak Stakes; subsequently produced a career best to win the Burj Nahar, beating The Camden Colt by 4l and stall one is ideal for his preferred tactics; yard's first-string and he's a big player.
runners.comment: Progressive front-runner who arrives on back of career best; stall 1, yard's first-string

runners.name: Mendelssohn Bay
runners.spotlight: Progressive this season, producing a career best when getting the better of Commissioner King in the Firebreak Stakes on his penultimate start; well below form in the Al Maktoum Classic but warrants respect back at his preferred trip.
runners.comment: Progressive winner of the Firebreak Stakes; too bad to be true over 1m2f since

runners.name: Telemark
runners.spotlight: Gained his second course win of the season over C&D on his penultimate start but was behind three of these when beaten roughly 10l in the Burj Nahar; looks up against in this grade from a wide draw.
runners.comment: C&D winner but behind 3 of these when beaten 10l in Burj Nahar and up against it here

runners.name: The Camden Colt
runners.spotlight: C&D winner who posted a career best when beaten 4l in the Burj Nahar behind Commissioner King last month; would need another sizeable step forward to play a part.
runners.comment: C&D winner; career best when beaten 4l last month; needs another sizeable step forward

runners.name: Diamond Dealer
runners.spotlight: Four wins over 1m this term including two C&D handicaps on his last two starts; had a bit to spare here 15 days ago but this is a big step up in grade and he has stall 11 to overcome.
runners.comment: 4 wins at 1m this term but this is a big step up in grade and he has stall 11 to overcome

runners.name: David Of Athens
runners.spotlight: Has won back-to-back over 1m at Jebel Ali on last two starts, including Group 3 deadheat success (with Masmak) last month; form of those races has been knocked since and while he's the first-string on jockey bookings, others look stronger.
runners.comment: Arrives on back of 2 1m wins at Jebel Ali inc' Gr 3 but form knocked since

runners.name: Hypnus
runners.spotlight: Third in a pair of C&D Group 3s and ran to a similar level when 2l behind Zandvoort at Jebel Ali latest but much more required here.
runners.comment: 3rd in 2 C&D Gr 3s; similar level when 2l behind Zandvoort at Jebel Ali latest; needs more

runners.name: Generous Tipper
runners.spotlight: Only beaten a neck in a 1m2f handicap here but only a distant fourth when stepped up in class for the Al Maktoum Classic last month; needs career best and his best form has come over a bit further.
runners.comment: Neck defeat over 1m2f in h'cap but only distant 4th in Al Maktoum Class; drops in trip

runners.name: Zandvoort
runners.spotlight: C&D winner who followed up a 7f Jebel Ali success with a 1m win there 13 days ago; didn't appear to have much left in the locker though and this demands improvement.
runners.comment: C&D winner; 2 recent wins at Jebel Ali but this demands more

runners.name: Masmak
runners.spotlight: Deadheat with David Of Athens in Group 3 at Jebel Ali last month was a return to his best but lesser effort in the Al Maktoum Challenge since and this looks tough from the widest draw.
runners.comment: Deadheat win in Gr 3 at Jebel Ali was return to best; lesser run in Al Maktoum Challenge
runners.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum

races.name: Dubai Gold Cup Sponsored By Al Tayer Motors (Group 2) (Turf)

runners.name: Al Riffa
runners.spotlight: Irish St Leger winner last autumn; has run to a similar level on both runs since in the Melbourne Cup and the Hong Kong Vase, doing well to get as close as he did to Sosie at Sha Tin, given a muddling gallop over 1m4f would not have played to his strengths; back up in trip and leading claims down in grade (no Group 1 penalty) with a visor the headgear of choice; handles give in the ground.
runners.comment: Group 1 winner who ran well at Sha Tin last time; return to this trip should suit; claims

runners.name: Caballo De Mar
runners.spotlight: Much improved last year, with his best effort to land the Group 1 Cadran (2m4f, soft) at Longchamp on Arc weekend; below that level in the Royal Oak three weeks later, but that race may have come too quickly; no penalty and will appreciate rain; each-way prospect.
runners.comment: Much-improved last year, with best effort to win the Group 1 Cadran; each-way claims

runners.name: Sons And Lovers
runners.spotlight: Group 3 winner who ran to best when landing a Group 2 handicap at Riyadh over 1m7f last time; the emphasis that day was more on speed than stamina so he will have a bit to prove over this longer trip if this turns into a slog; seems to handle give in the ground and gives trainer a strong hand.
runners.comment: Ran to best over 1m7f in Feb; stamina to prove, especially if rain gets into the ground

runners.name: Fairy Glen
runners.spotlight: Had no problem with the drop in trip when winning a sub-standard Balanchine on her UAE debut last time; stays 1m6f but plenty to prove on these terms up to this trip for the first time.
runners.comment: Won the Balanchine over 1m1f last time but work to do over this trip for first time
runners.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum

runners.name: Sunway
runners.spotlight: Consistent sort who is closely matched with Al Nayyir on Nad Al Sheba Trophy form over 1m6f last month; had previously beaten Epic Poet over that trip and his effort behind Caballo De Mar in the Cadran shows he stays well; blinkers back on but will probably have to raise his game if he's to win, though.
runners.comment: Consistent sort who is closely matched with a few of these; needs to raise game to win
runners.owner: Guy Pariente Holding & Qatar Racing Ltd

runners.name: Dubai Future
runners.spotlight: Last year's winner who has failed to match that form since, most recently when down the field in the Lonsdale Cup at York in August (behind Al Nayyir); goes well fresh but all his best efforts have been on a sound surface so a wet week is a concern; hood discarded.
runners.comment: Won this last year but hasn't matched that since and seems best suited by a sound surface

runners.name: Epic Poet
runners.spotlight: Conditions winner over 1m6f at this venue over a year ago but, although he's not added to his tally, he's posted several creditable efforts, including at this venue and at Riyadh on last two starts; finished third to Dubai Future in this race last year and he'll have to raise his game.
runners.comment: Mainly creditable efforts since last win over a year ago but will have to raise his game

runners.name: Al Nayyir
runners.spotlight: Has run in the last three renewals, with his best effort a second-placing behind Tower Of London two years ago; showed he retains all his ability when beating Sunway a neck at level weights following a six-month break in last month's Nad Al Sheba Trophy; no problem with rain or the return to 2m but he'll have to be at his best to beat Al Riffa.
runners.comment: Second in this race in 2024 and warmed up for this renewal with a Group 3 win last time

runners.name: Burdett Road
runners.spotlight: Smart on the Flat and over hurdles but, although he's been running respectably this year, he didn't get home over 1m7f behind Sons And Lovers at Riyadh last month and he has a bit to find on these terms; hood refitted and won't mind rain but others appeal more.
runners.comment: Smart on Flat and over hurdles but needs to better this year's form if he's to win this

runners.name: Ruling Dynasty
runners.spotlight: Ran well in the face of a stiff task when fourth in the City Of Gold on Super Saturday but he's got plenty to prove on these terms going up to 2m for the first time; has only raced on a sound surface.
runners.comment: Ran well in the face of a stiff task on Super Saturday but plenty to prove going up to 2m

races.name: UAE Derby Sponsored By Jumeirah (Group 2) (Dirt)

runners.name: Pyromancer
runners.spotlight: Unbeaten after three starts in Japan and found more when challenged in the Derby prep at Kawasaki in December (same race won by Forever Young before his Saudi/UAE Derby wins); leaves the impression there's more to come from him back up in trip and he won his maiden on a sloppy track, so slower conditions will unlikely inconvenience if there's more rain.
runners.comment: Unbeaten after 3 runs in Japan; should improve back up in trip and won maiden on sloppy

runners.name: Six Speed
runners.spotlight: Progressed with every start and followed up his 7f UAE Guineas trial win with a 5l success over Devon Island in the UAE Guineas; has a lot of speed so he's not certain to see out this distance but could take some pegging back from stall two if he does stay.
runners.comment: Progressed with every run inc' UAE Guineas win latest; hard to peg back if he stays
runners.owner: Brunetti Dugan Stables Black Type Thoroughbreds et al

runners.name: Go Man
runners.spotlight: Heavy ground Listed winner in France but not sure to go on this surface and stall 12 will be tough to overcome.
runners.comment: Heavy ground Listed winner in France but not sure to go on this surface; stall 12 is tough
runners.owner: Christian Faure J-M Lefebvre et al

runners.name: Devon Island
runners.spotlight: Had Brotherly Love in behind in his two wins at the beginning of the season but was no match for Six Speed in the UAE 2,000 Guineas when beaten 5l; mixed signs in his pedigree on whether he'll stay this far but not ruled out.
runners.comment: Brotherly Love behind twice earlier this term but no match for Six Speed in UAE Guineas

runners.name: Labwah
runners.spotlight: Improved with each step up in trip including UAE Oaks success last month to make it three wins from four starts; Oaks form has taken a couple of knocks and good chance this requires fair bit more.
runners.comment: Firmly on up and improved with each step up in trip inc' UAE Oaks success; more required
runners.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum

runners.name: Wonder Dean
runners.spotlight: Won a dirt maiden in Japan before finishing half a length behind Pyromancer at Kyoto; runner-up at Hanshin since and had a tough trip out wide when fourth in the Saudi Derby last month; no surprise if he was in the mix with a better trip this time.
runners.comment: Dirt maiden winner; closely matched on form with Pyromancer; tough trip in Saudi Derby

runners.name: Brotherly Love
runners.spotlight: Won over 1m here and bounced back from a lesser effort behind Six Speed in UAE 2,000 Guineas when winning a C&D Listed contest last month; more required but could come into the picture if they go hard up front.
runners.comment: Won 2 of last 3 starts inc' Listed contest latest; more requried but not ruled out

runners.name: Rammaas
runners.spotlight: Won a maiden in US and bounced back from a low-key yard debut to win a 1m conditions event at Jebel Ali this month; much more required but his US win came on sloppy so further rain may help and there's stamina on the dam's side of his pedigree (dam won UAE Oaks).
runners.comment: Won US maiden on sloppy; arrives on back of Jebel Ali win; dam won UAE Oaks

runners.name: Duke Of Immatin
runners.spotlight: 7f Jebel Ali winner on his debut and bounced back from a couple of lesser efforts when runner-up in Listed company over C&D last month behind Brotherly Love; however, has a lot to find and stall 10 is wider than ideal.
runners.comment: Bounced back with C&D 2nd to Brotherly Love latest but needs a lot more here

runners.name: Lino Padrino
runners.spotlight: Maiden winner who was fourth behind Six Speed twice before finishing 2l behind Brotherly Love over C&D last month; type to improve with time but this could be beyond him at this stage.
runners.comment: In behind a few of these since winning on debut; should improve with time but tough ask
runners.owner: Suited & Booted - My Future Champions Racing

runners.name: Union Security
runners.spotlight: Won a 6f maiden here on debut; in behind Six Speed twice since and didn't run much of a race in the Saudi Derby; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Won 6f maiden here on debut; in behind Six Speed twice since and struggled in Saudi Derby

runners.name: Salloom
