runners.spotlight: Looked a fine prospect when winning a 1m maiden here by roughly 7l on his only start under rules; withdrawn after he became upset in the stalls subsequently and that's a cause for concern but it's noteworthy that O'Shea prefers him of the four Seemar runners.
runners.comment: Looked like a nice prospect when bolting up in a maiden; noteworthy he's first-string
runners.owner: King Abdullah Bin Abdulaziz Sons Stables

races.name: Al Quoz Sprint Sponsored By Azizi Developments (Group 1) (Turf)

runners.name: Lazzat
runners.spotlight: Has won eight of 15 starts, including Group 1 events at Deauville and at Ascot; better than bare facts of last run at Riyadh, where he was a neck behind Reef Runner over 6f166yds; won't mind the return to 6f and no problem with any give in the ground; leading contender.
runners.comment: Has won eight of 15 starts and better than bare facts of last run over 7f; leading claims

runners.name: Reef Runner
runners.spotlight: Has won eight of 23 starts and made it three wins from last four starts when successful upped to 6f166yds in Riyadh Group 2 last time; no problem with slight drop in distance and has decent claims but remains to be seen whether he's as effective if there's give in the ground.
runners.comment: Comes here at the top of game but has to prove he's as good with any give in the ground

runners.name: Rayevka
runners.spotlight: Best effort when dividing subsequent Group winners in the Commonwealth Cup at Royal Ascot; behind Lazzat in Group 1 at same venue in October but shaped better than the bare facts on reappearance over C&D (drawn on wrong side to main action); won't mind rain and no surprise to see her run well.
runners.comment: Third in last year's Commonwealth Cup and better than bare facts of UAE debut; claims

runners.name: Monteille
runners.spotlight: Career-best effort to beat Rayevka in the Group 3 Petit Couvert in September but 5l behind that rival on her UAE debut following a five-month break last time (when both were drawn away from the main action); won't mind rain but needs to get back to best.
runners.comment: Group 3 winner in France last autumn but needs to step up on form of her UAE debut

runners.name: Great Wish
runners.spotlight: Decent strike-rate in Scandinavia and sprang a surprise when winning C&D Listed event on UAE debut in January; better than bare facts of last run (best of those to race away from the far-side group) and handles give in the ground but he's another who'll need a clear pb to win in this higher grade.
runners.comment: Decent strike-rate and bit better than bare facts of last run but needs a clear pb to win
runners.owner: Lone Kaj-Nielsen S Bo Estate Of The Late Lone Kaj-Nielsen

runners.name: Northern Champion
runners.spotlight: Has done well this year, winning conditions events over both 6f and 7f at this venue and latest effort can be upgraded given he was away from the main action; no 3yo has won this race and plenty more needed if he's to figure in this company.
runners.comment: In good form winning both races in the UAE; first run in Group 1 and much more needed

runners.name: Cover Up
runners.spotlight: Improved performer this season over 5f but that progress stalled back over 6f when only third to Native Approach and Run Boy Run in Nad Al Sheba Turf Sprint on Super Saturday; needs a clear pb.
runners.comment: Improved over 5f this term but only third over 6f here last time; will have to do better

runners.name: Arabie
runners.spotlight: Respectable efforts this year but had the best of the draw when fifth to Native Approach in C&D Group 3 last time and, although cheekpieces worn that day now left off, he would be an unlikely winner on this stable debut.
runners.comment: Not won since 2024 and needs to step up significantly on recent form; stable debut

runners.name: Lugal
runners.spotlight: Grade 1 6f turf winner at Nakayama in 2024 but below that level since, though did run well when touched off over 7f on penultimate start; respectable effort in Grade 3 last time but he'll have to raise his game to win this.
runners.comment: Grade 1 winner in 2024; respectable efforts of late but he'll have to improve to win this

runners.name: Marbaan
runners.spotlight: Won last year's Al Fahidi Fort and Rhas Al Khor (both over 7f) before finishing sixth to Believing in this race; could only manage sixth in this year's Al Fahidi Fort and he'll need a big step forward if he's to win this; handles give in the ground.
runners.comment: Only sixth in this race last year and needs a big step forward if he's to win this

runners.name: Khaadem
runners.spotlight: Lost all chance at the start (slowly away, rider lost irons) in Hong Kong when last seen in December but better judged on previous efforts at Del Mar and Keenland; each-way claims if he gets a strong gallop to aim at, though may not want too much rain.
runners.comment: Lost chance at the start last time but each-way claims judged on previous efforts in US

runners.name: Run Boy Run
runners.spotlight: Last year's Ayr Gold Cup winner (soft) who won over 5f here on penultimate start; ran well in favoured far side group over C&D last time but surprise if there's not one or two too strong in this grade.
runners.comment: Running well of late but things went his way last time; may find one or two too good

runners.name: Native Approach
runners.spotlight: Well drawn and caused a shock tried sprinting for the first time when back to winning ways in Group 3 over C&D last time; that form leaves him with something to find and it'll be a surprise if he follows up; has only raced on a sound surface on turf.
runners.comment: Caused a shock down to sprinting for first time last month; more needed to follow up

races.name: Dubai Golden Shaheen Sponsored By Nakheel (Group 1) (Dirt)

runners.name: Bentornato
runners.spotlight: Won seven of his 11 starts in the US including the Breeders' Cup Sprint from stall 10 last time out and the runner-up has boosted the form with a win in the Group 2 Riyadh Dirt Sprint; looks like the one to beat.
runners.comment: Won 7 of his 11 in US including Breeders' Cup Sprint from wide draw; the one to beat
runners.owner: Leon King Stable Corp Michael Iavarone & Julia C Iavarone

runners.name: El Nasseeb
runners.spotlight: Five-time C&D winner; hold-up performer who followed up a Listed success on return with a 3l Group 3 success with a couple of these in behind; didn't appreciate a switch of tactics when runner-up to Drew's Gold in the Al Shindagha Sprint but posted a career best when beating Dark Saffron by 1l last month; has a bit to find but this race could set up well for him.
runners.comment: 5-time C&D winner who beat Dark Saffron in Gr 3 latest; this could set up well for him
runners.owner: Mohd Abdulla Mohd Rashed Al Shehhi

runners.name: Lovesick Blues
runners.spotlight: Hold-up performer who won a Grade 1 at Del Mar last season but was no match for Bentornato at the Breeders' Cup; got a pace to aim at when only fifth in the Saudi Dirt Sprint last time out; they should go a decent gallop here but he'll need a career best.
runners.comment: No match for Bentornato at Breeders' Cup and only 5th in Saudi Dirt Sprint latest

runners.name: Midland Money
runners.spotlight: Two wins in US and has finished runner-up in his last three runs for Bob Baffert including a 1l defeat in 7f Grade 1 latest; that form hasn't really worked out but shaped like a return to this trip could be in his favour and he's an interesting recruit for this yard.
runners.comment: 2 wins in US; 2nd in 7f Grade 1 latest; form hasn't really worked out but 6f may suit

runners.name: Tuz
runners.spotlight: Won this in 2024 and prolific with four C&D wins last year before finishing 1l third to Dark Saffron in this; missed most of current campaign and probably needed last month's return when fading late to finish fifth of 10 in Group 3; that should put him spot on and he'll likely be involved if back to his best.
runners.comment: Won this in 2024 and beaten 1l last year after 4 C&D wins; probably needed recent return

runners.name: Cats By Five
runners.spotlight: Won a couple of C&D handicaps this season but was behind five of these when stepped up in class in Group 3 latest and hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Behind five of these when stepped up in class in Group 3 latest and hard to fancy
runners.owner: Sh Humaid Bin Rashid Al Nuaimi

runners.name: Mufasa
runners.spotlight: Behind El Nasseeb on all four starts this campaign; further rain may not be an issue given that he won on a sloppy track when trained in US and should get a pace to aim at so may be capable of outrunning his odds.
runners.comment: Behind El Nasseeb on all 4 starts this campaign; should get strong pace

runners.name: Dark Saffron
runners.spotlight: Highly progressive last season culminating in shock win in this, with Nakatomi and Tuz in behind; winning return but subsequently struggled to get anywhere near his best before a major step back in the right direction when beaten 1l by El Nasseeb last month; however, he's going to face plenty of pressure for the lead in this renewal.
runners.comment: Won this in 2025; step back in right direction latest; will face pace pressure this time

runners.name: Nakatomi
runners.spotlight: No match for Tuz when third in 2024 but finished in front of that rival when runner-up to Dark Saffron in this last year; back to form when landing a Grade 2 at Keeneland in October and had tough trip behind Bentornato since; may do better back at this venue and he has winning form on a sloppy track, so more rain may not be an issue.
runners.comment: Placed in the last 2 renewals; Gr 2 winner in October before tough trip at Breeders' Cup
runners.owner: Mrs Fitriani Hay And Qatar Racing LLC

runners.name: Colour Up
runners.spotlight: Three-time C&D winner on a long losing run; regularly behind Tuz and Dark Saffron last season before a fifth in this and he's been no match for El Nasseeb and a few others this term.
runners.comment: 3-time C&D winner; long losing run and no match for El Nasseeb this term

runners.name: American Stage
runners.spotlight: No excuses when sixth in this last term and similar story when fourth to Bentornato in Breeders' Cup Sprint; ran to a similar level when fourth in the Saudi Dirt Sprint last month and has bit to find back up in class.
runners.comment: No excuses when 6th in this last term and behind Bentornato in Breeders' Cup Sprint

runners.name: Khanjar
runners.spotlight: Both dirt wins at 7f including a handicap in February; poor turf run since and not sure to be suited by the return to 6f back on dirt.
runners.comment: Both dirt wins at 7f; poor turf run latest and not sure to be suited by 6f back on turf

races.name: Dubai Turf Sponsored By DP World (Group 1) (Turf)

runners.name: Ombudsman
runners.spotlight: Top-class performer and clear form choice; took his form to a higher level last season, with wins in the Prince of Wales's and Juddmonte International and when second to Calandagan in the Champion Stakes; all best form is on a sound surface but has won on soft and he's reportedly a stronger and more mature sort this time round; really soft ground would be a worry but otherwise he's got plenty in his favour; trainer won this race three times with Lord North this decade.
runners.comment: Clear form choice on last year's achievements; handles soft and seems sure to run well

runners.name: Fort George
runners.spotlight: Steadily progressive sort who turned in best effort when second to prolific scorer Rebel's Romance in the City Of Gold on his first run over 1m4f last time; no problem with the drop back in distance but he needs a clear career best if he's to win this; only raced so far on a sound surface on turf.
runners.comment: Steadily progressive sort who ran well over 1m4f last time; has only raced on quick ground

runners.name: Dividend
runners.spotlight: Won two handicaps around this trip before progressing again when second to Quddwah in the Singspiel on Super Saturday; needs clear pb and only raced on fast ground on turf so will have something to prove if the ground eases.
runners.comment: Progressing nicely at this venue but needs a clear pb and soft ground would be a worry

runners.name: Quddwah
runners.spotlight: Had the rub of things but matched best effort when beating the in-form Dividend over C&D in the Singspiel on Super Saturday; that will need improving on returned to Group 1 company but is flexible ground-wise.
runners.comment: Well placed in slowly run race but matched best effort over C&D last time; more needed

runners.name: Make Me King
runners.spotlight: Made it seven wins from 27 starts when beating Holloway Boy (unplaced in this last year) over 1m at Doha last time; stays this trip and handles soft ground and, although he goes up to this level for the first time, there's worse each-way chances.
runners.comment: Multiple winner who stays this far and handles soft ground; minor place claims at a price

runners.name: Folk Festival
runners.spotlight: Hasn't had too many chances but turned in an improved effort when fifth to Quddwah in the Singspiel; has only raced on a sound surface and makes no appeal on these terms.
runners.comment: Improved effort when fifth to Quddwah in the Singspiel last time; plenty more needed
runners.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum

runners.name: Elnajmm
runners.spotlight: Better than ever following ten months off and comes here on the three-timer having won the Al Fahidi Fort and Ras Al Khor over 7f at this track; has won over this trip and seems to handle soft but he'll have to improve again if he's to figure.
runners.comment: Comes here in good form with two 7f wins in 2025; more needed up in grade/back up in trip

runners.name: Tumbler
runners.spotlight: Smart performer but yet to win a Listed or Group event and, although he ran well in the face of a stiff task in the Al Fahidi Fort, he's been below that level since and he's an unlikely winner.
runners.comment: Smart sort but yet to win a Listed/Group event; needs a clear career-best effort to figure
runners.owner: Scea Haras Des Forets Regis Clarot Et Al

runners.name: Gaia Force
runners.spotlight: Won three of first five outings but had to wait until last October (Grade 2 at Tokyo) before he registered another success; placed five times at the highest level in Japan, including last time, and no problem with this trip; has only raced on fast ground so far, so remains to be seen how he'll fare should the ground soften appreciably.
runners.comment: Broke losing run in October and placed in Grade 1 latest; unproven away from quick ground

runners.name: Facteur Cheval
runners.spotlight: Won this race two years ago and, although he's not added to his tally since, he ran well in the Neom Turf Cup at Riyadh following a four-month break last time and his last three runs on soft (all in Group 1 company) saw him finish second each time; will be one to be interested in if the forecast rain on Friday lands on the track.
runners.comment: Won this in 2024 and goes well on soft; will be a big danger if the forecast rain lands
runners.owner: Team Valor International LLC & Gary Barber

runners.name: Andreas Vesalius
runners.spotlight: Running consistently well over 7f and 1m this season, including when third to Elnajmm in the Ras Al Khor last time; won on soft ground when trained in Ireland but he makes little appeal over the longest trip he's tackled in this company.
runners.comment: String of consistent efforts this season but will have to raise game by some way to figure

races.name: Longines Dubai Sheema Classic (Group 1) (Turf)

runners.name: Calandagan
runners.spotlight: Runner-up to Danon Decile in a steadily run Sheema Classic a year ago (when ridden by Mickael Barzalona for first time) but ended up as the world's highest-rated racehorse of 2025 following four straight Group 1 wins, including the King George, the Champion Stakes and the Japan Cup; reportedly both mentally and physically stronger than last year and, although the prospect of another falsely run race is a concern, he'll likely have too much in the way of pace for these rivals; handles most ground.
runners.comment: Runner-up in this last year but went on to become the world's highest-rated racehorse

runners.name: Royal Power
runners.spotlight: Steadily progressive sort; won a handicap over C&D last February and shaped as though retaining all his ability when accounting for West Wind Blows in Listed event at Abu Dhabi exactly a year later; unwise to rule out further progress but he has a long way to go to get to the level shown by Calandagan and Ethical Diamond; first run in a Group event; all turf runs on a sound surface; hood removed.
runners.comment: Showed he retains his ability after a year off in February; considerably more required

runners.name: Giavellotto
runners.spotlight: Plenty to find with Calandagan on 2025 form in this race and in the Coronation Cup but he's a high-class performer who ran well in the Arc, in the Hong Kong Vase and in Doha last month (would have preferred a stronger gallop on last two starts); lack of pace is again a concern and he'll have to improve to beat an in-form Calandagan but no surprise to see him give another good account; handles most ground.
runners.comment: Behind Calandagan in this race and in the Coronation Cup in 2025 but has place claims
runners.owner: Scuderia La Tesa Limited & Vaibhav Shah

runners.name: By The Book
runners.spotlight: Hasn't had much racing for one of his age but, although he wasn't far off his best when third to Al Nayyir and Sunway (both run in the Dubai Gold Cup) over 1m6f last month, he needs a big step forward if he's to figure on this first run in a Group 1; best run came on yielding ground (over C&D).
runners.comment: Unexposed sort who ran well over 1m6f last time; considerably more to do in this grade

runners.name: Ethical Diamond
runners.spotlight: Vastly improved last year, going from Royal Ascot handicap winner (off 96) to beating prolific scorer Rebel's Romance in the Breeders' Cup Turf (course record) and has reportedly acclimatised well in Dubai; off since November but one from this yard is unlikely to fall short on the grounds of fitness; handles give and, although he's another who would like a gallop to aim at, he is capable of better this year; only the trainer's second World Cup night runner but is a fascinating contender.
runners.comment: Much improved in 2025, ending with a win in the Breeders' Cup Turf; off since but a player

runners.name: West Wind Blows
runners.spotlight: Dual Group 3 winner who has been placed in Group 1 company at Sandown and in Australia in 2023; below that level since returning from a lengthy absence, though, and he'll likely struggle in this company; handles most ground.
runners.comment: Capable sort; held by Royal Power on recent run and plenty to find in this company

races.name: Dubai World Cup Sponsored By Emirates Airline (Group 1) (Dirt)

runners.name: Forever Young
runners.spotlight: Top-class dirt performer who won the UAE Derby in 2024 before an unlucky defeat in the Kentucky Derby; just prevailed in last season's Saudi Cup after a battle with Romantic Warrior before a 2l defeat in this contest when probably still feeling the effects of the Saudi race; unbeaten since, including a Breeders' Cup Classic success over Sierra Leone and had a much easier time when winning this year's Saudi Cup latest; versatile from a tactical perspective and hard to oppose.
runners.comment: Excuse in this last term; Breeders' Cup Classic/Saudi Cup wins last 2 runs; hard to oppose

runners.name: Magnitude
runners.spotlight: Stayed on well when runner-up in the Grade 1 Pennsylvania Derby in September before a Grade 2 success at Churchill Downs in November; justified odds on favouritism when dropped in class for his prep run and more to come, although he needs a sizeable step up on what he's achieved so far to win; yard won this with 4yo Curlin in 2008.
runners.comment: Grade 1 2nd in Sept'; unbeaten in 2 runs since; more to come but this is his toughest test

runners.name: Hit Show
runners.spotlight: Got up late to record a 66-1 shock in this race last season and has won three of his six starts since at a lesser level in US; likely to have more to do in this renewal, particularly if Forever Young is on song but he's versatile with regards to tactics and not one to underestimate.
runners.comment: Got up late for 66-1 shock in this last season; arrives in good form and not ruled out

runners.name: Tap Leader
runners.spotlight: Winning yard debut in a C&D handicap in January but third behind Meydaan and Imperial Emperor since and hard to see him turning that form around.
runners.comment: Winning yard debut in C&D h'cap but only third behind Meydaan and Walk Of Stars since

runners.name: Tumbarumba
runners.spotlight: Chased home Imperial Emperor before finishing roughly 5l behind Forever Young in the Saudi Cup; hard to see him reversing that form and he's drawn widest.
runners.comment: 2nd to Imperial Emperor before finishing 5l behind Forever Young in Saudi Cup; tough ask

runners.name: Meydaan
runners.spotlight: Listed winner on turf but took his form to a new level when bolting up in the Group 2 Al Maktoum Classic on dirt debut last month, an effort worth marking up having come from rear in a steadily run race; this is another step up but expect him to be finishing off well when others have cracked.
runners.comment: Al Maktoum Classic winner on dirt debut' another step up but likely to be finishing well

runners.name: Heart Of Honor
runners.spotlight: Four-time course winner including Listed race in December; stayed on for third behind Imperial Emperor in Group 1 Al Maktoum Challenge in Janaury but was a disappointing favourite in the Al Maktoum Classic since; often misses the break and is held up so he'll need them to overdo it in front.
runners.comment: Gr 1 3rd behind Imperial Emperor but disappointing favourite since; needs them to go hard

runners.name: Imperial Emperor
runners.spotlight: Progressive last term culminating in an Al Maktoum Classic success before a no-show in this race (finished lame); Al Maktoum Mile winner on return before gaining a first Group 1 success in January's Al Maktoum Challenge, beating Tumbarumba by roughly 1l; however, this is a deeper Group 1 and he'll need a career best but yard know what it takes to win this and he's the first-string.
runners.comment: Breakthrough Gr 1 success latest; this is deeper but yard know what it takes to win this

runners.name: Walk Of Stars
runners.spotlight: Had Imperial Emperor back in second when winning the Al Maktoum Challenge last season but starting to look a little flattered by that Group 1 success; no match for Meydaan in the Al Maktoum Classic last time out; probably needs an uncontested lead to get competitive, which isn't completely out of the question.
runners.comment: Group 1 winner last term but starting to look flattered; no match for Meydaan latest

races.name: 1xBet.ie Supports Safe Gambling Fillies Maiden

runners.name: Little Sure Shot
runners.spotlight: Good run on debut at Leopardstown (7.5f, good) last June but disappointed twice since, including on soft ground when last seen at Naas last September; blinkers now tried.
runners.comment: Hasn't built on debut promise, struggled on soft when last seen, blinkers on

runners.name: Spring Is Here
runners.spotlight: Excellent run on debut behind Lady Iman, True Love and Power Blue in 5f listed race at this venue (good) but has proven expensive to follow in three runs since; disappointing run when last seen in a 5f maiden at Dundalk in January (hampered at start) and a bit to prove now.
runners.comment: Hasn't built on debut promise in three starts since, a bit to prove now

runners.name: Closely
runners.spotlight: Stepped forward from a disappointing debut with a 6l fourth of 10 at Cork (6f, good) last June; off since and different ground here but she's a half-sister to a heavy ground winner so could handle it; top yard has started the season well and she has to be considered with Moore up for the first time.
runners.comment: Better effort second time out at Cork, ground an unknown but has to be considered
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Opalite
runners.spotlight: Zoustar filly; 7,000GBP yearling; first foal; dam unraced half-sister to winners Vitalogy (US 8.5f Grade 3), Teomaria (1m) and Carnage (2m hurdle), out of Listed-placed 7f GB/US winner; market can reveal expectations on debut.
runners.comment: Zoustar filly, dam half-sister to Grade 3 US winner, market will reveal expectations

runners.name: Meriden
runners.spotlight: Bated Breath filly; 32,000gns 2yo; fifth foal; closely related to Listed-placed French/US 7f/1m winner Halfway Line (inc 2yo; RPR 103), half-sister to 1m/8.3f winner Mercury Day (87); dam French Group-placed 1m 2yo winner (108); makes some appeal on paper so check the market.
runners.comment: Bated Breath filly, dam a Group placed 2yo winner, check the market

runners.name: Aboveheronlysky
runners.spotlight: Outran long odds on debut at Navan in 2024 but missed all of last season and moderate comeback run over C&D 13 days ago; more needed.
runners.comment: Outran odds on debut, missed all of last year, moderate run on comeback, more needed

runners.name: Focaccia
runners.spotlight: Kodiac filly; E68,000 yearling; sister to US 5f/5.5f dirt/turf winner Salt Spray, half-sister to winners Big Leader (1m inc AW; RPR 101) and Cashew (6f 2yo; 83); dam placed 9.7f-1m4f (inc AW; 59), half-sister to 5f 2yo Group 2 winner; yard can ready one so worth a market check.
runners.comment: Kodiac filly, half-sister to two winners, yard can ready one, worth a market check

runners.name: Slaney View
runners.spotlight: Promise both starts early last season over 5f here on soft (3.25l behind subsequent Group 1 winner Power Blue) and at Cork on good ground; has been off the track since that 2.5l third at Cork last April but that debut effort stands out and if she repeats that, she should be thereabouts.
runners.comment: 5f promise early last year here and at Cork, repeat of debut effort may be enough
runners.owner: Molly & Paul Willis Racing Mrs J S Bolger

runners.name: Espritroyale
runners.spotlight: Showed little in a barrier trial and two starts over 7f on good ground for Sheila Lavery; hood worn for both those runs discarded; soft ground an unknown and best watched on first run for new yard.
runners.comment: Showed little for Sheila Lavery, soft ground an unknown, watch on stable debut

runners.name: Amerilis
runners.spotlight: Showed a glimmer of promise over C&D on debut (good) last May but didn't build on it upped to 7.5f on softer ground at Leopardstown in July; may be one for handicaps.
runners.comment: Mild promise over C&D on debut last May, not as good latest, may be one for h'caps

runners.name: Deirdreanme
runners.spotlight: Failed to beat a rival either start; tongue tie goes on; unlikely to be the answer here.
runners.comment: Last in both starts, tongue tie goes on, unlikely to be the answer here

runners.name: Ochtar Againn
runners.spotlight: 50-1 and never figured on sole start last season over C&D (heavy); hard to fancy on the evidence of that effort.
runners.comment: 50-1 and well beaten over C&D on debut last year, hard to fancy on that effort

races.name: Your Game. Your Odds. Your 1xBet.ie Handicap

runners.name: Dmaniac
runners.spotlight: Won over 6f here on final start of 2025 and reappeared with another 6f win here two weeks ago when he had a few of these behind, both on heavy; raised 7lb for that and drops back to 5f for the first time since his debut; will need a career best but could be more to come; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Two 6f wins here last two starts, up 7lb for latest, drops to 5f, may be more to come

runners.name: Stag Night
runners.spotlight: Course winner over 6f but effective at this trip; posted a solid effort on seasonal return when a 4.75l third behind Dmaniac here two weeks ago (6f, heavy); 8lb swing should enable him to get closer to that rival now; player.
runners.comment: Course winner, 4.75l behind Dmaniac here a fortnight ago, 8lb better off now, player

runners.name: Bodhi Bear
runners.spotlight: Returned to action last season with a 5f win in a rated race at Cork (good); lost way subsequently but was running in some hot handicaps; assessor giving him a chance but soft ground may not be ideal.
runners.comment: Won first time up last year, handicapper giving him a chance but soft ground a worry

runners.name: Staysound Susie
runners.spotlight: Won over C&D (heavy) on final run of 2025  off 7lb lower; moderate effort when a 7.75l fifth behind Dmaniac here on seasonal return (6f, heavy); will be sharper now but may need a bit more help from the handicapper.
runners.comment: C&D winner off 7lb lower, sharper for latest but may be on a tough mark for now

runners.name: Smoke Them Out
runners.spotlight: Notched up a third career win when making all on seasonal return here two weeks ago (6f, heavy); that was his second 6f success here but he has yet to win over 5f so might be vulnerable off 4lb higher.
runners.comment: Up 4lb for all the way 6f win here on seasonal return, has yet to win over 5f

runners.name: Airspeed
runners.spotlight: One of three for yard; ran well over C&D on final two starts of 2025; 0.75l behind Gazelle d'Or last September (yld-sft) and 2l behind Staysound Susie here in November (heavy); could go well if fit for his return.
runners.comment: Two good C&D runs last year, could go well if fit for his seasonal reappearance

runners.name: Gazelle dOr
runners.spotlight: Enjoyed a fruitful campaign last year with three wins, including here over 6f on her seasonal return; also won the Joe McGrath off 83 over C&D and ended the season rated 89; will need a career best here but goes well fresh and dangerous to dismiss.
runners.comment: Three wins last year inc' over C&D, needs career best off this mark but can't dismiss

runners.name: Heavenly Power
runners.spotlight: One of three for yard; five-time winner including three 6f victories at this venue; on losing run since winning at Naas in October 2024 (6f, yielding); no joy last season but did run well in defeat at times and returns 3lb below his last winning mark; record fresh suggests he might need this.
runners.comment: Two-time course winner at 6f, 3lb below last win, might need this first run back
runners.owner: Exors of the Late Mrs S Power

runners.name: Nezeeh
runners.spotlight: Gained fifth win of career off 3lb lower at Bellewstown last October (5f, soft); also won here over 6f (good) in August; out of form on AW when last seen in November and this looks a big ask on his seasonal reappearance.
runners.comment: Won four last year including here over 6f, this a big ask on his seasonal reappearance

runners.name: Magical Vision
runners.spotlight: C&D winner off 75 in April 2024 but hasn't won since; decent fourth on seasonal return at Naas (6f, soft) last weekend but 8lb wrong here and looks up against it on these terms.
runners.comment: C&D winner in April 2024, no joy since, decent run at Naas latest but 8lb wrong here

runners.name: Tolebi
runners.spotlight: Won a 3yo maiden at Saint-Cloud (7f, gd-sft) last year for Andre Fabre; subsequently placed in a 6f listed heat at Deauville (good), running to an RPR of 99; bought for 10,000 euros at Arqana Deauville sale in November; interesting recruit but trip and ground queries for Irish debut.
runners.comment: Decent form in France at 6f-7f, trip and ground queries for Irish debut

runners.name: Keke
runners.spotlight: One of three for yard; loves it here, as is evidenced by his three C&D victories; returns to action 6lb above his last win and has tended to improve for his seasonal reappearances in the past so might need this but can't totally discount given his liking for this venue.
runners.comment: Three time C&D winner, 6lb above latest success, could go well if fit after break

runners.name: Erosandpsyche
runners.spotlight: Smart sort on his day but hasn't won since 2022; highly tried last season, his best run a 0.5l second in listed company at Tipperary (5f, yielding); won his last start in handicap company but was 8lb lower then and would probably prefer better ground.
runners.comment: No win since 2022, 8lb above last handicap win, would probably prefer better ground

runners.name: Go Athletico
runners.spotlight: Two-time course winner including a Group 3 in 2023 and the Scurry handicap last year off 2lb higher; well beaten on seasonal return here (6f, heavy) behind Dmaniac; should strip fitter now but probably best over a bit further these days.
runners.comment: Two-time course winner, strip fitter for seasonal return but this trip may be too sharp

runners.name: Sommelier
runners.spotlight: Smart juvenile form on AW; got to within 1.5l of Big Evs in a 3yo listed race at York (5f, good) in May 2024 when trained by Marco Botti; lost way since that fine run and picked up for 26,000gns last June; unraced on slower than good and likely best watched on stable debut.
runners.comment: Smart form in 2024 but lost way since, unraced on slow ground, watch on Irish debut

races.name: McDonnell's Bar Handicap

runners.name: Tassarolo
runners.spotlight: Two-time hurdles winner is 1-6 on the Flat; always behind when failing to beat a rival home at Dundalk (1m4f) on return from a break 13 days ago; needs to improve plenty on that to make an impact here.
runners.comment: Always behind on return from break at Dundalk latest, needs more to feature here

runners.name: Nancee Spain
runners.spotlight: Built on promise of first two handicap runs when making all and winning by 15l at Roscommon (1m4f, soft) in October; hiked up 14lb so will need to improve again to follow up but that's not impossible; can't be discounted if ready after break.
runners.comment: Made all to win by 15l at Roscommon in October, up 14lb but can't discount

runners.name: Dutch Gold
runners.spotlight: Course winner over 1m2f in October 2023 but winless since; tailed off in the Lincolnshire over 1m here 13 days ago ago; step up in trip a plus but hard to fancy for now.
runners.comment: No win since 1m2f success here in 2023, tailed off latest, hard to fancy for now
runners.owner: Albert Dravins & Exors of the Late Eamonn Scanlon

runners.name: Arch Enemy
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose; 5-55 on turf on the Flat but on losing run since scoring at Leopardstown (1m4f, heavy) in October 2024; is now 17lb below that win and she shaped promisingly over an inadequate 1m here 13 days ago in a first-time tongue tie (retained); could go well back up in trip.
runners.comment: 17lb below last win in Oct' 2024, shaped well over 1m here latest, go well back up in trip

runners.name: Coul Dreamer
runners.spotlight: Winning hurdler is 0-6 on turf on the Flat; well beaten on sole handicap start at Roscommon last October and just 1lb lower now; lacks a run and probably best watched in first-time visor.
runners.comment: 0-6 on turf on the Flat, well beaten in sole h'cap at Roscommon, watch in first-time visor
runners.owner: Mrs Suzanne McAuley & Ms C A Jennings

runners.name: Factual Fact
runners.spotlight: Went down by a nose in a C&D maiden on his last flat start last November; pulled up over hurdles on sole start since; opening mark looks fair and worth monitoring in the market on seasonal return and handicap debut.
runners.comment: Beaten a nose over C&D in Nov', pulled up over timber latest, check market in first h'cap

runners.name: Blue Moon Boy
runners.spotlight: Two-time winner on the Flat; won off 3lb lower at Killarney  (1m3f, soft) last October; returns to the level after three runs over hurdles so won't lack for fitness; worth considering.
runners.comment: Two-time winner, 3lb above last success, fit from hurdling, worth considering

runners.name: Raydamann
runners.spotlight: Winning hurdler is 0-10 on the Flat but has run well in defeat; pulled up over hurdles on latest last month and best efforts have come on good ground; others preferred.
runners.comment: Winning hurdler, 0-10 on the Flat, pulled up over timber latest, ground a concern

runners.name: Rock Of Ireland
runners.spotlight: 0-12 for Alan McIntyre but finished second off 12lb higher at Tipperary (1m4f, yielding) last May; well-treated on that run but form dipped since and likely best watched on stable debut and seasonal reappearance.
runners.comment: 0-12 for former yard, placed off 12lb higher last May, poor since and best watched

runners.name: God Of Thunder
runners.spotlight: Dual purpose; 2-16 on the Flat, the wins coming on the AW and on good ground at Limerick; just 1lb above that Limerick win but best form on turf is on good ground and recent AW runs have been moderate; others preferred.
runners.comment: 1lb above last win at Limerick, moderate AW efforts recently, soft ground a concern

runners.name: Maeve Brennan
runners.spotlight: Handicap debutante; clear promise in three maidens here last year, including two over C&D; just over 3l to find with Factual Fact on their C&D maiden clash in November but 2lb worse off here so may not turn that form around; still has a place chance if fit.
runners.comment: Handicap debutante, behind Factual Fact here latest and 2lb worse off, place shout if fit
runners.owner: Eleanora Kennedy & Mrs J S Bolger

runners.name: Rain
runners.spotlight: Won off 6lb lower at Galway (1m4f, soft) last September but struggled off revised mark twice since; off since October and has tended to need her seasonal returns in the past; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Won off 6lb lower at Galway last year, poor twice since, may need this run
runners.owner: Exors of the Late Mrs E P Harty

runners.name: Neptunes Staircase
runners.spotlight: Off the track since dominant maiden win at Roscommon in July 2024 (1m4f, good); handles testing ground and has C&D form so he needs checking in the market.
runners.comment: Off since maiden win in July 2024, has C&D form on testing ground, check market
runners.owner: David Spratt & Sean Jones & Mrs Lynne Lyons

runners.name: Mr Rango
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner at 1m2f on soft-heavy, including one win here; on losing run since April 2024; now 8lb below last win but struggled in the Lincolnshire here 13 days ago and can only be watched for now.
runners.comment: No win since April 2024, struggled here 13 days ago, can only be watched for now

runners.name: Nelson Muntz
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose hurdles winner is 0-8 on turf on the level; well beaten in both previous handicap starts on the Flat and not easy to make a case for him.
runners.comment: Hurdles winner, 0-8 on turf on the Flat, has struggled in two previous h'caps

races.name: 1xBet.ie Supports Irish Racing Gladness Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Rahmi
runners.spotlight: Three-time handicap winner, including over 7f at Naas on his final start in 2024; ran only three times last season; produced a smart display to win a 7f premier handicap at the Irish Champions meeting; has run in only one stakes race, finishing a fair fourth of seven in a Listed race at Cork on last year's seasonal debut.
runners.comment: Three-time handicap winner, suited by this trip, has run in only one stakes race,
runners.owner: Richard McNally & Leo Walsh & L Murrin

runners.name: Albert Einstein
runners.spotlight: First foal out of a Group-placed 5f AW winner from the family of Giant's Causeway, winner of this race in 2000; home reputation preceded him at Naas when beating the second favourite readily, with a 12l gap back to the third; beat subsequent Group 1 winner Power Blue in the 6f Marble Hill here last May; has maintained profile as a potential star without running since.
runners.comment: Billed as an exciting prospect from the early part of last season, massive home reputation

runners.name: East Hampton
runners.spotlight: 6f course maiden winner at two; started last season with victory in the 7f Madrid Handicap at Naas; career-best when close second in Group 2 Minstrel Stakes over C&D (7f, good to yielding) behind Diego Velazquez who followed up in the Group 1 Marois; not at best in a Listed race at Cork (Rahmi fourth); never counted in a Group 2 at Doncaster on final start.
runners.comment: Career-best when close second in Group 2 Minstrel Stakes over C&D, can run well when fresh

runners.name: Ten Bob Tony
runners.spotlight: Reappeared last season with win in Group 3 John Of Gaunt at Haydock (7f, good) in May; ran only three times subsequently; excellent display when third of 16 in the Group 1 Foret at Longchamp in October (7f, very soft); found 6f inadequate at Group 1 level on final start.
runners.comment: Showed he could run well fresh with Group 3 win last May, fine third in the Foret on soft

runners.name: Princess Child
runners.spotlight: Improved form last August, winning a 7f Galway handicap and a Group 3 over an extended 7f at Tipperary; sustained that progress when fourth of ten in a 1m Group 2 event at Longchamp in October; suited by soft ground.
runners.comment: Progressive in the second half of last season, two domestic wins and fine effort in France

runners.name: Big Gossey
runners.spotlight: Splendid veteran with eight Curragh wins to his name; thoroughly deserved his career highlight when beating a subsequent Classic winner in this race last year; has also produced many good displays in defeat here; showed plenty of zest with Dundalk second in December; eased when beaten on reappearance in the Irish Lincoln.
runners.comment: Recorded career highlight when winning this race last year, unlikely to repeat

runners.name: Wannabe Royal
runners.spotlight: Fair form at this trip last season for Joseph O'Brien, highlighted by a 6l maiden win at Limerick (soft) and third of six in a conditions event at Gowran (heavy); confirmed a liking for testing conditions when fourth of 18 over 6f here last October; faces a tough task at these weights.
runners.comment: Useful 7f form in testing conditions, faces a tough task at these weights, change of yard

runners.name: Lord Massusus
runners.spotlight: Seven career wins include a 1m Listed race at Leopardstown early last season; best subsequent effort when fourth over C&D in the Minstrel Stakes last July.
runners.comment: Seven career wins include a Listed race at Leopardstown early last season, two course wins

runners.name: Saracen
runners.spotlight: E500,000 yearling; has run only three times; won a 6f course maiden on soft on only juvenile start; promising third in Group 3 Greenham at Newbury (7f, good); did not appear again after finishing in rear in the Jersey Stakes; first time with a tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Has run only three times, promising third in the Greenham, in rear in the Jersey Stakes

runners.name: Neolithic
runners.spotlight: Stepped up from his first two runs with a 6f win at Naas last July; yard's second-string.
runners.comment: Stepped up from his first two runs with a 6f win at Naas last July, yard's second-string
runners.owner: Westerberg & Mrs Magnier & M Tabor & D Smith

races.name: 1xBet.ie Proud Supporters Of Irish Racing Maiden

runners.name: Listentodwindblow
runners.spotlight: Ran creditably in two valuable conditions races last season, tenth of 30 over C&D in the Goffs Million and fifth in a Naas event; ended the season with a run at Group 3 level in the 1m1f Eyrefield Stakes at Leopardstown, appeared not to stay; first time in a maiden now; official rating of 95 may flatter him, but he could be good enough here; tongue-tie on.
runners.comment: First time in a maiden for this 95-rated colt, holds an obvious chance, tongue-tie on

runners.name: Loch Gamhna
runners.spotlight: Nondescript run at Naas on debut last September; promising third over C&D the following month on soft-to-heavy; winner Causeway franked the form at Naas on return, runner-up Iron Lily has won on at Dundalk.
runners.comment: Promising third over C&D last October, the winner and second have franked the form

runners.name: Mr Perth
runners.spotlight: Gelded son of Australia is the first foal out of a winning Zoffany mare; dam, sister to a French middle-distance winner, out of maiden half-sister to 1m2f Group 1 winner Zahrat Dubai.
runners.comment: Gelding by Australia, first foal out of a winning Zoffany mare, likely to need experience

runners.name: Zenford
runners.spotlight: Ran a bit green when third over this trip at Leopardstown on only start at two; has potential for improvement.
runners.comment: Ran a bit green when third at Leopardstown on only start at two, potential for improvement

runners.name: Spacebound
runners.spotlight: Put in a promotional debut effort in an older-horses maiden at Dundalk; form was boosted by a win for the third in a handicap; this is more demanding.
runners.comment: Good debut effort in an older-horses maiden at Dundalk, this is more demanding

runners.name: Lewis Carroll
runners.spotlight: Siyouni colt; first out of a Listed-placed 5f 2yo winner, half-sister to Racing Post Trophy and 2,000 Guineas winner Saxon Warrior, out of Moyglare Stud Stakes winner Maybe, family of Derby winner Dr Devious; stable outsider of three on debut over 6f here last June, noted staying on; likely to be a big player here if he has progressed satisfactorily.
runners.comment: Stable outsider of three over 6f here on only juvenile start, likely to be competitive now
runners.owner: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor

runners.name: Templenoe
runners.spotlight: From the family of Classic winner Tarascon; started slowly in a 7f maiden at Galway on only start at two; showed up quite well mid-race before fading; did not quite see out the 1m trip in testing conditions when sixth of 23 here at the season's opening fixture.
runners.comment: Fair sixth of 23 over 1m here at the season's opening fixture, could be in the mix now

runners.name: Hiltons Pass
runners.spotlight: 66-1 when finishing towards the back over 6f here on the opening day; unlikely to feature.
runners.comment: 66-1 when finishing towards the back over 6f here on the opening day, unlikely to feature

runners.name: Billy Jean
runners.spotlight: Beaten 15l when last of five at Dundalk on recent debut; stable's twice-raced Templenoe is more appealing; seems safe to rule out.
runners.comment: Beaten 15l when last of five at Dundalk on recent debut, seems safe to rule out

runners.name: Akissfromarose
runners.spotlight: Two AW runs; flopped when apparently well fancied on debut; in rear at 100-1 on second run.
runners.comment: Two AW runs, flopped when apparently well fancied on debut, in rear at 100-1 on second run

runners.name: Darwins Angel
runners.spotlight: Twice finished fourth at a modest level last year, no appeal in a maiden at this venue.
runners.comment: Twice finished fourth at a modest level last year, no appeal in a maiden at this venue

runners.name: Thisistheway
runners.spotlight: Useful hurdler who won the Red Mills Auction Final over 2m3f at last season's Punchestown festival; only previous Flat attempt was over 1m6f; runs over an inadequate trip here; worth a look with a view to her likely return to hurdling.
runners.comment: Useful hurdler, won at last year's Punchestown festival, runs over an inadequate trip here

runners.name: The Wee Man
runners.spotlight: Will need to qualify for a handicap on the evidence of outings at Galway last season and this venue 13 days ago.
runners.comment: Will need to qualify for a handicap on the evidence of his first two outings

races.name: Feel The Rush With 1xBet.ie Handicap

runners.name: Shoot To Kill
runners.spotlight: Veteran has won 12 of his 81 starts including five wins on turf; best form is on good ground, though he wasn't beaten that far over C&D on testing ground last October; has had a pipe opener at Dundalk and could outrun odds off 4lb lower.
runners.comment: Veteran, not beaten far over C&D in October, 4lb lower now, could outrun likely odds

runners.name: Earls
runners.spotlight: Veteran has won 11 races; sole success last year came off 76 at Roscommon (7.5f, soft) in September; 6lb higher now, was out of form when last seen here in November and usually needs a run or two to find top form.
runners.comment: Won off 6lb lower last Sept, out of form when last seen in Nov', may need this run

runners.name: Sir Jeremy
runners.spotlight: Sole win for Ado McGuinness came in a 7f Down Royal nursery in September 2024; ran well on soft ground at the Galway festival last August and 2lb lower now; off since September but worth a market check on first start for Noel Meade.
runners.comment: Ran well off 2lb higher on soft at Galway last August, new yard now, check market

runners.name: Electric Beauty
runners.spotlight: Was fit from three spins on the AW when gaining a third career win (and her first over this C&D) 13 days ago; hit with a 6lb rise for that but she'll relish conditions and should remain competitive.
runners.comment: Up 6lb for C&D win 3 days ago, will relish the ground, should remain competitive

runners.name: Perry Mason
runners.spotlight: C&D winner chased home Titanium in a premier handicap over C&D on his final run of 2025; was 1l behind that rival when ninth in the Irish Lincoln 13 days ago (2lb wrong); each-way shout with a repeat of that.
runners.comment: Behind Titanium the last twice, C&D winner has each-way claims on his latest effort

runners.name: Charming Whisper
runners.spotlight: Won five when trained in Britain by Philip McBride; all those wins came on ground with good in the description so conditions have to be a concern here (0-5 on soft); tongue tie goes on.
runners.comment: Five-time winner in Britain, 0-5 on soft ground, tongue tie goes on, needs track to dry

runners.name: Frank Pickle
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden has shown his best form on good ground over 1m4f; struggled on his sole start on testing ground and lacks a run; a bit to prove dropped back to 1m.
runners.comment: Maiden, best form over further on good ground, lacks a run, a bit to prove

runners.name: Space Warrior
runners.spotlight: Hurdles winner is 0-8 on the Flat and yet to trouble the judge; soundly beaten over timber at Cork last week and others hold more pressing claims.
runners.comment: 0-8 on the Flat, well beaten over hurdles last week, others make more appeal
runners.owner: Mrs Helen Mullins & M J Lomax

runners.name: Annies Angel
runners.spotlight: Won a Galway maiden on second start for Joseph O'Brien in October 2024 but no joy since; has had three runs over hurdles since joining Ado McGuinness so will at least be fit and new visor might provide a spark.
runners.comment: No win since Oct' 2024, at least fit from hurdling and new visor may provide a spark
runners.owner: Conor OConnor & Colum Currie & Mark Devenny

runners.name: Abbey Actress
runners.spotlight: Likes soft ground and won off 9lb lower on her final start for John O'Donoghue when last seen at Navan (1m2f, soft) last April; absence since a concern, as is this shorter trip; best watched on debut for new connections.
runners.comment: Off since Navan win last April, 9lb higher now, drop in trip and long absence are concerns

runners.name: Titanium
runners.spotlight: Six-time winner on turf (including a premier handicap over C&D off 8lb lower) ran a respectable race when sixth of 25 in the Irish Lincoln 13 days ago when 1lb wrong, beaten 8.5l; handles soft ground and these are calmer waters; in the mix.
runners.comment: C&D winner off 8lb lower, respectable run latest, this easier, ground fine, in the mix
runners.owner: D C Nolan & D P Kilmurray & Exors of the Late L G & K Clarke

runners.name: Inventress
runners.spotlight: Gowran maiden winner on her second start (1m, good) last May; soundly beaten in Gowran Classic on only start since (9.5f, good) last June; unraced on slower than good and likely best watched in first handicap unless the market speaks.
runners.comment: Gowran maiden winner on good, off since last June, soft a query, watch unless backed

runners.name: Pillar Of Hope
runners.spotlight: 5-21 on turf; bolted up off 70 at Leopardstown in October and narrowly denied here next time off 7lb higher (both 1m2f, heavy); 1m proved too sharp when last seen here in November and might need this after 146 days off.
runners.comment: 5-21 on turf, won easily off 13lb lower in October, might need this after 146 days off

runners.name: Spanish Temptress
runners.spotlight: 3-9 on turf, her last win coming over 7.5f at Roscommon in October; got hit with a 9lb rise for that and struggled behind Titanium over C&D off her revised mark when last seen in November; others appeal more.
runners.comment: 3-9 on turf, struggled off this mark over C&D when last seen in Nov', others preferred

races.name: Final Furlong Podcast At 1xBet.ie Maiden

runners.name: Shaihaan
runners.spotlight: Improved from his debut in a 7f course maiden dominated by Aidan O'Brien when chasing home the subsequent Saint-Cloud Group 1 winner Pierre Bonnard over 1m at Dundalk; found the competition too hot in the Group 2 Beresford Stakes; 102 official rating (or even the more conservative 94 RPR) more than enough to win if he handles the ground.
runners.comment: Second to a subsequent Group 1 winner at Dundalk, ran in a Group 2, can win a maiden

runners.name: George Stubbs
runners.spotlight: Ninth foal; colt by Camelot is half-brother to seven winners, all by Galileo, including Johannes Vermeer (Group 1 juvenile winner, Melbourne Cup second), Covent Garden (Irish Derby third) and Elizabeth Browning (Group 2); Ryan Moore prefers to King Of Sparta.
runners.comment: Half-brother by Camelot to seven winners by Galileo, Ryan Moore prefers to King Of Sparta
runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith

runners.name: Asakir
runners.spotlight: Third foal; half-brother to a Swiss middle-distance winner; dam 1m2f winner, from the family of Azamour; stable has three other runners with senior riders aboard.
runners.comment: Dam from the family of Azamour, stable has three other runners with senior riders aboard
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

runners.name: Latin America
runners.spotlight: Gleneagles colt cost 280,000gns as a yearling; third foal; half-brother to a winner in Sweden; dam 5f 2yo winner, out of French 7f 2yo Group 3 winner; one of four runners for the stable; may prove best of the bunch.
runners.comment: 280,000gns yearling, one of four runners for the stable; may prove best of the bunch

runners.name: Sir Trancealot
runners.spotlight: Eighth foal; homebred is a half-brother by Camelot to the Prix de Diane winner Channel and three other winners; dam 7f AW 2yo winner; one of a stable quartet; gelded.
runners.comment: Half-brother by Camelot to Prix de Diane winner Channel and three other winners, gelded

runners.name: Halon Bay
runners.spotlight: Third foal; half-brother to two winners; dam unraced half-sister to several winners; others have stronger pedigree claims.
runners.comment: Third foal, half-brother to two winners, dam unraced, others have stronger pedigree claims

runners.name: Bell Ex Too
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam placed, half-sister to winners Marvada (7f Group 3, dam of 1m2f Group 2 winner Terebellum) and Paint In Green (dam of Italian 1m2f Group 1 winner Sound Of Freedom); interesting pedigree, best watched on debut.
runners.comment: First foal out of a mare with interesting pedigree connections, best watched on debut.

runners.name: Leeward
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam unraced half-sister to winners Viadera (1m US Grade 1), Crossed Baton (1m2f Listed) and Sacred Bridge (6f 2yo Group 3.
runners.comment: Frankel colt whose dam is an unraced half-sister to 1m US Grade 1 winner Viadera

runners.name: Mighty Wave
runners.spotlight: 13th foal; closely related to winners Young Ireland (Group-placed 1m2f) and Feile Na Mban (1m4f), half-brother to winner Ceol Na Nog; dam 1m winner (89), half-sister to Irish Derby winner Soldier Of Fortune.
runners.comment: Dawn Approach gelding, dam a half-sister to Irish Derby winner Soldier Of Fortune

runners.name: King Of Sparta
runners.spotlight: Very smart pedigree; beaten 13l in sixth behind a stablemate on debut at Dundalk after running in snatches; handled heavy groud when second to a shorter-priced stablemate at Listowel; tongue-tie and cheekpieces on now; stable outsider again
runners.comment: Handed heavy ground when second to a shorter-priced stablemate at Listowel, second string
runners.owner: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor

races.name: Cavalor Equine Nutrition Handicap

runners.name: Green Icon
runners.spotlight: Won two for Owen Burrows and best run for this stable came over 1m here on good ground last August; out of form on AW last autumn, 33-1 when last of 12 over 6f here on reappearance.
runners.comment: Out of form on AW last autumn, 33-1 when last of 12 over 6f here on reappearance

runners.name: Kilgharrahs Love
runners.spotlight: Made the frame in two nurseries in 2024; Form deteriorated last year after a fair fourth in an early-season 7f handicap at Gowran; suited by heavy ground; 3lb out of the handicap.
runners.comment: Form deteriorated last year after a fair fourth in an early-season 7f handicap at Gowran

runners.name: Mickey The Steel
runners.spotlight: Useful sprinter in this grade; suited by testing conditions; worth a shot at 7f after two good runs at 6f in the last fortnight; third here with Merisi Diamond back in sixth; same place at Naas with Eichan San among those behind, not guaranteed to uphold that form.
runners.comment: Useful sprint handicapper at a modest level, worth a shot at 7f after two good runs at 6f

runners.name: Goldrush Kid
runners.spotlight: Eighth of 26 here 13 days ago when bidding to repeat last year's win in a 1m contest; may do better with that run behind him.
runners.comment: Eighth of 26 here 13 days ago when bidding to repeat last year's win, may do better

runners.name: Zipster
runners.spotlight: Won at Killarney in July over 1m on yielding-to-soft; also ran well to take third over the same trip at Ayr; stable had a handicap winner at Naas last week.
runners.comment: Probably best over a bit further than this, stable had a handicap winner at Naas last week

runners.name: Eichan San
runners.spotlight: Ran well in three turf maidens last year; too keen over 1m on final outing but still managed a respectable fourth; form of that race boosted by wins for the placed horses, Naples and Tina's Charm, and fifth-placed Daler; found 6f inadequate at Naas last Sunday, can make an impact over this extra furlong and may turn the tables on third-placed Mickey The Steel.
runners.comment: Found 6f inadequate at Naas last Sunday, can make an impact over this extra furlong

runners.name: Secret Magician
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner, including twice over 6f here early in his career; uneven form last year; in rear over 6f here on return.
runners.comment: Twice a course winner over 6f, uneven form last year, in rear over 6f here on return

runners.name: Rampage
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner, including twice on turf early in his career; won at Dundalk during a busy 2025 campaign; made a satisfactory return here on the opening day; has a bit to find with Lough Leane.
runners.comment: Made a satisfactory return here on the opening day, has a bit to find with Lough Leane
runners.owner: Mrs P McCourt/Whytes Stamullen Syndicate/S Sweeney

runners.name: Lough Leane
runners.spotlight: Six-time AW winner for David Simcock; first two runs for this stable last summer were respectable; disappointed later in the campaign; can be rated a leading contender based on his excellent second of 26 at this venue on reappearance; was in front at this distance
runners.comment: Was in front at this distance when second of 26 over 1m here on return, strong chance

runners.name: Not Simple
runners.spotlight: Won a 7f soft-ground handicap at Cork last September; failed to sustain that progress in three subsequent outings; still 9lb above her winning mark
runners.comment: Won a 7f soft-ground handicap at Cork last autumn, failed to match that form subsequently

runners.name: Genuine Jim
runners.spotlight: Both wins on soft, including over C&D; should be better for his run here on the opening day; appears well held by Lough Leane, but should not be ruled out with Nicola Burns taking the mount in preference to Deuteronomy who gave her three wins last season
runners.comment: Has plenty to find with Lough Lane on running 13 days ago but should not be ruled out

runners.name: Giselles Defence
runners.spotlight: Won four on turf for Ian Williams at 1m2f on varying ground; has failed to beat a single rival in three Dundalk starts for this stable.
runners.comment: Four wins in Britain, has failed to beat a single rival in three AW starts for this stable

runners.name: Mogwli
runners.spotlight: Eight-time winner, including twice at this track over 6f early in his career; won twice last year but prone to inconsistency; mid-field over 1m here on seasonal debut
runners.comment: Won twice last year but prone to inconsistency, mid-field over 1m here on seasonal debut

runners.name: Royal Impact
runners.spotlight: Ordinary form in two runs over further on turf; has improved since switching to AW at Dundalk; winner over this trip last month, form has been boosted by third-placed Ohailbhic; good chance if he proves as effective on this surface.
runners.comment: Won over this trip at Dundalk last month, form has been boosted by third-placed Ohailbhic

runners.name: Ferrari Desert
runners.spotlight: Dundalk winner over this trip; 0-8 on turf; off for ten months before an AW run in January; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: 7f AW winner, 0-8 on turf. off for ten months before AW run in January, hard to fancy

runners.name: God Knows
runners.spotlight: Runs well when fresh, second at this venue on her first start of the 2024 season and won a claimer on seasonal debut last year; worth considering on the form of her final two starts of the campaign.
runners.comment: Runs well when fresh, worth considering on the form of her final two starts in 2025

runners.name: Merisi Diamond
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner whose best form has been on soft and heavy at around 7f; has run respectably in recent outings here and at Naas; needs to find extra.
runners.comment: Likes it soft, has run respectably in recent outings here and at Naas, needs to find extra
runners.owner: David Brennan & J Tuite & Ms Joanna Morgan & Jeremy Greene

runners.name: Deuteronomy
runners.spotlight: In the form of his life in June last year, when notching three consecutive wins (1m/1m1f) following the addition of a visor/blinkers; a top rider booked here, but the trainer's daughter Nicola now rides Genuine Jim, who had a recent run.
runners.comment: Completed a hat-trick at 1m1/1m1f last June for Nicola Burns who rides Genuine Jim instead

runners.name: Hell Left Loose
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner on turf at 6f-1m, including on heavy ground; has also won at Laytown and at Dundalk; lost form on AW after 1m Dundalk win last November; showed a return to form when sixth of 23 over 1m here on return to turf action.
runners.comment: Showed a return to form when sixth of 23 over 1m here 13 days ago

runners.name: Merepark
runners.spotlight: Huge odds and no impact in three maidens at 1m-1m2f; hard to make a case on handicap/seasonal debut.
runners.comment: Huge odds and no impact in three maidens at 1m-1m2f; hard to make a case on handicap debut

runners.name: Red Charlie
runners.spotlight: Ran five times for Emmet Mullins in 2024; attracted the attention of the stewards on handicap debut at Fairyhouse; several possible excuses were advanced (hampered at start, hit a ridge two furlongs out, lost both hind shoes); not a bad run at Dundalk on final start; missed all of last year; could be interesting on stable debut off a 7lb lower mark.
runners.comment: Missed all of last year;, could be interesting on stable debut off a reduced mark

runners.name: Anightaway
runners.spotlight: Weak form in turf maidens; no better on AW on handicap debut or over hurdles last time
runners.comment: Weak form in turf maidens, no better on AW on handicap debut or over hurdles last time

runners.name: Zabriskie Point
runners.spotlight: Has plunged in the ratings from marks in the 90s in Britain two seasons ago to a current 65; no encouragement from winter AW form; pulled up here on the opening day and never in the hunt; up against it.
runners.comment: Has plunged in the ratings from marks in the 90s in Britain two seasons ago to current 64
runners.owner: B White & M W White & Ms H McGuinness & D Sheridan

races.name: Asahi Super Dry Neville Sellwood Stakes (Group 2) (Turf)

runners.name: Caviar Heights
runners.spotlight: First-up (27-weeks). Has performed well previously first-up without winning but this isn't easy. First time around Rosehill. Untested in wet.

runners.name: Wootton Verni
runners.spotlight: Failed to run on in the small field here over this trip. Has plenty going for him here.
runners.owner: C J Waller Go Bloodstock Australia Et Al

runners.name: Barnavara
runners.spotlight: Resuming (24-weeks). Ex Mrs John Harrington. Trained here now. Recorded a breakthrough Group One win last campaign. Stakes winning International runner.

runners.name: Bois DArgent
runners.spotlight: Close placing in the Parramatta Cup at Rosehill. There to threaten just couldn't quite finish it off at Randwick in second run from spell. Form sound at this course and journey. Ready to peak third-up.
runners.owner: Ontrack Bois DArgent Ontrack Bois DArgent No 2 Et Al

runners.name: Les Vampires
runners.spotlight: Very tame performance in rain affected going at Canberra second-up. Rough place prospect.
runners.owner: T Gorgovski Est S A Hardie Et Al

runners.name: Portland
runners.spotlight: Only better for two Aust runs. Battled home here second-up. Fitter for those two runs and ready to show best. Sound claims.
runners.owner: McS Racing Snap & Run Racing Et Al

runners.name: Star Of India
runners.spotlight: Finished out the back at Randwick at this distance. Unplaced in wet. Can fill the minors here.
runners.owner: B K Racing & Breeding Pty Ltd B Vassallo & Symphony Racing

runners.name: Our Anchorage
runners.spotlight: First-up (24-weeks). Two quiet trials for return and proven here. Ticks the track and distance win box. Can lead or take a sit.
runners.owner: Darby Racing G Aldridge Et Al

races.name: Vinery Stud Stakes (Group 1) (Fillies) (Turf)

runners.name: Salty Pearl
runners.spotlight: Made easy work of VOBIS Platinum Guineas win at Caulfield at $2.05. Should appreciate stepping up in distance. Can repeat dose.
runners.owner: Starblue Racing TLO Malice CMR LOF Et Al

runners.name: Panova
runners.spotlight: Ran home respectably into a non-threatening 5th this track second-up. Should appreciate stepping up in distance. Usually gets back. Rates highly.

runners.name: Ohope Wins
runners.spotlight: Now 5 weeks between runs. Ex L O'Sullivan & A Scott. Trained here now. NZer having first run across the ditch. Since trialled at Randwick 16 Mar.

runners.name: Belle Cheval
runners.spotlight: Ex Mark Walker & Sam Bergerson in New Zealand. NZer having first run across the ditch. First real test of stamina here. Betting may provide best clue.
runners.owner: D L Archer S C Archer Et Al

runners.name: After Summer
runners.spotlight: Very tame performance at Flemington at first run back. Will be fitter second-up. Minor money hope at best.
runners.owner: Sutton Racing Pty Ltd S Chappell Et Al

runners.name: Long Legs
runners.spotlight: Gallant as anything on a soft deck at Kembla Grange second-up. Won only other time at Rosehill. Handles wet ground. Drawn to get an economic trail.
runners.owner: B K Racing & Breeding Pty Ltd & B J Vassallo

runners.name: Aisle Two
runners.spotlight: Rosehill winner two back. Brave effort in busy finish on a soft deck at Kembla Grange. Winner at only other start at Rosehill. Each way prospect.
runners.owner: D A Dollimore & Ms R M Bennett

runners.name: Celestial Award
runners.spotlight: Just got there in a gripping finish at at Geelong at her second race start. Stamina on trial here. Tackling stronger line-up.

runners.name: Stand My Ground
runners.spotlight: Delivered a steely performance to score Super Mdn Plate at Newcastle at her second race start. First real test of stamina here. Can make her presence felt.
runners.owner: Dr U Crosson Mrs S Crosson Et Al

races.name: Schweppes Emancipation Stakes (Group 2) (Turf)

runners.name: Idle Flyer
runners.spotlight: Handy and boxed on solidly at Randwick at resumption. Since trialled at Warwick Farm 20 Mar. Has won multiple times in wet. Fitter, proven second-up.
runners.owner: A B Tall Mrs S J Tall Et Al

runners.name: Snow In May
runners.spotlight: Battled home at Randwick first up. Best results on firm going. Fitter second-up. Minor money hope at best.
runners.owner: R A Pegum Werrett Bloodstock Pty Ltd Et Al

runners.name: Churchills Choice
runners.spotlight: Kept coming and just fell short at Newcastle at first run back. Handles wet ground. Will be fitter second-up. One for exotics.

runners.name: Arctic Glamour
runners.spotlight: Trailed pace and ran on gamely this venue over this trip. Stats are good this course. This looks very winnable.
runners.owner: M H Wood Mrs M E Wood Et Al

runners.name: Dark Glitter
runners.spotlight: 4 weeks since last run. Long time since last saluted. Got back, never a serious danger but not disgraced at Randwick. Awkwardly drawn.
runners.owner: M F Van Gestel Mrs L M Van Gestel et al

runners.name: Shohisha
runners.spotlight: Ran home respectably into a non-threatening 7th this track second-up. Handles the sting out of the ground. Good draw. Can fill the minors here.
runners.owner: W N Boyde Ms G M Chapo & Dodgy Investments Pty Limited

runners.name: Firestorm
runners.spotlight: Never a threat after showing no early speed from the barriers here second-up. Home track advantage but has a tricky draw and rising in weight. Minor money hope at best.

runners.name: Tuileries
runners.spotlight: Gave solid kick from handy position to triumph at Newcastle second-up. More suitable draw. Can repeat dose.
runners.owner: Snowden Racing Pty Ltd Pinecliff Racing Et Al

runners.name: Perfumist
runners.spotlight: Prominent to 400m but threw the towel in quickly this venue first up. Fitter but second-up record isn't flash. Adverse barrier tests. Looking elsewhere.
runners.owner: OTI Racing S Cheetham Et Al

runners.name: Modella
runners.spotlight: Gave them a start but picked up strongly to win quite impressively in 3yo+ (Bm88) this track in second run from spell. Can go back to back.
runners.owner: Mystery Downs Mrs C M Cook Et Al

runners.name: Verona Rose
runners.spotlight: Won Guy Walter Stakes Randwick 28 Feb. Quietly handled in back half, shaken up and finished full of running this track at this distance. Worth a look.
runners.owner: Remy Racing Mrs K Colquhoun Et Al

runners.name: Blindedbythelight
runners.spotlight: Resuming (19-weeks). In the minors at Bendigo first-up last campaign. Testing race to come back in.

runners.name: Sunset Park
runners.spotlight: First-up (20-weeks). Has performed well previously first-up without winning although hasn't shown much in recent trials but this isn't easy. Keener on others.
runners.owner: Cross Park P B James Et Al

races.name: Kia Tancred Stakes (Group 1) (Turf)

runners.name: Dubai Honour
runners.spotlight: Resuming (14-weeks). Elite performer. Has a good first up record and proven here. Ticks the track and distance win box.
runners.owner: Exors of the late Mohamed Obaida

runners.name: Aeliana
runners.spotlight: On a quick back-up. Close placing in the Verry Elleegant Stakes at Randwick. Recorded a hard fought win on a soft deck here under heavy support. Should appreciate stepping up in distance.
runners.owner: Star Thoroughbreds Pty Ltd Boeara Holdings Et Al

runners.name: Vauban
runners.spotlight: Multiple G1 winner. Prominent and responded strongly here at resumption. Will be fitter second-up. Can add to tally.
runners.owner: Australian Bloodstock AB Vauban Et Al

runners.name: Piggyback
runners.spotlight: Ran on late from the back at Randwick. One for multiples.
runners.owner: Dynamic Syndications Racing S D Murray Et Al

runners.name: Zarir
runners.spotlight: Tame to line in small field this venue in second run from spell. Long out of form and hard to recommend.

runners.name: Valiant King
runners.spotlight: Never looked a serious threat in the small field this venue. Looks tested.
runners.owner: A Kheir Qatar Bloodstock Ltd Et Al

runners.name: River Of Stars
runners.spotlight: Last win was 23 Jun 2024. Failed to run on in the small field this venue at second run back. Stiff task.

runners.name: Soul Of Spain
runners.spotlight: Ran home into the minors Sky High Stakes here. Untried in the wet. Could get into the placings.
runners.owner: A J A Bosma N J C Smith Et Al

races.name: TAB Australian Cup (Group 1) (Turf)

runners.name: Tom Kitten
runners.spotlight: High class galloper. Terrific winning the All-Star Mile over 1600m this venue at $3.90. Should appreciate stepping up in distance. Form good enough for this.

runners.name: Light Infantry Man
runners.spotlight: Got a stitch late after leading in the small field at Caulfield. Very fit and could give some cheek at odds.
runners.owner: Halo Racing Services Pty Ltd A Kheir Et Al

runners.name: Pride Of Jenni
runners.spotlight: Multiple G1 winner. Prominent and showed plenty of ticker to just go under in thrilling finish in the All-Star Mile this track at first run back. Fitter, proven second-up. On pacer.
runners.owner: A Ottobre Mrs L Ottobre & M Ottobre

runners.name: Apulia
runners.spotlight: Ran on soundly into second in the small field at Caulfield. Can play a role.

runners.name: Birdman
runners.spotlight: Lining up for three wins on the trot. Made short work of the small field in the Peter Young Stakes at Caulfield. Should appreciate stepping up in distance. This looks very winnable.
runners.owner: A Kheir N Greenhalgh Et Al

runners.name: Leica Lucy
runners.spotlight: Not disgraced here second-up. Ready to peak third-up. Sound claims.
runners.owner: A Kheir Mrs H J Crofskey Et Al

runners.name: Basilinna
runners.spotlight: Drifted back and never a threatening factor at Caulfield at first run off a long break. Hard to enthuse.
runners.owner: Sparta Racing Syndicate G Devonport Et Al

runners.name: Sabaj
runners.spotlight: Close placing in the The Elms Hcp at Flemington. Gave good account this track. Stamina on trial here. Can make his presence felt.
runners.owner: S Scaffidi-Muta Mrs A Rossi Et Al

runners.name: Damask Rose
runners.spotlight: On a quick back-up. Never better than midfield in the Sunline Stakes at Caulfield. First real test of stamina here. Others with stronger claims.
runners.owner: Te Akau Coming Up Roses Racing Partnership 1 & 2

runners.name: Philia
runners.spotlight: On a quick back-up. Handily placed and worked home soundly at Caulfield. Will have to improve to make an impact.

runners.name: Mark Twain
runners.spotlight: Given time to freshen up after 1st-up run. Sent back and finished with a full head of steam in Open company this track at first run for new trainer. Looking elsewhere.
runners.owner: OTI Racing J P Spence Et Al

runners.name: Just Fine
runners.spotlight: Sent forward, whacked away for the minors Sky High Stakes at Rosehill. This looks stronger.
runners.owner: MyRacehorse No 1 Australian Chinese J C Racing 7 Et Al

races.name: Arkansas Derby (Grade 1) (Dirt)

runners.name: Blackout Time
runners.spotlight: Ellis Park maiden winner last August; second in Grade 1 Futurity at Keeneland (clearly best form) but only fourth to Class President in Grade 2 Rebel here last time, tiring and beaten 8l; may have needed that latest run after five months away and could go well, if all remains well.
runners.comment: Grade 1 second; only fourth in Grade 2 here five months later but may have needed that run
runners.owner: BR Thoroughbreds Inc Lance Gasaway & Magdalena Racing

runners.name: Litmus Test
runners.spotlight: Landed the odds in 8.5f Grade 2 Los Alamitos Futurity last December, stalking pace until taking over in straight to win comfortably by just over 1l; again favourite, beaten 5l by Class President and Silent Tactic when third in Grade 2 Rebel here, setting contested pace until straight; smart.
runners.comment: Grade 2 winner last December, faded in Grade 2 Rebel here, pressured on lead; smart type
runners.owner: SF Racing LLC Starlight Racing Et Al

runners.name: Napoleon Solo
runners.spotlight: Impressive 6l winner of 1m Grade 1 Champagne at Aqueduct in October on second start, setting strong pace and keeping on very well; lost unbeaten record in stacked renewal of Grade 2 Fountain of Youth at Gulfstream, unable to dominate and only fifth, beaten about 12l; can bounce back to make bold bid from front.
runners.comment: 6l winner of Grade 1 last autumn; unable to lead again last time; can bounce back

runners.name: Redland Rebels
runners.spotlight: Won maiden at Gulfstream Park last August; in frame both starts since, latterly 3l fourth to The Puma in Grade 3 Tampa Bay Derby early this month; useful form but needs to improve a good deal.
runners.comment: Useful form in frame last two starts, latterly 3l fourth in Grade 3 at Tampa Bay; outsider

runners.name: Silent Tactic
runners.spotlight: 12-1, improved to win Grade 3 Southwest Stakes by just over 3l from Soldier N Diplomat, confidently ridden by Cristian Torres and powering home; forced wide and arguably unlucky when beaten a nose by Class President in Grade 2 Rebel Stakes, again 8.5f here; capable of even better and must go well.
runners.comment: Impressive in Grade 3 Southwest then unlucky when touched off in Grade 2 Rebel; big player

runners.name: Taptastic
runners.spotlight: Well-bred colt (by Tapit out of Curlin mare) who was not missed by the market when odds-on for 1m maiden here (muddy) on debut; value for a lot more than the 1.5l winning margin, having to wait for a run then surging home when in clear; interesting that top trainer rolls the big dice here.
runners.comment: Odds-on for debut, won by 1.5l but had lots in hand after trouble in running; promising
runners.owner: Winchell Thoroughbreds LLC & Stonestreet Stables LLC

runners.name: Bricklin
runners.spotlight: 7-1 when impressive winner of allowance race here in January, emerging from a duel and keeping on in great style to string out the field; well held when about 5l third to Pavlovian in Listed race at Sunland Park but that form is working out well; needs to find a lot more but a tough sort.
runners.comment: Allowance winner here but well held in third in Sunland Park Listed; tough but needs more

runners.name: Exosome
runners.spotlight: Fairly useful form, as when winning allowance race here last time by neck, always close up; needs huge improvement.
runners.comment: Takes huge step up after his narrow win in an allowance race here last time; outsider

runners.name: Renegade
runners.spotlight: Into Mischief colt; second to classy Paladin in 1m1f Grade 2 Remsen at Aqueduct (raced wide); favourite, broke maiden in Listed race at Tampa Bay in February, picking up in fine style from well off pace and drawing nearly 4l clear; relished fast pace there and is thriving; surely a smart horse in the making.
runners.comment: Grade 2 second to Paladin at Aqueduct, then won Listed at Tampa readily by 4l; big chance
runners.owner: Robert Low Lawana L Low & Repole Stable

races.name: Curlin Florida Derby Presented By Hill 'N' Dale Farms At Xalapa (Grade 1) (Dirt)

runners.name: The Puma
runners.spotlight: Progressive colt who gained first win on third start in 8.5f Grade 3 Tampa Bay Derby, last early then making relentless progress to better Further Ado by just under 1l in stirring effort; would need another overly strong pace early to trouble best of these but open to improvement and cannot be written off.
runners.comment: Came from last to win Grade 3 Tampa Bay Derby by under 1l; needs fast pace to make frame
runners.owner: OGMA Investments LLC JR Ranch & High Step Racing LLC

runners.name: Commandment
runners.spotlight: Progressing well and has won here on last three starts, notably hard-fought success in Grade 2 Fountain of Youth last time, pressed hard by Chief Wallabee but prevailing by a neck in fast time (edged right); resolute rather than brilliant but type to improve again; strong claims.
runners.comment: Neck winner of Grade 2 Fountain of Youth from Chief Wallabee; progressive, strong claims

runners.name: Chief Wallabee
runners.spotlight: Second favourite for Grade 2 Fountain of Youth after impressive maiden win (where beat The Puma), also at Gulfstream; ran a highly promising race to press Commandment all the way, losing by just a neck, and improvement gives him a big shot to reverse the form; strong contender.
runners.comment: Neck second to Commandment in Grade 2 Fountain of Youth on second start; top prospect

runners.name: Nearly
runners.spotlight: Won maiden and allowance here before impressive in 8.5f Grade 3 Holy Bull Stakes, in which he tracked fast pace while running wide, attacked in straight and drew nearly 6l clear in manner of a high-class prospect, though closing fraction was slow; looks sure to improve again but slight doubt at 1m1f.
runners.comment: Won maiden, allowance and Grade 3 Holy Bull, all here and impressive; first go at 1m1f

runners.name: Albus
runners.spotlight: Landed the odds in maiden at Tampa Bay on third start, tracking leader and going clear in straight with field well strung out to win by nearly 7l with the third over 15l away; that was a useful effort but he will need an awful lot more to compete here.
runners.comment: Useful 7l winner of Tampa Bay maiden with rest strung out; sure to improve but stiff task

runners.name: Timeless Victory
runners.spotlight: Fairly useful form to win here in a maiden and two allowances; won by 6l on latest start after tracking pace and powering home; looks a strong stayer and has profile of an underrated prospect, yet to be tested in deep waters; needs to find plenty.
runners.comment: Won three times here in lesser grade, by 6l latest; fairly useful form but could improve
runners.owner: Leon Ellman Glassman Racing LLC & Laurie Plesa

runners.name: Gregarious
runners.spotlight: 18-1 when beaten nearly 6l on debut in maiden here; it would be a massive shock if he won this.
runners.comment: Second in maiden here on debut but needs to pull off a massive shock at this level

runners.name: Waynes Law
runners.spotlight: Won maiden and black type event here last autumn; near 50-1 when chasing home impressive 4l winner Renegade in Listed Sam Davis Stakes at Tampa last time, left behind final furlong (The Puma third); useful but does not appear of this quality yet.
runners.comment: Placed all five starts, winning two here; 4l second in Listed at Tampa; useful but exposed
